#### Learning 30 ‚Äî second PLANNING deliverable; the PARENT PLAN was stale; inventory test helpers by CALL FORM (S30, Phase 8 sub-plan / issue \#39)

Planned Phase 8 of the conversion (enable the shinytest2 E2E harness) ‚Üí
`docs/planning/phase8-e2e-harness-subplan.md`. Planning/architecture
session ‚Äî TDD code-phases INAPPLICABLE (Learning \#21a); the plan is the
deliverable, no code written (FM \#18/#19). **(a)** A planning session‚Äôs
\[verify-first\] target is the PARENT PLAN‚Äôs own inventory, not just an
external audit (extends \#21b ‚Äúthe audit was STALE‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúthe parent plan
was stale‚Äù). The conversion plan ¬ß9 Phase 8 (S21) claimed ‚Äú**3** missing
helpers / **one** session‚Äù; a firsthand call-site census found **6
undefined helpers + 1 undefined constant `E2E_TIMEOUT`** and a
**4-session mini-campaign**. Re-derive the symbol inventory by grep ‚Äî
never trust a prior plan‚Äôs count. **(b)** For a TEST-HELPER inventory,
key on the CALL FORM (arity + named args), NOT just symbol presence
(\[idiom-inventory\] applied to helpers): `navigate_to_tab` has **109
three-arg vs 27 two-arg** calls ‚Üí MUST be
`navigate_to_tab(app, label, fallback=NULL)` (a 2-arg signature errors
‚Äúunused argument‚Äù on 109 sites); `create_app_driver` gets
`height`/`width` at 2 sites ‚Üí MUST forward `...`; `E2E_TIMEOUT` at
error-states.R:232 is TOP-LEVEL (hard error) while
L46/boundary-conditions.R:44 are `tryCatch`-swallowed. A bare
symbol-existence grep (the parent‚Äôs method) misses all three. **(c)**
HIDDEN-DOM: a `navbarPage` renders ALL tabs‚Äô static UI into the DOM at
boot (verified `as.character(appUI())` = 85,106 chars containing every
tab‚Äôs keywords), so the suite‚Äôs dominant `grepl(kw, "body")` pattern
PASSES TRIVIALLY on boot ‚Üí ‚Äúharness runs green‚Äù ‚âÝ ‚Äúvalidates behavior.‚Äù
The planning fork is therefore TWO deliverables: **make-executable**
(helpers + CI; issue \#39) vs **make-validate** (rewrite ~41
`expect_true(TRUE)` tautologies + a wrong-tab-nav bug:
summary-statistics-module navigates to ‚ÄúGenetic Value Analysis‚Äù in 7/8
tests yet passes). Surfaced both to the owner (\[author-decision\]);
owner scoped \#39 to executable-only, filed validate (‚Äú8e‚Äù) as a
separate issue. Do NOT over-sell ‚Äúgreen.‚Äù **(d)** A ‚Äúone session‚Äù parent
phase can be a multi-session MINI-CAMPAIGN once inventoried ‚Äî the
planning output is the realistic risk-ordered session map (8a helpers
browser-free RED‚ÜíGREEN / 8b boot-smoke + CI üêâ first browser run / 8c 15
shallow files / 8d 5 interaction+menu files ‚Üí close \#39), with all 159
tests assigned. DEFER inert work: the `input`‚Üí`dataInput` namespace
mismatch is real but the polling helpers are NEVER CALLED (verified) ‚Üí
8e, not 8a; fixing it early is churn with no observable effect
(module-side signaling already uses `session$ns`, so the live app is
correct). **(e)** \[completeness-workflow\] for a plan (discovery
fan-out ‚Üí adversarial completeness-critic): the census of all 23 files ‚Üí
a critic that returned **16 findings (4 HIGH the single-pass synthesis
dropped** ‚Äî the 3 extra helpers, the constant, the arity, the
`...`-forwarding) ‚Üí re-verify EACH firsthand (greps/`sed`) ‚Äî the critic
CONFIRMS, is not trusted (#21c). \[right-sized-orchestration\]: one
read-only workflow (7 agents), no third pass. **Reflexes:**
\[verify-first\]\[completeness-workflow\]\[author-decision\]\[idiom-inventory\]\[right-sized-orchestration\].
**Apply:** for a sub-plan of a parent-plan phase ‚Äî re-derive the
inventory firsthand by grep (the parent plan‚Äôs symbol count is a
\[verify-first\] target, often stale); inventory test helpers by CALL
FORM (arity / named args), not just name; if the suite is a `navbarPage`
E2E, test the hidden-DOM hypothesis and split make-executable vs
make-validate as separate owner-scoped deliverables; size the phase
honestly as a mini-campaign; run discovery ‚Üí completeness-critic ‚Üí
re-verify-firsthand.

#### Learning 31 ‚Äî first sub-phase EXECUTION; the approved plan‚Äôs ¬ß4 pseudo-code was a verify-first target (duplicate-arg splice); browser-free TDD via fake AppDriver stubs (S31, Phase 8a / issue \#39)

Implemented Phase 8a of the conversion: define the 6 shinytest2 E2E
driver helpers + `E2E_TIMEOUT` in `tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R`
(browser-free RED‚ÜíGREEN, strict TDD RESUMED after the two planning
sessions \#21/#30). **(a)** An APPROVED plan‚Äôs ¬ß4 helper pseudo-code is
itself a \[verify-first\] target ‚Äî implementing it VERBATIM ships a
latent bug (extends Learning \#30‚Äôs ‚Äúthe parent plan‚Äôs count is stale‚Äù
from PLANNING to EXECUTION). The ¬ß4
`create_app_driver(app_dir, name, ...)` body hardcodes
`height=800, width=1200` then splices `...`; the 2
`boundary-conditions.R` sites that pass `height=`/`width=` as NAMED args
make `AppDriver$new(... height=800 ... height=600 ...)` ‚Üí R error
*‚Äúformal argument ‚Äòheight‚Äô matched by multiple actual arguments‚Äù*
(verified by introspection:
`formals(shinytest2::AppDriver$public_methods$initialize)` has EXPLICIT
`height`/`width` ‚Äî NOT absorbed by AppDriver‚Äôs own `...`). Fix = expose
them as named formals `(app_dir, name, height=800, width=1200, ...)` so
a caller‚Äôs named arg binds there; `...` still forwards other args
(e.g.¬Ý`seed`). Surfaced the one-line deviation-from-¬ß4 in the
PRE-RED‚ÜíRED gate (\[author-decision\]); it FULFILLS the plan‚Äôs stated
intent (‚Äúheight/width override the defaults‚Äù), not deviates from it.
**(b)** Audit a fix-all/wire-up helper‚Äôs signature by CALL FORM (arity +
named-vs-positional) BEFORE baking it in (\[idiom-inventory\] applied to
EXECUTION, the \#30 lesson): firsthand greps re-derived every call form
‚Äî `create_app_driver` 144 two-arg + 2 named-height/width;
`navigate_to_tab` 109 three-arg (POSITIONAL `fallback`) + 28 two-arg (no
named 3rd arg, no 4-arg); `get_html_safe` 153√ó `(app,"body")`;
`click_element_safe`/`navigate_to_menu_item`/`get_values_safe` 5/4/3
sites; return-value consumption (`success <-` √ó129 for navigate_to_tab ‚Üí
must return logical; `body <- ...; grepl(kw, body)` ‚Üí must return ‚Äú‚Äù not
error). **(c)** \[right-sized-orchestration\] HONESTY about a workflow
failure: launched a read-only 7-agent call-site-audit workflow to
confirm the signatures; it FAILED on a framework hiccup (one subagent
never emitted StructuredOutput after 2 nudges ‚Üí the bare `agent()`
critic call threw, failing the run). Rather than re-run, did the
call-form audit firsthand by grep (deterministic, grep-settled) ‚Äî and it
CAUGHT the (a) duplicate-arg bug. When a verification workflow dies on
framework mechanics and the underlying check is deterministic grep, do
it by hand ‚Äî don‚Äôt fight the framework; the workflow‚Äôs INTENT (catch
signature bugs) was met by the manual fallback. **(d)** BROWSER-FREE TDD
of browser helpers via FAKE APPDRIVER STUBS: a plain
[`list()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/list.html) of functions stands in for
the R6 AppDriver (`app$method(args)` resolves `$` on a list and calls
the element). Three stubs discriminate the contracts: *throwing* (every
method [`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)s ‚Üí proves the
`*_safe` helpers return
`""`/[`list()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/list.html)/`FALSE` and
`navigate_to_tab`‚ÜíFALSE), *recording-ok* (`set_inputs` stores into an
env ‚Üí `get_value` reflects it ‚Üí `navigate_to_tab` read-back TRUE),
*silent-no-op* (`get_value` never reflects the set tab ‚Üí
`navigate_to_tab` FALSE ‚Äî proves the read-back DISCRIMINATES a silent
failed navigation, sub-plan finding 15; \[discriminating-RED\]).
`create_app_driver`‚Äôs body inherently needs Chrome ‚Üí covered by
existence+formals only in 8a (real construction is 8b); stated as a
browser-deferred limit, NOT FM \#24 (the helper is test infra, not app
runtime). **(e)** Phase 3E (runtime APP smoke) is N/A here ‚Äî 8a changes
NO running-app behavior; the helpers live only in the test tree
(`helper-shinytest2.R`, auto-sourced by testthat), so the test SUITE is
the runtime + the verification (distinct from every prior modular slice
S22‚ÄìS29, which changed appServer/app and needed a
[`runModularApp()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runModularApp.md)
smoke). `tests/` is `.lintr`-excluded (`.lintr:35` `"tests"`) ‚Üí
lint-exempt (direct lint = ‚ÄúNo lints found‚Äù); `document()` zero
`man/`+`NAMESPACE` delta (only `tests/` touched, no `R/` roxygen);
test-infra only ‚Üí CHANGELOG only, no NEWS. The new file
`test_helper_shinytest2.R` (underscore ‚Üí default suite, browser-free,
always-runs like `test_create_test_app.R`) added 14 `test_that` / 32
assertions; full non-e2e suite 0 failed/0 error, 2154 passed (+32).
**Reflexes:**
\[verify-first\]\[idiom-inventory\]\[discriminating-RED\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[regression-read\]\[refactor-only\]\[document-zero-delta\]\[lint-net-zero\]\[author-decision\].
**Apply:** for a sub-phase EXECUTION of an approved plan ‚Äî treat the
plan‚Äôs helper pseudo-code as a \[verify-first\] target (re-audit every
call form by arity/named-vs-positional before baking the signature; a
verbatim splice of defaults + `...` duplicate-crashes on named
overrides); if a confirmation workflow dies on framework mechanics and
the check is deterministic grep, finish it by hand; TDD browser helpers
browser-free via fake-AppDriver
[`list()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/list.html) stubs (throwing / recording
/ silent-no-op) to discriminate error + success + read-back contracts;
Phase 3E is N/A when the deliverable lives only in the test tree (say
so) ‚Äî the suite is the runtime.

#### Learning 32 ‚Äî first CONFIG-ONLY sub-phase (CI infra, no RED‚ÜíGREEN); the silent-skip CI blocker the adversarial review caught (S32, Phase 8b / issue \#39)

Implemented Phase 8b of the conversion: the first-ever real browser run
of the modular app (3 boot-smoke files green opt-in) + the CI rewire of
`.github/workflows/shinytest2.yaml`. **(a)** An ‚Äúimplementation‚Äù
sub-phase can be **CONFIG-ONLY** ‚Äî no new R unit, no RED‚ÜíGREEN (extends
\#21a/#18c from PLANNING/REFACTOR to CI-INFRA). The 3 boot-smoke files
are pre-existing and use `create_test_app()`+`AppDriver$new` DIRECTLY /
`testServer` (no Phase-8a derived helpers), and sub-plan ¬ß11 says the
E2E files are *run/triaged, not rewritten* ‚Üí the **verify-first browser
SPIKE is the runtime verification** and the only deliverable change is
the CI YAML. Declare TDD code-phases INAPPLICABLE (config-only) and gate
the CLASSIFICATION via `AskUserQuestion` with the spike evidence in hand
‚Äî do NOT manufacture a synthetic RED. The spike also settled the ¬ß8.1
nav spike: `app$click('a[data-value="Input"]')` clicks the live bslib
navbar (no self-skip ‚Äî `app-navigation: ...` 3 dots, no `S`). **(b)**
The CI-authoring \[regression-read\] footgun (\[ci-suite-parity\],
discovered here): I set `NPRC_RUN_E2E` in the CI env but **DROPPED
`NOT_CRAN`** ‚Äî on the non-interactive `Rscript {0}` runner
`testthat::on_cran()` (`if NOT_CRAN=='' then !interactive()`) is TRUE ‚Üí
all 3 files‚Äô `skip_on_cran()` fires ‚Üí every test SILENTLY SKIPS ‚Üí
`stop_on_failure` is blind to skips ‚Üí the scheduled job goes GREEN
running nothing. My own glossary \[regression-read\] names this exact
footgun, and the sub-plan‚Äôs verify command carried `NOT_CRAN`, but its
¬ß7 CI spec OMITTED it and I propagated the gap. **Reproduced firsthand**
(NOT_CRAN unset, non-interactive ‚Üí `nb:4 skipped:4 failed:0`; my local
pass only held because I set `NOT_CRAN=true`). Fix = `NOT_CRAN:'true'`
at job env + a positive `if (sum(res$passed)==0L) stop()` guard so the
silent-skip class fails loud. **(c)** Non-obvious browser-E2E CI
requirements the parent ¬ß7 omitted: must **INSTALL the package**
(`R CMD INSTALL .` after `setup-r-dependencies` ‚Äî `inst/shinytest/app.R`
does
[`library(nprcgenekeepr)`](https://rmsharp.github.io/nprcgenekeepr/),
`create_test_app()` uses `system.file("shinytest", package=)`; pure-R ‚Üí
fast, no `src/`); the renv autoloader makes `R CMD INSTALL` target
renv‚Äôs PRIVATE lib which the AppDriver subprocess (starts in the
installed `shinytest/` dir, no project `.Rprofile`) can‚Äôt see ‚Üí
`RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED:'false'` to install to the SITE lib; the
`find_chrome()` assert must check a single EXISTING path (`nzchar(NULL)`
passes vacuously); `browser-actions/setup-chrome@v2` `chrome-path` ‚Üí
`CHROMOTE_CHROME` via `$GITHUB_ENV`; chromote auto-adds `--no-sandbox`
when `CI` is set (no manual flag on ubuntu 24.04). **(d)** The
adversarial review EARNED ITS KEEP + I re-verified firsthand
(\[completeness-workflow\]/\[right-sized-orchestration\]: the workflow
CONFIRMS, I verify): a read-only 5-agent workflow (4 lenses \[gh-actions
‚Äñ chromote-chrome ‚Äñ r-pkg-install ‚Äñ conformance\] + a
completeness-critic) caught the HIGH `NOT_CRAN` blocker (2 lenses), the
renv-lib-path omission (critic), and the `find_chrome` vacuity ‚Äî I
reproduced the blocker firsthand and confirmed the critic‚Äôs 2 flagged
false-positives (R CMD INSTALL does NOT build vignettes; the
find_chrome-NULL path is unreachable since CHROMOTE_CHROME is set
first), rather than trusting the agents. Grounded the design in
researched current best practice (<setup-chrome@v2> latest = v2;
ubuntu-latest ships Chrome; chromote CI defaults) and validated the
EXACT run-step + Chrome-assert R locally. **(e)** Static-only CI
verification HONESTY (not FM \#24): the live GitHub run was DEFERRED
(owner chose static+adversarial; branch `add-methodology` not on remote
‚Üí a live run creates a remote feature branch + outward CI). Verify
statically (YAML parse + adversarial review + the exact run-step R
validated locally green = 37/0/0/0) AND flag the ONE thing not
statically verifiable ‚Äî the renv lib-path / AppDriver-subprocess
interaction ‚Äî as the **\#1 live-run watch item**; don‚Äôt treat
static-clean as runtime-clean. No R/test code changed ‚Üí `document()` N/A
(no roxygen), `tests/`+`.github` lint-exempt, full non-e2e suite 0
failed/0 error (5 pre-existing `modPyramid` warnings, unchanged S31
baseline); CI infra ‚Üí CHANGELOG only, no NEWS. **Reflexes:**
\[verify-first\]\[ci-suite-parity\]\[regression-read\]\[completeness-workflow\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[author-decision\]\[refactor-only\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[news-vs-changelog\].
**Apply:** for a CONFIG/CI-infra sub-phase ‚Äî run the verify-first spike
to settle whether there is any RED‚ÜíGREEN (often none ‚Üí declare
config-only, gate the classification via `AskUserQuestion`, no synthetic
RED); when authoring CI that RUNS the suite, replicate the local verify
env (`NOT_CRAN`+the opt-in var) and guard the silent-skip class
(`sum(passed)>0`); for browser-E2E CI also install the package + disable
the renv autoloader + assert `find_chrome()` resolves to an existing
path; verify the YAML statically +
adversarial-review-then-re-verify-firsthand, and flag the
not-statically-verifiable bits as explicit live-run watch items.

#### Learning 33 ‚Äî second CONFIG/run-and-observe sub-phase; right-sizing the orchestration DOWN (no workflow w/o ultracode); validate the EXACT broadened CI filter (S33, Phase 8c / issue \#39)

Implemented Phase 8c of the conversion: run-and-observe the 15 shallow
per-module E2E files (103 tests) green opt-in + broaden the CI run-step
filter in `.github/workflows/shinytest2.yaml` to the 18 verified 8b+8c
files. **(a)** The \#32 config-only pattern REPEATS for 8c but THINNER:
not just the test files but ALSO the 8a helpers already exist, so the
verify-first spike is *pure run-and-observe* ‚Äî no triage, nothing to
write. The per-module-group browser spike was 103/103 green (input 19 /
pedigree 19 / pyramid 12 / genetic-value 22 / summary 8 / breeding 23,
0/0/0) ‚Üí classify config/run-and-observe via `AskUserQuestion` with the
spike evidence in hand (no synthetic RED ‚Äî Learning \#18c/#32). The
deliverable‚Äôs only ARTIFACT is a one-line `filter` broaden (vs \#32‚Äôs
6-edit CI rewrite). **(b)** \[right-sized-orchestration\] cuts BOTH WAYS
‚Äî and the cut depends on the ultracode state AND the change surface.
Ultracode was **OFF** this session (no keyword, no budget directive, no
confirming system-reminder), so the standing ‚Äúauthor a workflow by
default‚Äù did NOT apply; S31/S32 ran workflows because they were under
ultracode AND had a real verification surface, whereas a one-line filter
broaden validated end-to-end locally is honestly ‚Äúalready verified‚Äù ‚Üí I
ran NO workflow and SAID SO. Declining a workflow is the same reflex as
running one (#32 ran a 5-agent review for a 6-edit rewrite; \#33
declined for a 1-line change) ‚Äî the discipline is matching the
orchestration to the surface, not always-orchestrating. **(c)** A
broadened `test_dir(filter=‚Ä¶)` regex is itself a \[verify-first\]
target: the S32 handoff‚Äôs ‚Äúbroaden **toward `^(app|e2e)-`**‚Äù ‚Äî taken
literally ‚Äî would pull in the 5 UNVERIFIED Phase-8d files. Narrowed to a
positive 8b+8c include-list and PROVED firsthand it selects EXACTLY 18
files / excludes EXACTLY the 5 8d, by replicating testthat‚Äôs
stripped-name match in R
(`sub("[.][rR]$","",sub("^test[-_]?","",files))` then
`grepl(filt, stripped)`) ‚Äî do NOT trust the regex by eye. Then re-ran
the EXACT run-step expression
(`res<-as.data.frame(test_dir(filter=‚Ä¶, stop_on_failure=TRUE))` + the
`sum(passed)==0` guard) in a SINGLE process ‚Üí 18 files / passed=140 /
0/0/0, exit 0 ‚Äî which simultaneously discharges the ¬ß5(8c)
**AppDriver-process-count dragon** (15+ files √ó drivers in one
`test_dir`; no flaky timeout / resource exhaustion). The single-process
run-step validation is the Phase-3E analog for a CI-config change (the
suite IS the runtime). **(d)** ‚ÄúGREEN‚Äù on a `navbarPage` hidden-DOM
suite (¬ß2.3) means harness-EXECUTABLE, not behavior-VALIDATED ‚Äî
confirmed firsthand by two masked defects that still pass: pyramid‚Äôs
`navigate_to_tab(app,"Age-Sex Pyramid","Pyramid")` 3rd arg is the
ignored `fallback` (it navigates the TOP-LEVEL tab; modPyramid‚Äôs
‚ÄúPlot‚Äù/‚ÄúStatistics‚Äù sub-tabs are never targeted, yet `grepl`-body
passes), and summary-statistics-module navigates to the WRONG tab
(‚ÄúGenetic Value Analysis‚Äù) in 7/8 tests and still passes (¬ß2.4). All 6
nav labels DO exactly match real navbar titles (appUI.R:24-204) so
navigation genuinely occurs (read-back TRUE) ‚Äî but even a silent no-op
would pass via the whole-page body. These are 8e items, NOT 8c blockers
(don‚Äôt fix). **(e)** Live GitHub run DEFERRED again (branch not on
remote, same posture as S32) ‚Äî broadening the filter does NOT change
S32‚Äôs \#1 live-run watch item (the renv-lib-path / AppDriver-subprocess
interaction is the SAME risk category whether CI runs 3 or 18 files). No
R/test code changed ‚Üí `document()` N/A, `tests/`+`.github` lint-exempt,
no NEWS (CI infra ‚Üí CHANGELOG only). \[macos-dupe-scan\]: the `* 2.*`
hits are all `.Rproj.user/` RStudio session state (gitignored), NOT the
source-tree `R/foo 2.R`/`test_foo 2.R` class ‚Äî
`git status --porcelain '*2.*'` was empty, so the
`document()`/`test_dir` corruption risk is absent. **Reflexes:**
\[verify-first\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[author-decision\]\[regression-read\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[refactor-only\]\[news-vs-changelog\]\[macos-dupe-scan\].
**Apply:** for a second/Nth config/run-and-observe sub-phase ‚Äî run the
spike (pure run-and-observe when files+helpers already exist; classify
config-only via `AskUserQuestion`, no synthetic RED); RIGHT-SIZE the
orchestration to BOTH the change surface and the ultracode state
(decline a workflow for a one-line change validated end-to-end locally
and SAY SO ‚Äî declining is the same reflex as running); treat a broadened
test-filter regex as a \[verify-first\] target ‚Äî prove it selects
EXACTLY the intended files and excludes the rest (replicate testthat‚Äôs
stripped-name match in R), then re-run the EXACT run-step in ONE process
(discharges the process-count dragon); remember ‚Äúgreen‚Äù on a navbarPage
hidden-DOM suite = harness-executable, not behavior-validated (the
masked wrong-tab / no-op navigations are 8e, not blockers).

#### Learning 34 ‚Äî third CONFIG/run-and-observe sub-phase; the spike PREVENTED helper work; a single validation run MASKED a process-count flake the ultracode review caught; close \#39 + document (S34, Phase 8d / issue \#39)

Implemented Phase 8d of the conversion (the FINAL \#39 sub-phase): the 5
interaction/menu E2E files green opt-in + broaden the CI run-step filter
to the full `^(app|e2e)-` tier (23 files) + **close \#39** + file 8e
(#40). **(a)** The config/run-and-observe pattern repeats, but 8d had a
REAL spike (¬ß8.2 navbarMenu) that could have meant helper work ‚Äî and
\[verify-first\] PREVENTED it (a spike can SHRINK scope, not just
de-risk): `set_inputs(mainNavbar="Settings"/"About"/"Help")` ‚Üí
`get_value(input="mainNavbar")` reads the child label back TRUE for all
3 (navbarMenu children are tab inputs just like top-level tabs), so the
provisional `navigate_to_menu_item` delegate-to-`navigate_to_tab` body
is already FINAL ‚Äî no DOM dropdown-open+click, no RED‚ÜíGREEN. Running the
spike BEFORE classifying turned the handoff‚Äôs ‚ÄúMEDIUM-HIGH üêâ, may need
a real helper‚Äù into config/run-and-observe; classify via
`AskUserQuestion` with the 53/53 evidence in hand (no synthetic RED).
The only code touch: finalize the docstring (body byte-`identical`) +
the CI filter. **(b)** \[flake-aware-validation\] ‚Äî the headline lesson,
and a direct CORRECTION of Learning \#33‚Äôs claim that the single-process
run ‚Äúdischarges the AppDriver-process-count dragon‚Äù: at 18 files it
didn‚Äôt bite, but broadening to **23 in one process** SURFACED it. A
SINGLE green full-tier run (my 193/0/0/0) proved nothing about stability
‚Äî ‚Äúbroaden + **run once**‚Äù (the S33 handoff‚Äôs own phrasing) is exactly
what HIDES an intermittent flake. The ultracode 4-lens review caught it
(its run \#1 = 191/0/0/**1 error** in `workflow-integration.R`
‚Äúmaintains state‚Äù; isolated 8/8/8) ‚Äî and the review‚Äôs OWN 4 concurrent
agents pressuring Chrome is itself the trigger (contention-sensitivity).
\[completeness-workflow\]: the review CONFIRMS, I verify ‚Äî REPRODUCED
firsthand (my 2 fresh dedicated runs BOTH clean ‚Üí low-rate ~1/5,
contention-driven) rather than trusting the agent or panicking. **(c)**
A flake found mid-close is a SCOPE FORK, not a thing to paper over
(\[author-decision\]): under `stop_on_failure=TRUE` even a low-rate
flake reds the scheduled job, so I brought
NEW-information-not-at-the-original-gate to the owner as an
`AskUserQuestion` ‚Äî close \#39 + document (chosen) vs harden-now
(per-¬ß5(8c) ‚Äúrun grouped‚Äù fresh processes) vs keep-narrower. Hardening
that can ONLY be validated on the live GitHub runner (the flake is
environmental + the branch isn‚Äôt on remote) is DEFERRED to \#40, NOT
shipped unvalidated (shipping unvalidatable CI complexity is its own
FM-#24-adjacent risk). **(d)** \[right-sized-orchestration\] under
ultracode = run the review HERE ‚Äî the EXACT inverse of \#33‚Äôs honest
decline: \#33 had ultracode OFF + a 1-line change ‚Üí no workflow; \#34
had ultracode ON + a consequential DECISION surface (close a long-lived
tracking issue? is \#40 complete? is the diff minimal?) even though the
CODE surface was trivial (a comment + a 1-line filter). The panel earned
its keep: Lens-1/3 all-confirm (diff comment-only + minimal; \#40
captures every ¬ß2.4/¬ß2.5/¬ß6 item + the new navbarMenu false-positive);
Lens-4 caught a real **`.DS_Store` BLOCKER** ‚Üí commit with EXPLICIT
`git add` of only the 2 intended files (never `-A`; the pre-existing
macOS binary must not ride along ‚Äî \[macos-dupe-scan\]‚Äôs commit-hygiene
sibling); Lens-2 caught the flake. Match the orchestration to the
DECISION surface, not just the code surface. **(e)** Honesty nuances:
the spike resolved to ‚Äúthe input VALUE reaches the navbarMenu child‚Äù ‚Äî
NOT ‚Äúnavigation works‚Äù; the visible pane does NOT truly switch (navbar
‚ÄúMore‚Äù highlights; `grepl(body)` passes via ¬ß2.3 hidden-DOM) ‚Üí the ¬ß8.3
false-positive, deferred to 8e/#40 (do NOT overstate the spike). \#39
closed on the owner‚Äôs ACTUAL scope (¬ß1.1 ‚Äúexecutable + CI green opt-in‚Äù
= LOCAL validation + wired CI); TWO live-run watch items now ride on the
first master run (renv lib-path + the flake). Diff comment-only + CI ‚Üí
`document()` N/A, `tests/`+`.github` lint-exempt, no `* 2.*` source
dupes (only `.Rproj.user/`, gitignored); non-e2e regression 0 failed/0
error (2159 passed, 156 e2e-skipped, 5 pre-existing `modPyramid`
warnings); test-infra/CI ‚Üí CHANGELOG only, no NEWS. **Reflexes:**
\[verify-first\]\[flake-aware-validation\]\[completeness-workflow\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[author-decision\]\[regression-read\]\[refactor-only\]\[ci-suite-parity\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[macos-dupe-scan\]\[news-vs-changelog\].
**Apply:** for the final config/run-and-observe sub-phase with a real
spike ‚Äî run the spike FIRST (\[verify-first\]); it can SHRINK scope (a
navbarMenu `set_inputs` read-back works ‚Üí no helper change, no
RED‚ÜíGREEN) ‚Üí classify config-only via `AskUserQuestion`. NEVER trust a
single green run of a process-count-/timing-sensitive browser tier
(\[flake-aware-validation\]) ‚Äî validate N times or let an adversarial
review‚Äôs concurrency surface the flake, reproduce firsthand to
characterize rate+trigger, and bring a discovered flake to the owner as
a scope decision (document + defer hardening to a follow-on that can be
live-validated, vs harden now) rather than silently shipping a
known-flaky `stop_on_failure` CI. Run the review when ultracode is ON
AND the DECISION surface is consequential even if the CODE surface is
trivial (it also catches the `.DS_Store`-class commit-hygiene BLOCKER ‚Üí
explicit `git add`).

#### Learning 35 ‚Äî Phase 9: retire the monolith (irreversible delete); a deleted file‚Äôs sole `@import` is load-bearing NAMESPACE fallout the reference-grep misses; full `check()` is a strictly stronger gate than the regression-read (S35, Phase 9 / issue \#27)

The FINAL conversion phase:
[`runGeneKeepR()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runGeneKeepR.md)
‚Üí a
[`lifecycle::deprecate_soft()`](https://lifecycle.r-lib.org/reference/deprecate_soft.html)
alias launching `runModularApp(port=6013L, launch.browser=TRUE)`;
**deleted `inst/application/`** (17 files) + the orphans
`getMinParentAge`/`getLogo`/`shouldShowErrorTab`/`modMinimalTest`;
closed \#27. Strict TDD (RED‚ÜíGREEN gated) + 4 owner `AskUserQuestion`s +
the pre-RED‚ÜíRED / RED‚ÜíGREEN gates. **(a) Pre-flight an IRREVERSIBLE
delete by RE-RUNNING the (stale, Session-21) ¬ß10 grep-inventory as a
read-only multi-modal sweep + a completeness-critic, then
FIRSTHAND-verify every load-bearing claim**
(\[verify-first\]\[completeness-workflow\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]):
`system.file("application")` is the SOLE live ref (R/runGenekeepr.R:19);
`inst/www/` (the modular app‚Äôs `data-ready.js`, appUI.R:9) is a
DIFFERENT directory from the deleted `inst/application/www/` ‚Äî
conflating them breaks the app; `lifecycle` already a dep; all 17 files
tracked ‚Üí `git revert`-able (¬ß15). The sweep even self-contradicted on
`getLogo` (‚Äúexported ‚à¥ keep‚Äù vs ¬ß10 ‚Äúdelete‚Äù) ‚Äî exported ‚âÝ keep when the
SOLE caller is the monolith being deleted (it is a public-API removal ‚Üí
NEWS bullet). **(b) DELETION NAMESPACE FALLOUT ‚Äî the inventory‚Äôs blind
spot** (\[deletion-namespace-fallout\]): `getMinParentAge.R` was an
`@noRd`, 0-caller orphan, BUT it carried the package‚Äôs ONLY
`#' @import shiny`; deleting it silently dropped `import(shiny)` from
NAMESPACE (leaving only the partial `importFrom` list) ‚Üí the modular UI
died with `could not find function "h5"`. Neither ¬ß10 nor the sweep
flagged it (both reasoned about CALLERS, not what the file‚Äôs roxygen
EMITS); the REGRESSION RUN caught it. Fix: relocate `@import shiny` to
`R/nprcgenekeepr-package.R`; re-`document()`; re-verify. **(c) RED an
alias without launching a server**
(\[discriminating-RED\]\[testServer-mechanics\]):
[`testthat::local_mocked_bindings`](https://testthat.r-lib.org/reference/local_mocked_bindings.html)
BOTH `runModularApp` (same pkg) AND `runApp` (`.package="shiny"`) ‚Üí the
test runs clean at HEAD (returns the monolith sentinel, no deprecation)
and post-impl (modular sentinel + deprecation);
[`lifecycle::expect_deprecated()`](https://lifecycle.r-lib.org/reference/expect_deprecated.html)
forces verbosity; `system.file("application", package=‚Ä¶)==""` is a clean
RUNTIME RED for ‚Äúmonolith no longer ships‚Äù (split into its own
`test_monolith_removed.R` and PAIRED into the deletion commit so every
commit stays green). 3 commits: reversible code ‚Üí standalone deletion ‚Üí
docs (¬ß15 single-revert). **(d) The full `devtools::check()` is a
STRICTLY STRONGER gate than the \[regression-read\]** ‚Äî it caught a
PRE-EXISTING, unrelated defect prior sessions‚Äô regression-read never
surfaced: `a2interactive.Rmd`‚Äôs error-list table hardcoded 9
descriptions while
[`getEmptyErrorLst()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getEmptyErrorLst.md)
returns 10 (the NEW-45 `invalidIdChars` field), failing the vignette
build ([`data.frame()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/data.frame.html): 10 vs
9). It surfaced because prior sessions used the regression-read AS the
gate and never ran full `check()`. Owner-gated fixing it as its OWN
`fix:` commit (NOT bundled ‚Äî scope discipline / FM \#8). Final `check()`
= **0 errors / 0 warnings**; only pre-existing NOTEs (non-standard
top-level dev files; a stale `spelling.Rout.save` baseline of ~60 domain
words). **(e)** `a3manual`/`a2interactive` `.md/.html/.R` are
STALE-BY-DESIGN release artifacts (dated Jan 20, pre-Phases-1‚Äì8) ‚Äî do
NOT force-reknit (months of unrelated regeneration noise + heavy-chunk
failure risk); `check()` builds vignettes from SOURCE anyway, so the
source edit is what matters. Re-knit only the actively-maintained
`README.md`/`NEWS.md`. **Reflexes:**
\[verify-first\]\[completeness-workflow\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[author-decision\]\[discriminating-RED\]\[testServer-mechanics\]\[regression-read\]\[document-zero-delta\]\[deletion-namespace-fallout\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[news-vs-changelog\].
**Apply:** when deleting files, account for what each file‚Äôs ROXYGEN
tags uniquely contribute to NAMESPACE (a sole `@import`/`@importFrom`) ‚Äî
re-`document()` + diff NAMESPACE + run a full regression / UI smoke
after deletion; never trust the reference-grep alone
(\[deletion-namespace-fallout\]). Pre-flight an irreversible delete with
a re-run inventory sweep + firsthand load-bearing checks + an owner
gate. Run the FULL `check()` (not just the regression-read) for any
release-affecting deliverable ‚Äî it is the only gate that exercises
vignette-build / examples / spelling; fix any PRE-EXISTING failure it
surfaces as a separate `fix:` commit, not bundled.

#### Learning 36 ‚Äî third PLANNING deliverable; a ‚Äústrengthen tests‚Äù issue hid a PRODUCTION change; a discovery-workflow‚Äôs HEADLINE COUNTS are themselves a verify-first target (S36, Phase 8e plan / issue \#40)

Decomposed issue \#40 (the sub-plan ¬ß6 ‚Äú8e‚Äù follow-on) into
`docs/planning/phase8e-assertion-strengthening-subplan.md` ‚Äî 7
risk-ordered vertical TDD slices for the active-pane assertion
mechanism + tautology/wrong-tab conversion + namespace fix +
determinism + real flows + CI-stability. Planning/architecture session ‚Äî
TDD code-phases INAPPLICABLE (the plan is the deliverable, no code
written; mirrors \#30, FM \#18/#19). **(a) \[production-in-disguise\] ‚Äî
a ‚Äútest-quality‚Äù issue can hide a PRODUCTION change.** \#40 is titled
‚Äústrengthen E2E assertions‚Äù and reads as 5 test-only work items, but
item 5 (stochastic determinism) requires adding a gated
[`set_seed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/set_seed.md)
hook to TWO **exported** server functions
(`modGeneticValue.R`/`modBreedingGroups.R` ‚Äî which I firsthand-confirmed
have ZERO `set.seed`; the issue‚Äôs ‚Äúset.seed in modBreedingGroupsServer‚Äù
was imprecise, it must be ADDED; the RNG is delegated to
`geneDrop`‚Üí`chooseAlleles`/`groupAddAssign`‚Üí`fillGroupMembers`).
`AppDriver$new(seed=)` can‚Äôt control it (seeds startup, not the
click-triggered eventReactive after intervening RNG). Isolated it as
slice **8e-5**: its OWN owner-gated, full RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR + `check()`
slice using the existing exported
[`set_seed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/set_seed.md)
(pins `sample.kind="Rounding"`), gated `getOption/Sys.getenv` so the
default path is unchanged ‚Äî distinct from the test-only
run-and-observe+\[mutation-check\] conversion slices (8e-2/8e-3). This
BREAKS the sub-plan ¬ß11 ‚Äú8a‚Äì8d are test-only‚Äù boundary ‚Üí flagged
explicitly in the plan‚Äôs ¬ß11. **(b) A discovery-workflow‚Äôs HEADLINE
AGGREGATES are a \[verify-first\] target, reconciled against firsthand
greps AND prior authoritative docs**
(\[completeness-workflow\]/\[verify-first\] applied to the workflow‚Äôs
OWN counts, not just its findings). The 23-agent census reported ‚Äú**49**
tautologies‚Äù and per-file `nTestBlocks` summing to **163**; firsthand
`grep -c 'expect_true(TRUE'` = **41** (the census conflated ~8 of a
DISTINCT ‚âà18 `expect_true(nchar(html)>100)` near-tautology class into
the 49), and `grep -c 'test_that('` = **159** (4 census `n=` rows
off-by-one ‚Äî
input-detailed/genetic-value-detailed/error-states/home-navigation each
+1). The census‚Äôs OWN adversarial critic caught the 49-vs-41 conflation;
a SEPARATE adversarial review of the **synthesized PLAN itself** (not
the evidence) caught the 163-vs-159 ¬ß3-table error I‚Äôd propagated from
the census `n=`, plus a home-nav trap (`grepl("Home"/"Input")` at
:155-156 asserts navbar `<ul>` labels NOT inside any tab-pane ‚Üí
`assert_active_pane` would correctly FAIL them ‚Üí carve them out) and a
missing `get_js`/`wait_for_js` contract note. The TIEBREAKER for the
off-by-one rows was the PARENT plan‚Äôs own counts (6/7/13/10). The
workflow CONFIRMS; firsthand greps + prior docs SETTLE. **(c) Review the
PLAN, not just the evidence** ‚Äî running a second adversarial reviewer
over the written document (against the codebase + the issue) is a
distinct, high-ROI step that catches PROPAGATED transcription errors a
forward census can‚Äôt (the ¬ß3 count, the navbar-label trap, the
seed-insertion-point nesting, the appUI:156-vs-159 cite). **(d) The
active-pane mechanism is the load-bearing domain finding seeded for the
executor:** `get_html`/`get_text` serialize the WHOLE hidden navbarPage
DOM (every pane‚Äôs static UI is in the boot DOM ‚Äî the \#30 hidden-DOM
finding, now the thing 8e fixes), so only
`app$get_js(".tab-content > .tab-pane.active ‚Ä¶ innerText")` honors CSS
visibility, gated on `data-value`==title (== mainNavbar value ‚Üí no
`#tab-NNNN` id guessing). Code-readable on the static DOM, but the BS4
runtime `.active`/`.show` toggling + `innerText`-honors-visibility MUST
be confirmed in a live Chrome in slice 8e-1 as a HARD GATE (if it fails,
the helper design changes for every later slice). Confirmed firsthand:
shinytest2 0.5.0 exposes `get_js`/`wait_for_js`/`get_value`; `jsonlite`
is NOT a dep ‚Üí the helper quotes labels with base-R
`encodeString(x, quote="'")`, not
[`jsonlite::toJSON`](https://jeroen.r-universe.dev/jsonlite/reference/fromJSON.html).
**(e)** Verticality + ordering: 8e-1 load-bearing
(mechanism+spike+wrong-tab fix+first file) ‚Üí 8e-2/8e-3 (static-UI
conversion, independent) ‚Üí 8e-4 (namespace, prereq for) ‚Üí 8e-6 (real
flows; 8e-5 DECOUPLED ‚Äî Option-C structural-invariant assertions need no
production change) ‚Üí 8e-7 (CI per-group fresh processes, ORTHOGONAL,
live-runner-validated only ‚Äî FM-#24-adjacent, don‚Äôt claim it fixes the
flake until a live run shows it; pairs with the two S34 watch items).
Planning artifacts (workflow `wf_4ebcdb7f-f4b`) NOT committed; no
runtime/R change ‚Üí Phase-3E N/A (the plan is the deliverable), no NEWS
(planning doc ‚Üí CHANGELOG/handoff only). **Reflexes:**
\[verify-first\]\[completeness-workflow\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[author-decision\]\[idiom-inventory\]\[production-in-disguise\]\[news-vs-changelog\].
**Apply:** when planning a sub-phase that decomposes a
‚Äútest/quality/refactor‚Äù issue ‚Äî classify each work item by whether it
touches PRODUCTION code and isolate those as owner-gated full-TDD slices
distinct from the test-only run-and-observe ones
(\[production-in-disguise\]); treat a discovery-workflow‚Äôs HEADLINE
COUNTS (`n=`/aggregates) as a \[verify-first\] target, reconciling
against firsthand greps + the parent plan‚Äôs counts before citing; run a
SECOND adversarial review over the SYNTHESIZED PLAN itself (not just the
evidence) to catch propagated transcription errors; keep slices vertical
and risk-order the load-bearing browser spike FIRST as a hard gate.

#### Learning 37 ‚Äî first EXECUTION slice of 8e; the hard-gate spike FALSIFIED the plan‚Äôs load-bearing selector, and the bounded diagnostic that found the fix IS the spike succeeding (S37, Phase 8e-1 / issue \#40)

Implemented slice 8e-1: the 4 active-pane helpers
(`get_active_pane_text`/`get_active_pane_value`/`wait_for_active_pane`/`assert_active_pane`)
in `tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R` (browser-free RED‚ÜíGREEN) + the
live-Chrome spike (HARD GATE) + the
`test-e2e-summary-statistics-module.R` conversion (8
tautologies/wrong-tab ‚Üí behavioral active-pane assertions). Strict TDD,
gated. **(a) \[hard-gate-spike\] ‚Äî the load-bearing spike FALSIFIED the
planned mechanism (R1 fired).** The plan ¬ß2.3/¬ß4 specified
`.tab-content > .tab-pane.active` + innerText-honors-visibility, flagged
‚Äúconfirm in live Chrome FIRST, STOP if it fails.‚Äù The spike DISCONFIRMED
it: the modules nest their OWN tabsetPanels, so `.tab-content` is
NON-UNIQUE ‚Äî **5** containers (1 top-level navbar + 4 nested; nested
active sub-panes ‚ÄúInput Format‚Äù/‚ÄúPlot‚Äù/‚ÄúRankings‚Äù/‚ÄúGroups‚Äù),
`querySelector` first-match latched onto the nested ‚ÄúInput Format‚Äù after
the Settings nav (so `assert_active_pane(Settings)`=FALSE, item 4
failed), and a mid-fade nested element made innerText look like it
ignored visibility (hidden Home read 1895). A spike on a load-bearing
assumption is a hypothesis test ‚Äî it can FALSIFY, not just de-risk.
**(b) The bounded read-only DIAGNOSTIC that found the corrected
mechanism IS the spike succeeding** (not a redesign mode-switch). A
second read-only diagnostic spike (no conversion code) proved a
corrected selector ‚Äî the only `.tab-content` NOT inside a `.tab-pane`
(`!t.closest('.tab-pane')`), its direct-child `.tab-pane.active`:
structurally resolved (no dependence on the dynamic
`data-tabsetid=8517`), it tracked EVERY navigation incl.¬Ýthe
navbarMenu(‚ÄúMore‚Äù) ‚ÄúSettings‚Äù child, and ‚Äî correctly scoped ‚Äî innerText
DID honor visibility (hidden Pedigree=0). Because it deviates from the
approved plan‚Äôs mechanism (R1), I brought it to the owner as a
scope/approach `AskUserQuestion` (\[author-decision\]:
apply-fix-and-complete \[chosen\] vs checkpoint-and-stop vs
literal-stop) rather than silently redesigning past the gate (SAFEGUARDS
/ FM \#8); confirmed the corrected helpers 17/17 through a live spike.
**(c) Browser-free unit tests SURVIVE the mechanism rewrite because the
fake stubs key on the JS‚Äôs INVARIANTS, not its exact text** (the \#31
fake-AppDriver-stub idiom extended to active-pane helpers).
`fake_app_pane` discriminates by inspecting the `get_js` arg for the
`data-value` vs `innerText` substring and simulates `wait_for_js`
success only when the embedded label == its own `data_value`;
`fake_app_pane_liar` (wait succeeds but value‚âÝlabel) proves
`assert_active_pane`‚Äôs redundant
`identical(get_active_pane_value, tab_label)` guard. Both substrings
survive the selector change (the corrected JS still reads
`getAttribute('data-value')`/`innerText`), so all 11 new tests / 59
expectations stayed GREEN across the fix ‚Äî the unit layer proves the
CONTRACT (never-throw + pane/content discrimination), the live spike
proves the BROWSER TRUTH. **(d) The wrong-tab RED‚ÜíGREEN and the
\[mutation-check\] are the SAME proof in two directions, and the spike
already captured the RED.** Tests 2‚Äì8 navigated to ‚ÄúGenetic Value
Analysis‚Äù (the ¬ß2.4/#33d wrong-tab defect ‚Äî ‚ÄúSummary Statistics‚Äù is its
own `tabPanel`, appUI.R:156-159), so the converted
`assert_active_pane(app,"Summary Statistics",‚Ä¶)` is FALSE while nav
targets GVA (RED ‚Äî captured live: ‚Äúassert SS on GVA = FALSE‚Äù) and TRUE
once nav‚Üí‚ÄúSummary Statistics‚Äù (GREEN). The post-conversion
\[mutation-check\] (one boot: wrong-tab‚ÜíFALSE, correct-tab‚ÜíTRUE)
re-proves discrimination at the test-file level (the old
`expect_true(TRUE)` passed BOTH). Assert STATIC UI only ‚Äî export-button
labels, the h3 ‚ÄúSummary Statistics and Plots‚Äù, and the bottom
population-genetics guidance HTML that statically defines ‚ÄúFounder
Equivalents‚Äù/‚ÄúFounder Genome Equivalents‚Äù; the founder/quartile TABLES +
rendered plots are data-dependent ‚Üí deferred to 8e-6 (the ¬ß8e-1 dragon-2
deferral, honored). **(e)** Phase-3E / scope: the deliverable lives ONLY
in the test tree (`helper-shinytest2.R` + `test_helper_shinytest2.R` +
the one converted e2e file) ‚Üí the SUITE + the live AppDriver spike ARE
the runtime (the \#31 ‚ÄúPhase-3E N/A; the suite is the runtime‚Äù pattern);
I DID drive the real app (17/17 spike + 8/8 e2e browser run). `tests/`
is `.lintr`-excluded ‚Üí lint-exempt; no `R/` roxygen ‚Üí `document()` N/A;
test-infra ‚Üí CHANGELOG only, no NEWS. Non-e2e regression 0 failed/0
error (2122 passed, 159 e2e-skipped, 5 pre-existing `modPyramid`
warnings). Committed ONLY the 3 intended test files via explicit
`git add` (the pre-existing `.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/audit-html must NOT
ride along ‚Äî \[macos-dupe-scan\]‚Äôs commit-hygiene sibling, \#34d);
transient `/tmp/spike_*.R`+`/tmp/mutation_check.R` NOT committed.
**Reflexes:**
\[hard-gate-spike\]\[verify-first\]\[author-decision\]\[discriminating-RED\]\[mutation-check\]\[regression-read\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[news-vs-changelog\]\[macos-dupe-scan\].
**Apply:** when a plan flags a load-bearing mechanism as a HARD-GATE
spike ‚Äî run it BEFORE any dependent work; it can FALSIFY the planned
mechanism (a non-unique selector / a wrong visibility assumption), and a
BOUNDED read-only diagnostic that finds the corrected mechanism is the
spike SUCCEEDING ‚Äî bring the deviation to the owner as a scope fork
(\[author-decision\]), don‚Äôt silently redesign past the gate. TDD
browser helpers browser-free via fake-AppDriver stubs that key on the
JS‚Äôs INVARIANTS (substrings/contract) so they survive a mechanism
rewrite; prove the live truth with the spike. For a navbarPage with
nested tabsetPanels, scope the active-pane selector to the only
`.tab-content` not inside a `.tab-pane`. The wrong-tab RED and the
\[mutation-check\] are the same proof in two directions.

#### Learning 38 ‚Äî first PURE run-and-observe conversion slice (no defect, no RED); splitting an oversized conversion slice highest-value-first; the active-pane wrong-content mutant must use pane-ABSENT text (S38, Phase 8e-2 home-nav sub-slice / issue \#40)

Implemented the **home-nav + light-app-file** sub-slice of 8e-2:
converted `test-e2e-home-navigation.R` (8 of 10 blocks ‚Üí
`assert_active_pane`; 2 navbar-label carve-outs kept as whole-DOM
`grepl`), and strengthened `test-app-loading.R` +
`test-app-navigation.R`. Unlike 8e-1 (which carried a wrong-tab RED),
this slice has **no defect** ‚Äî the app already behaves and every
navigation targets the correct tab ‚Äî so it is the campaign‚Äôs first
**PURE \[refactor-only\] run-and-observe** conversion: green-on-arrival,
no RED/GREEN, rigor supplied entirely by the \[mutation-check\].
Classified at a single `PRE-RED‚Üírun-and-observe` \[author-decision\]
gate (no synthetic RED ‚Äî Learning \#20a). **(a) Splitting an oversized
conversion slice ‚Äî highest-value-first.** Plan ¬ß5 8e-2 = 11 files / **64
browser-booting `test_that` blocks** (each boots its own Chrome
AppDriver) ‚Üí past one comfortable session and squarely the plan‚Äôs
risk-R3 / ¬ß5(8c) process-count-flake exposure. Surfaced the split as an
owner \[author-decision\] (4 options: home-nav+app / input-family /
input+home+app / all-11); owner chose **home-nav + app files (14
blocks)**. The right first cut is the highest *behavioral* value, not
the literal slice: the 3 `#goto_*` clicks are genuine interactions wired
to `updateNavbarPage(session,"mainNavbar",selected=‚Ä¶)`
(`appServer.R:72-94`), so converting them to
`assert_active_pane(app,<target>,‚Ä¶)` turns a no-op-tolerant body-grepl
into a real pane-switch assertion ‚Äî more coverage per block than
static-keyword rescoping. Input/pedigree/pyramid families deferred to
later 8e-2 sessions (they correctly pass the FULL titles ‚ÄúPedigree
Browser‚Äù/‚ÄúAge-Sex Pyramid‚Äù as `navigate_to_tab`‚Äôs ignored `fallback` arg
‚Äî verified NO hidden wrong-tab defect there, unlike 8e-1‚Äôs
summary-statistics). **(b) The active-pane wrong-content mutant must use
text GENUINELY ABSENT from the target pane ‚Äî `innerText` carries EVERY
static control label.** The \[mutation-check\]‚Äôs wrong-pane arm (after
`#goto_input`, `assert_active_pane(app,"Home")`/`"Age-Sex Pyramid"` ‚Üí
FALSE) and the content-blind contrast
(`grepl("Age-Sex Pyramid Analysis|Bin Size", body)` ‚Üí TRUE while on
Input ‚Äî the hidden pane is in the whole-body DOM) cleanly prove
discrimination. But the *wrong-content* arm first FALSELY passed:
`assert_active_pane(app,"Input","Focal Animals")` returned TRUE because
the Input File-Content radio literally renders ‚ÄúFocal animals only;
pedigree built from database‚Äù (`modInput.R:70`) ‚Äî `innerText` includes
it. Refined to `"Color Scheme"` (a Pyramid-only control) ‚Üí FALSE.
Lesson: a ‚Äúright-pane / wrong-pattern‚Äù mutant needs a string in NO
sibling control of that pane; pick it by reading the module UI, not by
guessing a plausibly-foreign label. **(c) Static-anchor sourcing + the
carve-out as a STRUCTURAL strengthen.** Pattern choices came from
reading each pane‚Äôs module UI for data-independent text (Input h3 ‚ÄúData
Input and Quality Control‚Äù `modInput.R:42`; Pedigree h4 ‚ÄúFocal
Animals‚Äù/‚ÄúDisplay Options‚Äù `modPedigree.R:52,103`; Pyramid h3 ‚ÄúAge-Sex
Pyramid Analysis‚Äù/‚ÄúBin Size‚Äù/‚ÄúColor Scheme‚Äù `modPyramid.R:25-32`) ‚Äî
data-bearing tables/plots stay deferred to 8e-6 (plan R9). The carve-out
generalizes beyond home-nav: navbar `<ul>`/dropdown LABELS live outside
every `.tab-pane`, so `assert_active_pane` would correctly NOT match
them ‚Äî `home-navigation` ‚ÄúNavbar has all main tabs‚Äù/‚ÄúMore menu‚Äù stay
`grepl`, and `test-app-loading` block 2‚Äôs navbar grepl was strengthened
STRUCTURALLY instead ‚Äî `wait_for_element(app,'a[data-value="Input"]')`
(real tab anchors) rather than a body substring the Home pane‚Äôs ‚ÄúGo to
Input‚Äù button text would also satisfy. **(d)** Phase-3E / scope:
test-tree-only ‚Üí the browser run (14 blocks / **22** expectations, net
+2 from the strengthened app files) + the live mutation-check spike ARE
the runtime (the \#31 pattern); `tests/` is `.lintr`-excluded; no `R/`
change ‚Üí no `document()`/NEWS, \[news-vs-changelog\] ‚Üí CHANGELOG only.
\[regression-read\] held the S37 baseline exactly (2122 passed / 0
failed / 0 error, 159 e2e-skipped, 5 pre-existing `modPyramid`
warnings). Committed ONLY the intended files via explicit `git add`
(\[macos-dupe-scan\] commit-hygiene ‚Äî `.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/audit-html
must NOT ride along); `/tmp/mutation_check_8e2.R` not committed.
**Reflexes:**
\[refactor-only\]\[author-decision\]\[mutation-check\]\[verify-first\]\[regression-read\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[news-vs-changelog\]\[macos-dupe-scan\].
**Apply:** a conversion slice with no underlying defect is a PURE
run-and-observe \[refactor-only\] (green-on-arrival; \[mutation-check\]
is the rigor) ‚Äî gate `PRE-RED‚Üírun-and-observe`, do NOT force a synthetic
RED. When an oversized conversion slice must split, cut
highest-BEHAVIORAL-value first (real interactions wired to a state
change \> static-keyword rescoping), as an owner \[author-decision\].
For an active-pane \[mutation-check\], the wrong-content mutant must use
text in NO sibling control of the target pane (`innerText` carries every
static label) ‚Äî source it by reading the module UI. Strengthen
navbar-label assertions STRUCTURALLY (real `a[data-value]` anchors),
never via `assert_active_pane` (labels are outside the panes).

#### Learning 39 ‚Äî Input-family run-and-observe conversion (8e-2 part A, continued): the `innerText` VISIBILITY-MAP drives static-pattern choice; a tautology can encode a FALSE premise (assert pane-active, don‚Äôt fabricate a feature); the active nested-tab guidance HTML is the fallback anchor when a sidebar control is conditionally hidden (S39, Phase 8e-2 Input family / issue \#40)

Converted the 3 Input-family E2E files (`input-module` 5 /
`input-detailed` 6 / `input-tutorial` 8 = **19** browser-booting blocks)
from the content-blind
`navigate_to_tab ‚Üí grepl(get_html_safe(app,"body"))` idiom to
`assert_active_pane(app,"Input",<static>)`. Same class as \#38 ‚Äî PURE
**\[refactor-only\]** run-and-observe (no defect; the Input pane already
renders and `navigate_to_tab("Input")` already targets the right tab ‚Äî
‚ÄúInput‚Äù IS the `tabPanel` title, `appUI.R:120-124`), gated
`PRE-RED‚Üírun-and-observe` **\[author-decision\]**;
**\[mutation-check\]** supplies the rigor (no synthetic RED). **(a) The
`innerText` VISIBILITY-MAP is the load-bearing tool for choosing a
static pattern** ‚Äî extends \#38b (‚ÄúinnerText carries every static
label‚Äù) with the crucial corollary: `innerText` honors CSS
`display:none`, so within the active Input pane the reliably-visible
text is ONLY {sidebar controls NOT inside a false `conditionalPanel`} ‚à™
{the ACTIVE nested-tab content} ‚à™ {nested-tab NAV labels}. HIDDEN ‚Üí
unusable: the Separator radio ‚ÄúComma/Semicolon/Tab‚Äù (its
`conditionalPanel` condition `input.fileType=='fileTypeText'` is FALSE ‚Äî
fileType defaults `fileTypeExcel`, `modInput.R:55-85`), the 3
non-default `fileInput`s, and every non-active nested tab‚Äôs content (the
‚ÄúDownload Errors/Warnings/Cleaned Data‚Äù buttons + `DTOutput`s). So
‚Äúsupports comma/tab-separated‚Äù could NOT assert the hidden ‚ÄúComma‚Äù/‚ÄúTab‚Äù
labels ‚Üí drew anchors from the always-rendered guidance instead (see
(c)). Read the module UI for the DEFAULT input state (which
radios/conditionalPanels are selected) BEFORE picking a pattern; the
browser run is the **\[verify-first\]** check (a hidden-subtree pattern
‚Üí `assert_active_pane`=FALSE ‚Üí caught immediately). **(b) A tautology
test can encode a FALSE premise ‚Äî convert it honestly, never force a
match.** `input-detailed` ‚Äúhas example data option‚Äù was
`expect_true(TRUE)` with a dead `grepl("example|demo|sample|test")`; the
module has NO example/demo-data feature (the only ‚Äúexamples‚Äù text is
incidental ‚Äî ‚Äúthe name used in examples and output‚Äù in the format docs).
Asserting that string would manufacture false coverage. The honest
conversion is NULL-pattern `assert_active_pane(app,"Input")` ‚Äî it proves
navigation actually landed on + made visible the Input pane (a genuine
upgrade over `expect_true(TRUE)`) WITHOUT claiming a nonexistent
feature; flagged the name/feature mismatch in the handoff. Contrast
`input-tutorial` ‚Äúgenotype file support‚Äù (also a tautology) which DID
have real backing ‚Äî File Content radio ‚ÄúPedigree(s) and genotypes ‚Ä¶‚Äù
(`modInput.R:67-70`) + the Genotype-format docs ‚Üí real pattern
`"genotype"`. Rule: classify each tautology by whether its NAMED feature
ACTUALLY EXISTS ‚Äî real ‚Üí assert it; nonexistent ‚Üí NULL-pattern
pane-active + flag, never force-match incidental text. **(c) The active
nested-tab guidance HTML is the fallback static anchor when a sidebar
control is conditionally hidden.** The Input pane‚Äôs first/default nested
tab ‚ÄúInput Format‚Äù renders `includeHTML(input_format.html)`
(`modInput.R:142-157`), so its full text is in `innerText` ‚Äî it supplied
the CSV/comma/tab/format anchors the conditionally-hidden Separator
panel could not (‚Äúcomma-delimited‚Äù, ‚Äútab-delimited‚Äù). When the sidebar
can‚Äôt anchor a pattern (the relevant control sits in a false
`conditionalPanel`), reach for the always-rendered guidance in the
ACTIVE nested tab ‚Äî but confirm it IS the active nested tab (a
non-default nested tab is `display:none` too). **(d)** Phase-3E / scope:
test-tree-only ‚Üí the browser run (19 blocks / 19 expectations ‚Äî a 1:1
swap, net 0) + the live mutation-check spike ARE the runtime (#31
pattern); drove the real app. \[mutation-check\] PASS at all arms ‚Äî
correct `(Input,"Data Input and Quality Control")`‚ÜíTRUE, wrong-pane
`(Age-Sex Pyramid,‚Ä¶)`‚ÜíFALSE, wrong-content
`(Input,"Color Scheme")`‚ÜíFALSE (‚ÄúColor Scheme‚Äù is Pyramid-only, sourced
by READING `modPyramid.R` per \#38b not by guessing), old whole-body
`grepl("Color Scheme",body)`‚ÜíTRUE (content-blind contrast), active-pane
innerText grepl‚ÜíFALSE (sanity). **\[regression-read\]** held the S38
baseline EXACTLY (2122 passed / 0 failed / 0 error, 159 e2e-skipped, 5
pre-existing `modPyramid` warnings, 0 non-e2e offenders). `tests/`
`.lintr`-excluded; no `R/` change ‚Üí no `document()`/NEWS
(**\[news-vs-changelog\]** ‚Üí CHANGELOG only). Commit ONLY the 3 test
files + docs via explicit `git add` (**\[macos-dupe-scan\]** ‚Äî
`.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/audit-html must NOT ride along);
`/tmp/mutation_check_8e2_input.R` not committed. **8e-2 now ~half done**
(home-nav+app S38 + Input S39); PEDIGREE family
(module/detailed/tutorial = 19) and PYRAMID family (module/detailed =
12) remain as SEPARATE sessions (plan R3 / FM \#18/#25). **Reflexes:**
\[refactor-only\]\[author-decision\]\[mutation-check\]\[verify-first\]\[regression-read\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[news-vs-changelog\]\[macos-dupe-scan\].
**Apply:** for an active-pane conversion, choose each static pattern
from the `innerText` VISIBILITY-MAP ‚Äî only default-visible sidebar
controls + the ACTIVE nested-tab content + nested-tab nav labels are
present; `conditionalPanel`s with a false condition and non-active
nested tabs are `display:none` and unusable (read the module UI for the
DEFAULT state; the browser run is the \[verify-first\]). Convert a
tautology by whether its named feature EXISTS ‚Äî real ‚Üí assert it;
nonexistent ‚Üí NULL-pattern `assert_active_pane(app,"<Tab>")` + flag,
never force-match incidental text. When a sidebar control is
conditionally hidden, anchor on the always-rendered guidance HTML in the
active nested tab.

#### Learning 40 ‚Äî Pedigree-family run-and-observe conversion (8e-2 part B): the genuine-grepl-vs-tautology conversion split, the genuine-to-NULL data-dependent sub-case, and a PRE-GATE adversarial refutation of the whole map (S40, Phase 8e-2 Pedigree family / issue \#40)

Converted the 3 Pedigree-family E2E files (`pedigree-module` 5 /
`pedigree-detailed` 6 / `pedigree-tutorial` 8 = **19** browser-booting
blocks) from the content-blind
`navigate_to_tab ‚Üí grepl(get_html_safe(app,"body"))` idiom to
`assert_active_pane(app,"Pedigree Browser",<pattern>)`. Same class as
\#38/#39 ‚Äî PURE **\[refactor-only\]** run-and-observe (no defect; the
Pedigree pane already renders, ‚ÄúPedigree Browser‚Äù IS the `tabPanel`
title `appUI.R:130`, and `navigate_to_tab`‚Äôs 3rd `fallback` arg is an
explicit documented NO-OP `helper-shinytest2.R:245-250` ‚Äî re-verified
firsthand, NO wrong-tab defect), gated `PRE-RED‚Üírun-and-observe`
**\[author-decision\]**; **\[mutation-check\]** supplies the rigor (no
synthetic RED). **(a) The conversion is a PRINCIPLED SPLIT by the OLD
assertion‚Äôs kind, not a uniform rewrite.** Two classes need different
treatment: a **genuine `expect_true(grepl(orig, html))`** assert already
encodes real intent ‚Üí KEEP its original regex verbatim and only rescope
the haystack to the active pane (faithful refactor: same pattern,
tighter scope ‚Äî module L6/L25/L42/L76, detailed L6/L25/L82, both
dragons); a **`expect_true(TRUE)` tautology** (with a dead
`has_* <- grepl(...)`) has NO behavior to preserve ‚Üí free to choose a
PRECISE default-visible anchor for the named control (‚ÄúDisplay Unknown
IDs‚Äù/‚ÄúFocal Animals‚Äù/‚ÄúChoose CSV file‚Äù/‚ÄúTrim pedigree‚Äù/‚ÄúUpdate Focal
Animals‚Äù/‚ÄúClear Focal Animals‚Äù, `modPedigree.R:52,72,79,86,105,118`).
Don‚Äôt uniformly substitute precise anchors (loses faithfulness on the
genuine asserts) nor uniformly keep-regex (the dead tautology regexes
were never asserted ‚Äî a precise anchor is strictly more honest and
discriminating). **(b) A GENUINE assert can ALSO become NULL ‚Äî the
data-dependent sub-case, distinct from \#39‚Äôs nonexistent-feature
NULL.** \#39‚Äôs honest-tautology rule NULLs a tautology whose named
feature doesn‚Äôt exist. Here a *real*
`expect_true(grepl("table|dataTable", html))` assert (module L59,
detailed L63) must ALSO convert to NULL-pattern
`assert_active_pane(app,"Pedigree Browser")` ‚Äî not because the table is
fake, but because it is **data-dependent**:
`DT::DTOutput(ns("pedigreeTable"))` is gated by `req(pedigreeData())`
(`modPedigree.R:150,306`) and these tests load no studbook, so the table
renders an EMPTY div with no innerText. The original regex matched only
because ‚Äútable‚Äù appears elsewhere in the whole-body DOM (CSS/JS/the
`pedigreeTable` id ATTRIBUTE) ‚Äî exactly the content-blindness the
conversion closes. So the test of ‚Äúkeep the regex‚Äù is whether at least
one alternative is DEFAULT-VISIBLE in the pane innerText; if NONE is
(the target is data-bearing), drop to NULL + flag the content assertion
as deferred to 8e-6 (same destination as \#39‚Äôs data-bearing deferral;
DataTables ‚ÄúShow X entries‚Äù pagination, tutorial L28, is the same
data-dependent case). Result: 4 honest NULLs (module L59, detailed L63,
detailed L101 \[nonexistent ‚Äústatus filter‚Äù\], tutorial L28), 15 content
patterns; a NULL `assert_active_pane` still genuinely upgrades the old
`expect_true(TRUE)`/content-blind grepl (it catches a wrong-tab /
silent-no-op nav). **(c) The always-rendered LEFT-panel guidance HTML
carries the dragon column-keywords ‚Äî extends \#39(c) from the
active-nested-tab guidance to the unconditional left panel.** Both
dragons (`pedigree-detailed:57`
`sire|dam|parent|offspring|ancestor|descendant`, `pedigree-tutorial:174`
`sire|dam|sex|birth|exit|age|gen|population`) resolve against
`inst/extdata/ui_guidance/pedigree_browser.html` (`includeHTML` at
`modPedigree.R:41-44`), which lists ‚ÄúEgo ID, Sire ID, Dam ID, Sex,
Generation, and Population columns‚Ä¶ Birth Date, Exit Date, Age‚Äù. Unlike
\#39‚Äôs Input case (the anchor lived in the ACTIVE nested tab, so
nested-tab state mattered), modPedigree has no nested tabsetPanel ‚Äî the
guidance column is unconditionally visible, so the column names are
reliable static anchors. (Honesty self-correction: my draft cited a
helpText ‚Äúancestors of focal animals‚Äù; the actual
`modPedigree.R:124-127` text is ‚Äúrelatives of the focal animals‚Äù ‚Äî the
critic caught the misquote; verdict unaffected since guidance ‚ÄúSire ID,
Dam ID‚Äù independently satisfies `sire|dam`.) **(d) A PRE-GATE
adversarial refutation of the WHOLE map ‚Äî verify the conversion before
committing the owner to the gate, not after.** Rather than pose the gate
on a self-authored map and discover a bad pattern only in the (slow,
19-Chrome-boot) browser run, ran a 4-agent workflow (3 per-file skeptics
each told to default-to-refuted + a cross-checking critic) over all 19
proposed patterns BEFORE the `AskUserQuestion`: **0/19 refuted**, critic
GO, every pattern confirmed default-visible, the 4 NULLs confirmed
honest (no overlooked static anchor), and the \[mutation-check\] labels
‚ÄúColor Scheme‚Äù/‚ÄúBin Size‚Äù confirmed FOREIGN (grep: only
`modPyramid.R:31-32`). This is \[right-sized-orchestration\] applied to
a conversion slice (a MEDIUM-risk 19-block multi-file map earns a
read-only refutation sweep; the browser run remains the authoritative
\[verify-first\]) and a \[completeness-workflow\] (the critic hunts for
an overlooked honest anchor that would make a NULL dishonest). Cheap
relative to a wasted browser cycle; the owner gate then carried a
verified map. **(e)** Phase-3E / scope: test-tree-only ‚Üí the browser run
(19 blocks / 19 expectations ‚Äî 1:1 swap, net 0) + the live
mutation-check spike ARE the runtime (#31 pattern); drove the real app.
**\[regression-read\]** non-e2e **2162 passed / 0 failed / 0 error / 0
non-e2e offenders** (156 skipped, 5 pre-existing `modPyramid` warnings)
‚Äî the e2e-only change self-skips at `create_test_app()`
(`helper-shinytest2.R:196`) so non-e2e counts are definitionally
unaffected (count differs from S39‚Äôs reported 2122 only because this
invocation set `NOT_CRAN=true`, surfacing more non-e2e tests ‚Äî
orthogonal to the change, NOT a regression). `tests/` `.lintr`-excluded;
no `R/` change ‚Üí no `document()`/NEWS (**\[news-vs-changelog\]** ‚Üí
CHANGELOG only). Commit ONLY the 3 test files + docs via explicit
`git add` (**\[macos-dupe-scan\]** ‚Äî `.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/audit-html
must NOT ride along); `/tmp/mutation_check_8e2_pedigree.R` not
committed. **8e-2 now ~3/4 done** (home-nav+app S38 + Input S39 +
Pedigree S40); the PYRAMID family (`pyramid-module`/`pyramid-detailed` =
12) is the last 8e-2 cut, a SEPARATE session (plan R3 / FM \#18/#25).
**Reflexes:**
\[refactor-only\]\[author-decision\]\[mutation-check\]\[verify-first\]\[regression-read\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[news-vs-changelog\]\[macos-dupe-scan\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[completeness-workflow\].
**Apply:** for an active-pane conversion, split by the OLD assertion‚Äôs
kind ‚Äî genuine `grepl` asserts KEEP their regex (rescope only);
tautologies get a precise default-visible anchor. A genuine assert whose
target is DATA-DEPENDENT (no default-visible innerText, e.g.¬Ýa
`req()`-gated `DTOutput`) drops to NULL-pattern + defer-to-8e-6, just
like a nonexistent-feature tautology ‚Äî the test is ‚Äúis ‚â•1 alternative
default-visible?‚Äù, not ‚Äúis the feature real?‚Äù. Anchor dragon
column-keywords on the unconditional guidance HTML. Refute the whole
pattern map with a read-only adversarial workflow BEFORE the gate; the
browser run is still the authoritative verify.

#### Learning 41 ‚Äî Pyramid-family run-and-observe conversion (8e-2 part C, FINAL ‚Üí 8e-2 COMPLETE): the NULL test is ‚Äúdoes ‚â•1 regex alternative match DEFAULT-VISIBLE innerText?‚Äù NOT ‚Äúis the named feature real / data-dependent?‚Äù (refines \#40b); keep a genuine regex VERBATIM even when the test NAME overclaims; and the pre-gate refutation EARNED its keep by correcting 2/12 self-authored over-NULLs (S41, Phase 8e-2 Pyramid family / issue \#40)

Converted the 2 Pyramid-family E2E files (`pyramid-module` 6 /
`pyramid-detailed` 6 = **12** browser-booting blocks) from the
content-blind `navigate_to_tab ‚Üí grepl(get_html_safe(app,"body"))` idiom
to `assert_active_pane(app,"Age-Sex Pyramid",<pattern>)`, the LAST 8e-2
cut. Same class as \#38/#39/#40 ‚Äî PURE **\[refactor-only\]**
run-and-observe (no defect; the Pyramid pane already renders, ‚ÄúAge-Sex
Pyramid‚Äù IS the `tabPanel` title `appUI.R:139`, `navigate_to_tab`‚Äôs 3rd
`fallback` arg the documented no-op `helper-shinytest2.R:250`), gated
`PRE-RED‚Üírun-and-observe` **\[author-decision\]**;
**\[mutation-check\]** supplies the rigor (no synthetic RED). Outcome:
10 keep-regex (module L6/L25/L42/L59/L76/L93; detailed
L6/L25\[üêâ\]/L44\[üêâ\]/L80), 2 tautology‚Üíanchor (detailed L63
`expect_true(TRUE)`‚Üí‚ÄúDownload Plot‚Äù; detailed L99 `nchar(html)>100`‚Üí‚ÄúAge
Plot‚Äù), **0 NULL**. **(a) The NULL test is purely ‚Äúdoes ‚â•1 regex
alternative match DEFAULT-VISIBLE innerText?‚Äù ‚Äî NOT ‚Äúdoes the test‚Äôs
NAMED target exist / is it data-dependent?‚Äù ‚Äî refining \#40b.** My FIRST
map over-NULLed D3 (‚Äúmaximum age setting‚Äù) and D6 (‚Äúdata requirement
message‚Äù) by reasoning from the test‚Äôs NAMED intent (no max-age control
exists; the empty-vs-loaded state is data-dependent). Wrong altitude:
the conversion is a HAYSTACK-RESCOPE, so the only question is whether
the kept/anchored pattern matches something DEFAULT-VISIBLE in the pane.
D3‚Äôs genuine regex `max|maximum|age|limit` matches the always-visible
age labels (‚ÄúAge Unit:‚Äù, ‚ÄúAge Label Size:‚Äù, h3 ‚ÄúAge-Sex Pyramid
Analysis‚Äù) ‚Äî the `max=10/1500/2.0` tokens are numeric widget BOUNDS (not
innerText), and there‚Äôs no max-age control, but ‚Äúage‚Äù IS visible ‚Üí KEEP
(rescope), do NOT NULL. \#40b‚Äôs ‚Äúgenuine‚ÜíNULL‚Äù fires ONLY when NO
alternative is default-visible (the `req()`-gated `DTOutput` whose only
‚Äútable‚Äù hit was a DOM id attribute); it is NOT triggered by a
nonexistent/overclaiming NAME when the regex still matches
incidental-but-visible static text. **(b) Keep a genuine regex VERBATIM
even when the test NAME overclaims; flag the dragon in a comment ‚Äî do
NOT rename/retarget.** D3 stays `max|maximum|age|limit` (the critic‚Äôs
‚Äúrescope to /age\|bin\|unit\|label/ + rename the test‚Äù is a regex+name
change = scope creep beyond a haystack-rescope slice). Faithfulness =
same pattern, tighter scope; the overclaiming name is pre-existing and
not this slice‚Äôs defect to fix. A code comment records that the match is
via static ‚Äúage‚Äù labels, not a real max-age control. **(c) A
content-LENGTH tautology (`nchar>100`, no regex to keep) whose pane has
an always-rendered guidance panel gets a GUIDANCE ANCHOR, not
NULL+defer.** D6‚Äôs test names a ‚Äúplaceholder or instruction shown
without data‚Äù ‚Äî the unconditional `includeHTML(pyramidPlot.html)` (‚ÄúA
Pedigree Age Plot plots an age-distribution of live animals‚Ä¶‚Äù,
`modPyramid.R:55-58`) IS that instruction, always visible pre-data ‚Üí
anchor ‚ÄúAge Plot‚Äù (distinct from D5‚Äôs
`population|distribution|pyramid|demographic`). NULL+defer-to-8e-6 was
over-conservative: the empty-state MESSAGE is static, only the rendered
PLOT is data-dependent ‚Äî and no block here targets the plot. Extends
\#39(c)/#40(c) (guidance-as-anchor) to the FALLBACK case: a
content-length tautology with no surviving regex still gets a real
anchor when the pane carries unconditional guidance. **(d) The pre-gate
refutation EARNED ITS KEEP this session by CORRECTING the map (2/12
refuted), not merely confirming it (S40 was 0/19).** Both refuted blocks
were my over-NULLs (a)/(c); the skeptics+critic, reasoning from the same
source, caught them BEFORE the `AskUserQuestion` gate and BEFORE the
slow 12-Chrome-boot browser run. Adopting the corrections produced the
0-NULL map. This is the payoff case for
**\[right-sized-orchestration\]**/**\[completeness-workflow\]** as a
PRE-GATE: when the author‚Äôs first map is wrong, a read-only adversarial
sweep is cheap insurance that converts a private misjudgment into a
corrected, owner-gateable map ‚Äî the value is exactly highest when
refutedCount\>0. (Verify the critic firsthand ‚Äî it CONFIRMS; here its
source-grounded reasoning was independently checked against
`modPyramid.R` + the guidance HTML before adopting.) **(e) The 0-NULL
outcome contradicted the S40 handoff‚Äôs anticipation** that the
data-dependent `plotOutput` would force NULLs/defers in the pyramid
family. It didn‚Äôt: none of the 12 blocks asserts the rendered
plot/Statistics table ‚Äî every block targets a static sidebar control, a
nav/tab label, the h3, or the unconditional guidance ‚Äî so the pyramid
pane‚Äôs rich static surface yields a real content anchor for all 12. A
handoff‚Äôs per-block anticipation is a hint, not a spec (FM \#6/#20
applied to a handoff): census the actual blocks firsthand. **(f)**
Phase-3E / scope: test-tree-only ‚Üí the browser run (12 blocks / 12
expectations ‚Äî 1:1 swap, net 0) + the live mutation-check spike ARE the
runtime (#31 pattern); drove the real app. **\[mutation-check\]** PASS,
INVERTED vs the Pedigree slice (Pyramid is now the TARGET pane) ‚Äî
correct `(Age-Sex Pyramid,"Bin Size")`‚ÜíTRUE, wrong-pane
`(Pedigree Browser,"Bin Size")`‚ÜíFALSE, wrong-content
`(Age-Sex Pyramid,"Focal Animals")`‚ÜíFALSE (‚ÄúFocal Animals‚Äù Pedigree-only
`modPedigree.R:52`, grep-confirmed foreign to Pyramid; ‚ÄúBin Size‚Äù
Pyramid-only `modPyramid.R:31`), old whole-body
`grepl("Focal Animals",body)`‚ÜíTRUE (content-blind contrast), active-pane
innerText grepl‚ÜíFALSE (sanity). **\[regression-read\]** non-e2e **2162
passed / 0 failed / 0 error / 0 non-e2e offenders** (156 skipped, 5
pre-existing `modPyramid` warnings) ‚Äî S40 baseline held EXACTLY; the
e2e-only change self-skips at `create_test_app()`
(`helper-shinytest2.R:196`). `tests/` `.lintr`-excluded; no `R/` change
‚Üí no `document()`/NEWS (**\[news-vs-changelog\]** ‚Üí CHANGELOG only).
Commit ONLY the 2 test files + docs via explicit `git add`
(**\[macos-dupe-scan\]** ‚Äî `.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/audit-html must NOT
ride along); `/tmp/mutation_check_8e2_pyramid.R` not committed. **8e-2
is now COMPLETE** (home-nav+app S38 + Input S39 + Pedigree S40 + Pyramid
S41); next is **8e-3** (genetic-value / breeding-groups / menu /
workflow), a SEPARATE session. **Reflexes:**
\[refactor-only\]\[author-decision\]\[mutation-check\]\[verify-first\]\[regression-read\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[news-vs-changelog\]\[macos-dupe-scan\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[completeness-workflow\].
**Apply:** in an active-pane conversion, decide NULL by ONE question ‚Äî
‚Äúdoes ‚â•1 pattern alternative match DEFAULT-VISIBLE pane innerText?‚Äù; if
yes (even via incidental static text under an overclaiming test name),
KEEP the genuine regex verbatim (rescope only, flag the dragon in a
comment, never rename/retarget); NULL ONLY when nothing default-visible
satisfies a faithful pattern (a `req()`-gated output). A content-length
tautology with no regex still gets a real anchor when the pane has
unconditional guidance HTML. Run the pre-gate adversarial refutation
precisely because it can CORRECT your map, not just confirm it ‚Äî its
value peaks when refutedCount\>0; adopt corrections, verify the critic
firsthand, then gate. Census the actual blocks; a predecessor‚Äôs
per-block anticipation is a hint, not a spec.

#### Learning 42 ‚Äî Genetic-Value family run-and-observe conversion (8e-3 part B-1, FIRST 8e-3 cut): a tautology that has a DEAD computed grepl pattern ‚Üí REVIVE that exact pattern rescoped (vs \#41‚Äôs fresh-anchor case); the pre-gate refutation NARROWS a revived pattern by pruning FOREIGN alternatives while keeping real-but-data-dependent ones; and a missing-`NOT_CRAN` regression read fakes a 40-pass ‚Äúregression‚Äù (S42, Phase 8e-3 Genetic-Value family / issue \#40)

Converted the 3 Genetic-Value-family E2E files (`genetic-value-module` 7
/ `-detailed` 7 / `-tutorial` 8 = **22** browser-booting blocks) from
the content-blind `navigate_to_tab ‚Üí grepl(get_html_safe(app,"body"))`
idiom to `assert_active_pane(app,"Genetic Value Analysis",<pattern>)`,
the FIRST 8e-3 cut. Same class as \#38‚Äì#41 ‚Äî PURE **\[refactor-only\]**
run-and-observe (no defect; the GV pane already renders, ‚ÄúGenetic Value
Analysis‚Äù IS the `tabPanel` title `appUI.R:148` == the module h3
`modGeneticValue.R:32`), gated `PRE-RED‚Üírun-and-observe`
**\[author-decision\]**; **\[mutation-check\]** supplies the rigor (no
synthetic RED). Outcome: **16 keep-regex ¬∑ 3 REVIVE ¬∑ 1 ANCHOR ¬∑ 2
NULL**. **(a) NEW sub-case ‚Äî a tautology WITH a dead computed grepl
pattern ‚Üí REVIVE that exact pattern, rescoped, NOT a fresh anchor
(#41(c) handled only `expect_true(TRUE)`/`nchar` tautologies with NO
surviving regex).** Three blocks read
`has_X <- grepl(PATTERN, html, ...)` then asserted `expect_true(TRUE)` ‚Äî
the author WROTE the intended check but never asserted it. The most
faithful conversion revives PATTERN against the active pane: D3
`founder|equivalent|FE|genetic` (‚úì‚Äúfounder‚Äù in guidance ‚Äúrare founder
alleles‚Äù + ‚Äúgenetic‚Äù in h3), D6 `report|export|download|summary`
(‚úì‚ÄúExport All/Subset‚Äù download buttons + ‚ÄúSummary‚Äù nested-tab label).
The remaining content-LENGTH tautology (D7 `nchar(html)>200`, no regex)
still gets a guidance ANCHOR (‚Äúranks animals‚Äù, `genetic_value.html`) per
\#41(c). **(b) The pre-gate refutation NARROWS a revived pattern ‚Äî
pruning FOREIGN alternatives while KEEPING real-but-data-dependent ones
‚Äî a distinct correction mode from \#40d/#41d‚Äôs NULL/un-NULL.** T8 (a
revived `focal|display|Show.*entries|search|filter`) was corrected to
just `filter`: four alternatives (focal/display/Show.\*entries/search)
are COPY-PASTE artifacts from another module‚Äôs test and appear NOWHERE
in the GV pane, while only ‚Äúfilter‚Äù matches default-visible text
(‚ÄúFilter View‚Äù/‚ÄúFilter by IDs‚Äù). Contrast D3/D6, whose non-matching
alternatives (founder-EQUIVALENTS, report) are REAL GV concepts rendered
WITH DATA in 8e-6 ‚Üí keep verbatim (faithful to author intent). **Rule:
when reviving a dead multi-alternative pattern, KEEP alternatives that
are genuine pane concepts (default-visible now, or its own data-bearing
output later); DROP alternatives foreign to the module entirely.** A
genuine, already-passing grepl (the 16 keeps, incl.¬Ýthe 3 dragons
M4/D1/T4) stays VERBATIM regardless (#41b) ‚Äî the prune applies only to
REVIVED dead patterns where you are authoring a new assertion. **(c) 2
NULL** (T5 ‚ÄúValue Designation‚Äù, T7 ‚ÄúZ-score‚Äù) ‚Äî data-dependent results
concepts absent from static UI/guidance, no faithful default-visible
alternative ‚Üí assert pane-active only, defer the data-bearing assertion
to 8e-6 (#40b/#41a). **(d) A regression read that OMITS `NOT_CRAN=true`
fakes a regression.** My first **\[regression-read\]** (no `NOT_CRAN`)
returned **2122 passed / 159 skipped** ‚Äî a 40-pass DROP vs the 2162
baseline that looked alarming. But a test-tree-only edit to e2e files
(which self-skip at `create_test_app()` `helper-shinytest2.R:196`)
CANNOT lower non-e2e passes ‚Äî that impossibility is the TELL. Cause:
without `NOT_CRAN=true`, ~40 NON-e2e unit tests with their own
`skip_on_cran()` guards silently skip (skip ‚âÝ fail, so FAIL stayed 0 and
it masqueraded as a clean-but-smaller suite). Re-running `NOT_CRAN=true`
restored **2162 / 0 / 0 / 156-skip / 5-warn / 0 offenders** EXACTLY, and
the 3 GV files showed 0/0/0/22-skip (proving the edit innocent).
Reinforces **\[regression-read\]**: `NOT_CRAN=true` is mandatory, AND a
suspicious pass-count delta from an edit that provably can‚Äôt cause it is
a MEASUREMENT artifact ‚Äî diagnose it firsthand (re-measure / reason
about the skip mechanism), never hand-wave or claim ‚Äúbaseline held‚Äù off
the wrong read. **(e) Family-per-session split extends to 8e-3
(owner-gated).** 8e-3 censused firsthand at **8 files / ~56 blocks**
(~3√ó an 8e-2 session, past the family boundary S38‚ÄìS41 set); per the
plan ¬ß5 ‚Äúmay split if oversized‚Äù + don‚Äôt-bundle (FM \#18/#25) I posed a
pre-RED scope **\[author-decision\]** and the owner scoped THIS session
to the genetic-value family only (breeding-groups family +
settings-about/workflow deferred to follow-ons). Confirms \#38‚Äôs ‚Äúsplit
an oversized conversion slice‚Äù at the next slice; the special
menu/workflow files (navbarMenu finalization of `navigate_to_menu_item`,
visit-N conversion) are distinct higher-value work that belongs in its
own cut. **(f)** Phase-3E / scope: test-tree-only ‚Üí the browser run (22
blocks / 22 expectations, 1:1 swap net 0) + the live mutation-check
spike ARE the runtime (#31 pattern). **\[mutation-check\]** PASS,
INVERTED (Genetic Value Analysis is the TARGET pane) ‚Äî correct
`(Genetic Value Analysis,"Run Analysis")`‚ÜíTRUE, wrong-pane
`(Pedigree Browser,"Run Analysis")`‚ÜíFALSE, wrong-content
`(Genetic Value Analysis,"Focal Animals")`‚ÜíFALSE (‚ÄúFocal Animals‚Äù
Pedigree-only `modPedigree.R:52`, grep-confirmed foreign to GV), old
whole-body `grepl("Focal Animals",body)`‚ÜíTRUE (content-blind contrast),
active-pane innerText grepl‚ÜíFALSE (sanity). `tests/` `.lintr`-excluded
(**\[lint-net-zero\]** N/A by config); no `R/` change ‚Üí no
`document()`/NEWS (**\[news-vs-changelog\]** ‚Üí CHANGELOG only). Commit
ONLY the 3 test files + docs via explicit `git add`
(**\[macos-dupe-scan\]** ‚Äî
`.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` must NOT ride
along); `/tmp/mutation_check_8e3_gv.R` not committed. Next 8e-3 cuts:
**breeding-groups family** (3 files, ~23) then **settings-about +
workflow-integration** (2 files, ~11 ‚Äî finalize `navigate_to_menu_item`
as a true visible-pane check, kill the workflow visit-N false-positive).
**Reflexes:**
\[refactor-only\]\[author-decision\]\[mutation-check\]\[verify-first\]\[regression-read\]\[right-sized-orchestration\]\[completeness-workflow\]\[phase-3E-smoke\]\[news-vs-changelog\]\[macos-dupe-scan\]\[lint-net-zero\].
**Apply:** in an active-pane conversion, split by the OLD assertion‚Äôs
kind ‚Äî a genuine passing grepl stays VERBATIM (rescope only, flag
dragons, never rename); a tautology that carries a DEAD computed grepl
pattern REVIVES that pattern rescoped (prune only alternatives FOREIGN
to the module, keep real-but-data-dependent ones); a
no-regex/content-length tautology gets a guidance anchor; NULL only when
nothing default-visible matches a faithful pattern. Always read the
regression with `NOT_CRAN=true`; if a test-only edit appears to drop the
pass count, suspect a missing `NOT_CRAN` (or another measurement
artifact) before suspecting a real regression ‚Äî re-measure firsthand.
When a slice is ~3√ó a session, pose a pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion`
and do one family.

#### Learning 43 ‚Äî Breeding-Groups family run-and-observe conversion (8e-3 part B-2): a THIRD NULL sub-case ‚Äî a STATIC feature that is present-but-in-an-inactive-NESTED-tab (`display:none`) ‚Üí NULL+defer (vs \#39‚Äôs nonexistent-feature and \#40‚Äôs data-dependent `req()`-gate); nested-tab NAV labels ARE in active-pane innerText while their CONTENT is not; and `sum(nb)‚àísum(failed)` is NOT the passed count (S43, Phase 8e-3 Breeding-Groups family / issue \#40)

Converted the 3 Breeding-Groups-family E2E files
(`breeding-groups-module` 7 / `-detailed` 7 / `-tutorial` 9 = **23**
browser-booting blocks) from the content-blind
`navigate_to_tab ‚Üí grepl(get_html_safe(app,"body"))` idiom to
`assert_active_pane(app,"Breeding Groups",<pattern>)`, the 2nd of three
8e-3 cuts. Same class as \#38‚Äì#42 ‚Äî PURE **\[refactor-only\]**
run-and-observe (no defect; the BG pane already renders, ‚ÄúBreeding
Groups‚Äù IS the `tabPanel` title `appUI.R:166`), gated
`PRE-RED‚Üírun-and-observe` **\[author-decision\]**;
**\[mutation-check\]** supplies the rigor (no synthetic RED). Outcome:
**12 keep-regex ¬∑ 6 REVIVE ¬∑ 1 ANCHOR ¬∑ 4 NULL**. **(a) A THIRD NULL
sub-case ‚Äî a STATICALLY-PRESENT control that lives in an INACTIVE nested
tab (`display:none`) is not in the active-pane innerText ‚Üí
NULL-pane-active + defer.** `modBreedingGroups` right column is a nested
`tabsetPanel` ‚ÄúGroups‚Äù(default)/‚ÄúStatistics‚Äù/‚ÄúGroup Detail‚Äù; the export
`downloadButton`s ‚ÄúExport Current Group‚Äù/‚ÄúExport Current Group Kinship
Matrix‚Äù (`modBreedingGroups.R:83-86`) are STATIC (not `req()`-gated) but
sit in the INACTIVE ‚ÄúGroup Detail‚Äù nested tab ‚Üí `innerText` (which
honors CSS visibility) excludes them, and the guidance HTML has no
export tokens. So D5/T7 (‚Äúexport‚Äù) and T9 (‚Äúkinship matrix export‚Äù) have
no faithful default-visible match ‚Üí NULL, deferred to 8e-6 / a
nested-tab-navigation enhancement. This is DISTINCT from \#39b (a
NONEXISTENT feature ‚Äî T5 ‚Äúinfants with dam‚Äù: no such control in the
modular UI at all, tutorial-only) and \#40b (a DATA-DEPENDENT
`req()`-gated output). One family carried two different NULL reasons (T5
