dictionary lookup `genoDict[genotype[, 2L]]` indexes a name-keyed vector
by a factor, so R uses the factor‚Äôs INTEGER CODES instead of its labels,
producing an inconsistent encoding (the same allele gets different codes
in the first vs second column). With character columns the lookup is by
name and the encoding is consistent (verified: same allele ‚Üí same code =
TRUE for character / FALSE for factor; the combined dictionary is 35
alleles, codes 10001‚Äì10035 ‚Äî vs the buggy per-column-levels ranges
10001‚Äì10018 / 10001‚Äì10023 under factors). The package‚Äôs own
`test_addGenotype.R` feeds `stringsAsFactors = FALSE` input, so the
shipped factor object was the anomaly, not the function‚Äôs contract.
Owner chose to coerce all three columns (full type-correctness). Same
coerce-in-place + `data-raw/` + atomic doc/man/test pattern as Learning
116; full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, idempotent,
and an independent CSV cross-check (all three columns identical to the
shipping `inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv`).

**A predecessor‚Äôs one-line characterization is a lead, not a spec ‚Äî
re-derive the full degradation against the live object.** ‚Äúid still
factor‚Äù was true but framed the task as cosmetic id-only. Probing the
object showed the real degradation was three columns; probing the
*consumer* (`addGenotype`) showed the named column was the irrelevant
one. Had I implemented the literal flag, I‚Äôd have shipped a
half-degraded object that still produced inconsistent genotype codes.
Learnings 110/113/114‚Äôs ‚Äúa flag / file-list is a sample‚Äù extends to a
predecessor‚Äôs scope LABEL: confirm the whole degradation firsthand
before fixing scope.

**A ‚Äútype cleanup‚Äù can be a correctness fix in disguise ‚Äî check how each
column flows through its consumers before calling it cosmetic.** What
looked like stringsAsFactors hygiene was, for the allele columns, the
difference between a correct and an inconsistent genotype encoding,
because factor-vs-character changes how a value is used as an index (and
as a [`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html) argument) downstream. That
made the scope gate a correctness decision (does the shipped example
produce right codes?), not a style preference ‚Äî which is exactly why it
warranted the owner‚Äôs call rather than a silent default.

**Flag the surfaced code fragility; keep the data change atomic.** The
investigation exposed that `addGenotype` is itself fragile to factor
inputs ‚Äî it would mis-encode ANY factor-columned genotype, not just the
bundled one. Hardening `addGenotype.R`
(e.g.¬Ý[`as.character()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/character.html) on the
allele columns inside the function, or in `checkGenotypeFile`) is a
separate code-fix deliverable with its own tests ‚Äî flagged for a future
session, NOT bundled (FM \#8). Fixing the data made the shipped example
correct; it did not make the function robust.

**Reflexes:** \[a predecessor‚Äôs one-line scope label (‚ÄúX still factor‚Äù)
is a lead ‚Äî re-derive the FULL degradation firsthand; the named symptom
may be the least consequential column\]\[before calling a type coercion
‚Äúcosmetic,‚Äù trace how each affected column flows through its consumers ‚Äî
factor-vs-character changes index/[`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html)
semantics, so a hygiene fix can secretly be a correctness fix\]\[when
the data fix exposes a latent CODE fragility (here: addGenotype
mis-indexing factor allele columns), FLAG it as a separate deliverable ‚Äî
keep the data re-export atomic (FM \#8)\]\[reuse the Learning 116
pattern: coerce-in-place via idempotent data-raw, bundle doc+man+tests,
value-preserve in tests + an independent cross-check (the shipping
CSV) + a consumer-on-shipped-object smoke test\]. **Apply:** any session
re-exporting a sibling/related opaque data object on a prior session‚Äôs
characterization ‚Äî verify the full set of degraded columns AND how each
flows through consumers before fixing scope; a column the predecessor
named may matter less than one it didn‚Äôt.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Learning 118 ‚Äî When realizing a predecessor‚Äôs flagged CODE fix, decide the fix‚Äôs HOME by the call graph, not the doc contract ‚Äî direct callers that bypass the documented ‚Äúgate‚Äù mean the only complete fix lives at the point of use. Coerce at the TOP of the function (not just at the buggy index expression) to also neutralize an adjacent version-fragile path. Drive the RED test from a minimal fixture whose per-column factor codes DIVERGE from the global sort. (S125, owner pick A9 ‚Äî harden `addGenotype()` against factor allele columns)

**What happened.** S124‚Äôs Learning 117 flagged that
[`addGenotype()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/addGenotype.md)
is fragile to factor allele columns ‚Äî `genoDict[genotype[, 2L]]` indexes
a name-keyed integer vector by a factor, so R uses the factor‚Äôs INTEGER
CODES not its labels, giving an encoding that is inconsistent between
the two allele columns. S124 deliberately did NOT fix the function (FM
\#8 ‚Äî kept the data re-export atomic) and pre-named the code fix as A9.
This session realized it. Strict TDD: a pre-RED scope gate
(addGenotype-only vs also-checkGenotypeFile), then RED (two failing
tests, 2 failed / 0 error) ‚Üí GREEN (2-line coercion; all green) ‚Üí
REFACTOR (one-line `@details` note + `man/` regen). Verified: full suite
0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, diff confined to three files,
and a Phase-3E smoke on a factor-coerced copy of the real
`rhesusGenotypes` (first/second identical to the character path; max
distinct codes per allele = 1; the 35-allele dictionary 10001‚Äì10035).

**The fix‚Äôs HOME is decided by the call graph, not the doc contract.**
`addGenotype`‚Äôs own roxygen says genotype ‚Äúis to be provided by
`checkGenotypeFile` so it is not checked‚Äù ‚Äî which reads as an invitation
to harden the upstream gate. A read-only breadth scan refuted that as
the complete answer: `addGenotype` has DIRECT callers that never touch
`checkGenotypeFile` (the roxygen example passing `rhesusGenotypes`,
`test_addGenotype.R`, `test_geneDrop.R`), so a gate-only coercion would
leave every direct caller exposed. The only fix that closes the bug for
all callers is at the point of use, inside `addGenotype`. The breadth
scan also confirmed the bug is isolated (no sibling function indexes a
named vector by a possibly-factor column), so the scope stayed narrow.
Lesson: when a function‚Äôs doc says ‚Äúinput is pre-validated upstream,‚Äù
verify who actually calls it before locating the fix at the gate ‚Äî an
unenforced contract is a comment, not a guarantee.

**Coerce at the TOP, not only at the buggy index expression.** The
breadth agent‚Äôs suggested patch wrapped just the two index lookups
(`genoDict[as.character(genotype[, 2L])]`). That fixes the lookup, but
the dictionary BUILD a few lines up
(`sort(unique(c(genotype[, name1], genotype[, name2])))`) still relies
on [`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html) combining two factors ‚Äî
behavior that differs across R versions (pre-4.1
[`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html) dropped factor attributes and
returned integer codes, which would then mis-name the dictionary).
Coercing both columns to character once at the top of the function makes
BOTH the build and the lookup operate on labels, in one place,
version-robustly. When a hardening fix has more than one downstream use
of the unsafe value, normalize at the source rather than patching each
use site.

**A minimal RED fixture for an index-by-factor bug must make the
per-column codes DIVERGE from the global order.** The naive fixture
(both columns the same allele set in sorted order) does NOT reproduce
the bug ‚Äî each column‚Äôs factor codes happen to align with the global
`sort(unique())` positions, so the buggy and correct encodings coincide.
The bug only manifests when a column‚Äôs factor level set/order differs
from the global combined order. The minimal trigger:
`first_name = c("a","b")`, `second_name = c("b","c")` ‚Äî globally
a‚Üí10001, b‚Üí10002, c‚Üí10003, but the per-column factor codes (a,b‚Üí1,2 and
b,c‚Üí1,2) make allele ‚Äúb‚Äù encode as 10002 in one column and 10001 in the
other. Designing the fixture required tracing the exact index
arithmetic, not just ‚Äúmake it a factor.‚Äù Character input is the oracle:
assert the factor-input output is identical to the character-input
output.

**Reflexes:** \[locate a flagged fix by the CALL GRAPH ‚Äî if direct
callers bypass the documented validation ‚Äúgate,‚Äù the complete fix lives
at the point of use, not the gate (an unenforced ‚Äúinput is pre-checked‚Äù
doc is a comment, not a guarantee)\]\[confirm the bug is isolated with a
breadth scan before sizing scope, but verify the scan‚Äôs load-bearing
claims firsthand\]\[when an unsafe value has multiple downstream uses,
normalize at the SOURCE (top of the function) ‚Äî patching only the
obviously-buggy use site can leave an adjacent version-fragile path
(here [`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html)-on-factors in the dict
build)\]\[a RED fixture for an index-by-factor bug must make per-column
codes DIVERGE from the global sort ‚Äî equal/aligned sets won‚Äôt reproduce
it; use character-input output as the oracle\]\[realize a predecessor‚Äôs
deferred code-fix flag as its own RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR deliverable ‚Äî do
not bundle it with the data change that surfaced it (FM \#8/#18)\].
**Apply:** any session implementing a fix a prior session
flagged-but-deferred ‚Äî re-derive the bug firsthand, decide the fix‚Äôs
home by who actually calls the function (not its doc), normalize unsafe
inputs at the source, and build the RED fixture to actually reproduce
(for index-by-factor bugs, diverge the per-column codes).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Learning 119 ‚Äî A data-doc `@source`/provenance citation is NOT an attribute of the live object, so an audit-vs-live-object pass structurally cannot catch it ‚Äî verify it against the FILESYSTEM (cited file exists AND is value-identical to the object), and drive the RED probe off the GENERATED `man/.Rd` so the failing assertion binds to the rendered artifact (which also proves the regen ran). The phantom-citation class is plural ‚Äî grep every sibling citation. (S126, owner pick A10 ‚Äî correct the `rhesusGenotypes` `@source`)

**What happened.** S124/S125 flagged A10: the `rhesusGenotypes` doc
(`R/data.R:345`) cited a source file `rhesusGenotypes.csv` that does not
exist; the real value-identical export is
`inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv`. Strict TDD:
a pre-RED scope gate (filename-swap-only vs +grammar-clarify; owner
chose swap-only) + the three transitions. RED: a probe parsing
`man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd` for its `\emph{}` source token, asserting the
cited file exists in `inst/extdata` + is value-identical to
`rhesusGenotypes`, and that the wrong name is absent from both
`R/data.R` and the man page ‚Äî 4 failures against the current doc. GREEN:
swap one token, regen `man/` (rd roclet only), 0 failures. Verified:
full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, diff confined to
two lines. Phase-3E N/A (doc-only). 0 stakeholder corrections.

**A provenance citation needs a different verification target than a
value claim.** The S114‚ÄìS122 data-doc audits checked doc claims ‚Äúvs live
objects‚Äù ‚Äî dims, column types, NA counts, spot-checked values ‚Äî every
one an attribute you can read off the bundled object. A `@source`
filename is NOT such an attribute: nothing in the loaded
`rhesusGenotypes` records where it came from, so a live-object audit
cannot, even in principle, catch a phantom source file. That is exactly
why `rhesusGenotypes.csv` survived nine sessions of data-doc work. The
right check for a provenance citation is against the FILESYSTEM: does
the named file exist under `inst/extdata`, and is it value-identical to
the object? Do not assume a prior ‚Äúfull data-doc audit‚Äù covered source
citations ‚Äî by construction it didn‚Äôt.

**Bind the RED probe to the generated artifact, not the source
comment.** The fix lives in a roxygen comment (`R/data.R`), but the
thing the user reads is the generated `man/.Rd`. A probe that parses
`man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd` for the `\emph{}` token and checks the cited
file (a) fails now and (b) passes only after BOTH the roxygen edit AND
the `man/` regen ‚Äî so it doubles as a regen check, catching the easy
mistake of editing the comment and forgetting to regenerate. Verifying
the rendered artifact is strictly stronger than verifying the source it
is generated from.

**The phantom-citation class is plural ‚Äî sweep the siblings before
closing out.** A single read-only sweep of every `\emph{...}` citation
in `R/data.R` found the sibling `rhesusPedigree` doc (`R/data.R:358`)
cites `rhesusPedigree.csv`, which also does NOT exist (candidate real
source: `rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv` or
`obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv`), while `ExamplePedigree.csv` (`:18`)
does exist. Flagged as A11, not fixed (FM \#8 ‚Äî A10 was the
deliverable). When you fix one instance of a documentation-claim class,
grep the whole class so the next session inherits a precise, pre-located
list instead of rediscovering it.

**Reflexes:** \[a `@source`/provenance citation is NOT a live-object
attribute ‚Äî verify it against the FILESYSTEM (cited file exists +
value-identical), since an audit-vs-live-object pass cannot catch a
phantom source file\]\[bind the RED probe to the GENERATED `man/.Rd`
(parse the `\emph{}`/source token), not the roxygen source ‚Äî it then
doubles as a regen check\]\[when fixing one doc-claim instance, sweep
the whole class (grep every sibling citation) and flag the others with
candidate fixes, don‚Äôt fix them (FM \#8)\]\[a doc-only `@source` fix is
Phase-3E-N/A ‚Äî no runtime behavior changes; the proportionate
build-equivalent is checkRd + load_all + suite + NAMESPACE byte-diff +
the man-source probe\]. **Apply:** any data-doc or provenance-citation
fix ‚Äî verify the cited file on disk (existence + value-identity), drive
RED off the rendered man page, and grep every sibling citation to hand
the next session a located list.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Learning 120 ‚Äî When more than one shipped file is value-identical to an opaque data object, a filename-based provenance guess can mislead ‚Äî verify each candidate against the object firsthand and cite the exact-shape value-identical export, not a superset. To close the LAST instance of a doc-claim class, re-run the class sweep after the fix to PROVE closure; don‚Äôt inherit ‚Äúthis is the only one left‚Äù from a predecessor‚Äôs note. (S127, owner pick A11 ‚Äî correct the `rhesusPedigree` `@source`)

**What happened.** S126 (Learning 119) flagged A11: the `rhesusPedigree`
doc (`R/data.R:358`) cited a phantom `rhesusPedigree.csv`, with two
candidate real sources ‚Äî `rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv` (which the
predecessor and `data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R` both call ‚Äúthe obfuscation
source‚Äù) or `obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv`. Strict TDD: a pre-RED scope
gate (which file + swap-only vs grammar-clarify; owner chose
`obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv`, swap-only) + the three transitions.
RED: a probe parsing `man/rhesusPedigree.Rd` for its `\emph{}` token,
asserting the phantom is absent and the cited file is present (both
`R/data.R` and the man page), exists, and is value-identical ‚Äî 4
failures (A1‚ÄìA4) against the current doc, preconditions A5‚ÄìA6 passing.
GREEN: swap one token, regen `man/` (rd roclet only), 0 failures.
Verified: full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, diff
confined to two lines. Phase-3E N/A (doc-only). 0 stakeholder
corrections.

**A filename is a hypothesis about provenance, not provenance.** The
name `rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv` reads as ‚Äúthe center‚Äôs original,
pre-obfuscation pedigree,‚Äù and `data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R` even narrates
it as the file the object ‚Äúwas obfuscated from.‚Äù Firsthand probing
overturned that: BOTH shipped candidate CSVs carry the SAME obfuscated
ids as the bundled object (BRI2MW, 677E7M, ‚Ä¶), so neither is
pre-obfuscation real data ‚Äî the un-obfuscated source is simply not
shipped (de-identification). `_fromCenter` is a post-obfuscation export
with an extra flag column, not the input. The only reliable way to know
which shipped file the `@source` should name is to compare values
firsthand; the filename, and even a prior provenance comment, can be
loose.

**When two files are both value-identical, cite the exact-shape twin,
not a superset.** `obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv` is 375√ó8 and
value-identical to the object across all 8 columns.
`rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv` is value-identical only on the 8
*shared* columns but is 375√ó9 (extra `fromCenter` column) ‚Äî a superset.
`@source` should name the file whose shape and values ARE the object,
and it keeps the citation consistent with the sibling genotypes doc
(S126, which cited the exact value-identical export). Cite the minimal
exact match.

**To close the LAST instance of a doc-claim class, prove it ‚Äî don‚Äôt
inherit it.** S126 closed `rhesusGenotypes` and flagged `rhesusPedigree`
as the last sibling phantom. Rather than trust that note, I re-ran the
`\emph{...csv}` sweep over `R/data.R` after the fix: all three citations
(`ExamplePedigree.csv`, `obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv`,
`obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv`) now resolve to existing files. The
class is closed by demonstration, not by inheritance. A predecessor‚Äôs
‚Äúthis is the only one left‚Äù is a lead; a fresh post-fix sweep is the
proof.

**Reflexes:** \[a filename ‚Äî even one a prior script calls ‚Äúthe source‚Äù
‚Äî is a HYPOTHESIS about provenance; confirm which shipped file is
value-identical by comparing values firsthand before citing it\]\[when
multiple files are value-identical, cite the exact-shape twin (same
columns), not a superset with extra columns\]\[to close the LAST
instance of a doc-claim class, re-run the class sweep AFTER the fix and
show every member resolves ‚Äî prove closure, don‚Äôt inherit it from a
predecessor‚Äôs note\]\[reuse Learning 119: provenance = filesystem
check + value-identity, and the RED probe binds to the generated
`man/.Rd`\]. **Apply:** any provenance/`@source` fix where more than one
candidate file exists ‚Äî verify value-identity firsthand (don‚Äôt trust the
filename or a prior provenance note), cite the minimal exact match, and
re-sweep the class to prove it is fully closed.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Learning 121 ‚Äî A REFACTOR-only deliverable (delete provably-redundant code) still runs the strict-TDD gate, but with NO RED phase ‚Äî the existing green suite IS the safety net; you need only a pre-REFACTOR scope gate + the GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR gate. Prove ‚Äúno-op‚Äù by whole-frame `identical(with, without)`, not per-column class checks, and sweep the file with a robust single-quoted ERE grep ‚Äî the handoff‚Äôs named line-ranges can be incomplete, and a BRE `\|`/`\$` sweep silently matches nothing on macOS (BSD) grep. (S128, owner pick A8 ‚Äî remove redundant test no-op conversions)

**What happened.** S123 flagged A8: `test_getPotentialParents.R` and
`test_modPotentialParents.R` re-coerced `rhesusPedigree`-sourced
fixtures‚Äô `id`/`sire`/`dam` to character and `birth` to Date ‚Äî
conversions that became no-ops once S123 re-exported `rhesusPedigree`
with canonical column types (Learning 116). The deliverable was to
delete them. Strict TDD with no RED: established the GREEN baseline
(both files pass), proved the no-op precondition firsthand, gated the
scope (which blocks) then GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR, deleted 12 lines across three
identical-class blocks, and re-verified (both files pass, full suite
0/0, diff confined to the deletions). 0 stakeholder corrections.

**A pure refactor has no RED phase ‚Äî the existing passing suite is the
test.** TDD‚Äôs ‚Äúnever refactor without a test‚Äù is already satisfied: the
code being cleaned is itself test code that passes before and must pass
after. There is no new behavior to drive with a failing test, so writing
one would be theater. What DOES apply is the gate sequence: a separate
pre-REFACTOR scope decision (the author‚Äôs call) plus the GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR
transition gate. Declaring RED here would be a phase-invention error;
skipping the gates would be a discipline error. Correct shape: GREEN
(prove baseline) ‚Üí scope gate ‚Üí GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR gate ‚Üí REFACTOR ‚Üí
re-prove GREEN.

**Prove ‚Äúno-op‚Äù by whole-frame identity, not column class.**
`class(col) == "character"` shows the conversion is *probably* idle; the
airtight proof that removing it preserves behavior is
`identical(frame-after-all-conversions, frame-before)`. That one check
covers every column and attribute at once and generalizes: any fixture
sourced from the same object inherits the proof (here both `pedOne` and
`pedDF` draw from
[`nprcgenekeepr::rhesusPedigree`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/rhesusPedigree.md),
so one identity check licensed deleting all three blocks).

**The handoff‚Äôs named line-ranges are a lead, not a census ‚Äî sweep the
file yourself.** S127‚Äôs handoff named two blocks; a robust ERE sweep
found a THIRD identical block in the same file (`pedDF`, lines 116-119)
it never enumerated, plus two look-alikes that were NOT the class (an
`as.Date` inside age arithmetic at `test_fillBins.R:22`; an
`as.character` inside an `expect_setequal` at
`test_modGeneticValue.R:1274`). Leaving the third would have left the
file internally inconsistent. The first sweep used double-quoted BRE
(`as.character(pedOne\$...\|...`) and matched NOTHING ‚Äî macOS `grep`
(BSD) does not honor `\|` alternation or treat `\$` as intended ‚Äî the
same shell/backslash trap that drives this project to file-based R
probes (Learnings 108/109), now seen in grep. Fix: single-quoted
`grep -rEn` with a real ERE.

**Reflexes:** \[a delete-redundant-code refactor has NO RED phase ‚Äî the
existing green suite is the safety net; run only the pre-REFACTOR scope
gate + GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR gate, don‚Äôt invent a RED probe\]\[prove a
conversion/transform is a true no-op by whole-frame
`identical(with, without)`, not per-column class checks ‚Äî one identity
check licenses every fixture drawn from that object\]\[treat a handoff‚Äôs
named line-ranges as a lead, not a census ‚Äî sweep the file/dir for the
same class with a single-quoted `grep -rEn` ERE; classify look-alikes
(coercions inside arithmetic/assertions are NOT redundant
self-assignments) and leave them\]\[the shell-backslash trap extends to
grep: double-quoted BRE `\|`/`\$` silently matches nothing on macOS BSD
grep ‚Äî use single quotes + `-E`\]\[a test-only refactor is Phase-3E-N/A
‚Äî no runtime/production code changes; the proportionate build-equivalent
is targeted files green + full suite 0/0 + diff confined to the intended
deletions\]. **Apply:** any ‚Äúremove now-redundant code‚Äù cleanup ‚Äî prove
the redundancy is a true no-op (whole-frame identity), sweep the
file/dir yourself with a robust ERE grep rather than trusting the
handoff‚Äôs line list, and run the scope + GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR gates without
inventing a RED phase.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Learning 122 ‚Äî A carried documentation nit‚Äôs wording is a LEAD, not a spec ‚Äî verify the ground truth before applying it literally; the literal fix can be wrong. A vignette doc-fix binds RED to the `.qmd` source + the live object (not a generated `man/.Rd`), and its build-equivalent is a pkgdown/Quarto render ‚Äî which writes HTML to the gitignored `pkgdown_site/` but LITTERS the tracked tree (`pkgdown/` favicons, a `*.rmarkdown` intermediate, an auto `vignettes/articles/.gitignore`); clean those before commit. (S129, owner pick A3 ‚Äî fix the studbook-QC vignette‚Äôs `pedGood` column description)

**What happened.** S116 flagged A3 (carried through S117‚ÄìS128): the
*Studbook Quality Control* article said `pedGood` ‚Äúhas deliberately
messy headers (`ego_id`, `si.re`, `dam_id`, `birth_date`)‚Äù ‚Äî and the
one-line carried framing was ‚Äúomits the `sex` column.‚Äù Strict TDD: a
pre-RED approach gate (rewrite the lead-in vs append a clause; owner
chose the rewrite) + `PRE-RED‚ÜíRED`, `RED‚ÜíGREEN`, and `GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR`
(N/A ‚Äî prose, structure preserved). RED: a file-based probe parsing the
lead-in paragraph from the `.qmd` and binding to `names(pedGood)` ‚Äî 3
ground-truth preconditions pass, 2 RED assertions (lead-in mentions
`sex`; states ‚Äúfive‚Äù) fail. GREEN: a 3-line rewrite ‚Üí 0 failures.
Build-equivalent: `pkgdown::build_article(...)` rendered clean; the new
sentence and the unchanged 4-rename `changedCols` output both verified
in the HTML. 0 stakeholder corrections.

**The literal carried wording would have produced a WRONG fix.** ‚ÄúOmits
the `sex` column‚Äù reads as ‚Äúadd `sex` to the parenthetical.‚Äù But a probe
on the live object showed `sex` is NOT a messy header: `names(pedGood)`
= `ego_id`, `si.re`, `dam_id`, `sex`, `birth_date`, and the article‚Äôs
own `changes` chunk renames exactly the four non-`sex` columns ‚Äî `sex`
is absent from `changedCols` because it already uses the canonical name.
Inserting `sex` into the ‚Äúdeliberately messy headers‚Äù list would have
mislabeled it AND contradicted the chunk‚Äôs rendered output two lines
below. The accurate fix conveys ‚Äúfive columns: four messy + an
already-clean `sex`.‚Äù The earlier, richer S116 framing (‚Äúthe dataset is
5 columns‚Äù) survived in the notes 350 lines down; the compressed
one-liner that propagated forward lost the nuance. Verify the claim
against the live object before applying it.

**A vignette doc-fix binds RED to the source `.qmd` + the live object ‚Äî
there is no generated `man/.Rd` to bind to.** Learning 119‚Äôs
man-page-binding pattern does not transfer directly: a vignette has no
checked-in generated artifact. The strongest available binding is (a)
ground-truth preconditions read from the live object (`names(pedGood)`),
so the test fails if the data ever diverges from the prose, plus (b) RED
assertions on the source paragraph‚Äôs text. The render is the separate
build-equivalent that proves the `.qmd` still compiles and the prose now
matches the chunk output.

**[`pkgdown::build_article`](https://pkgdown.r-lib.org/reference/build_articles.html)
writes to the gitignored output dir but litters the tracked tree ‚Äî clean
before commit.** The HTML landed in `pkgdown_site/articles/...`
(gitignored, line 39), so it never showed in `git status`. But the same
render created three NEW untracked items in the tracked tree: `pkgdown/`
(generated favicons),
`vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.rmarkdown` (a Quarto
intermediate), and `vignettes/articles/.gitignore` (Quarto
auto-created). Capture `git status --porcelain` BEFORE the render, then
`rm -rf pkgdown && rm -f vignettes/articles/.gitignore vignettes/articles/*.rmarkdown`
after, and re-check that the tree is back to the intended set. (Same
family as the stray-`Rplots.pdf` gotcha ‚Äî a build step can drop
artifacts the commit must exclude.)

**Reflexes:** \[treat a carried/handoff nit‚Äôs wording as a HYPOTHESIS ‚Äî
verify it against the live object before applying it; the literal fix
can be wrong (here, `sex` is not a messy header, so it must not join the
‚Äúmessy headers‚Äù list)\]\[for a vignette/`.qmd` doc-fix, bind RED to the
source paragraph + live-object ground truth
([`names()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/names.html)), since there is no
generated `man/.Rd`; the pkgdown/Quarto render is the separate
build-equivalent\]\[render the changed article and verify BOTH the new
prose AND that any nearby executed chunk‚Äôs output stays consistent with
it\]\[[`pkgdown::build_article`](https://pkgdown.r-lib.org/reference/build_articles.html)/`quarto render`
writes HTML to the gitignored `pkgdown_site/` but litters the tracked
tree (`pkgdown/`, `*.rmarkdown`, an auto `.gitignore`) ‚Äî snapshot
`git status` before, clean after, re-check\]\[`vignettes/articles/` is
website-only (`.Rbuildignore`d) ‚Äî no CRAN ship, no NEWS line,
quarto/pkgdown are dev-lib only\]. **Apply:** any documentation/vignette
fix carried as a one-line nit ‚Äî re-derive the ground truth firsthand,
drive RED off the source + live object, render as the build-equivalent,
and clean the render‚Äôs tracked-tree litter before committing.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Learning 123 ‚Äî To rewrite a large doc section deterministically, write the new section to a FILE and SPLICE it in by heading boundaries with R (read lines, find the start heading + the next heading, recombine head/new/tail) ‚Äî far more robust than a fragile multi-hundred-line Edit match. For a NEWS rewrite: bind RED to the rendered `NEWS.md`; fold the user-facing delta from the per-session CHANGELOG/handoffs (DROP internal/test/VCS sessions); then adversarially verify completeness (vs `git show HEAD:NEWS.md` + the plan‚Äôs classification), accuracy (every named function exists/exported), and dedup/style BEFORE tightening prose. `rmarkdown::render(output_format="github_document")` drops an untracked `NEWS.html` preview ‚Äî clean it before commit. (S130, owner pick B ‚Äî CRAN Phase 3a, the NEWS 2.0.0 rewrite)

**What happened.** The CRAN plan‚Äôs Phase 3 headline was to rewrite the
single verbose, internally-doubled `1.1.0.9000` NEWS section into a
terse `# nprcgenekeepr 2.0.0` Major/Minor entry and fold in the
S112‚ÄìS129 user-facing changes. The plan (line 154) authorized splitting
content-rewrite from version-bump+regenerate; the owner chose the split,
so this session delivered only the NEWS rewrite + re-render (Phase 3a).
Strict TDD: scope gate (full vs split) + the three transitions. RED: a
probe bound to the rendered `NEWS.md` (one 2.0.0 heading; 1.1.0.9000
gone; Major+Minor; no NEW-/PED-/XARCH-; `addGenotype` + rhesus data
folded in; module mechanics dropped) ‚Äî 6 fail ‚Üí 0 after rewrite+render.
A 3-lens adversarial workflow (completeness/accuracy/dedup-style)
returned 0 completeness, 0 accuracy, 0 dedup findings and 6 style nits;
the REFACTOR tighten resolved them with the probe staying 7/7. 0
stakeholder corrections.

**Splice by heading boundary, do not Edit-match hundreds of lines.** The
old section spanned 178 lines. An `Edit` requires the entire
`old_string` to match byte-for-byte ‚Äî fragile and error-prone at that
size. Instead: `Write` the new section to a scratch file, then an R
splice ‚Äî
`x <- readLines("NEWS.Rmd"); v <- grep("^# nprcgenekeepr 1\\.1\\.0\\.9000", x); w <- grep("^# nprcgenekeepr 1\\.0\\.8 ", x); writeLines(c(x[seq_len(v-1)], readLines(newfile), "", x[w:length(x)]), "NEWS.Rmd")`.
Deterministic, keys only on stable heading anchors, and leaves all prior
history untouched. The REFACTOR re-splice keyed on the new `2.0.0`
heading the same way. (This is the document-section cousin of the
dry-run-first R-script edits used for the roxygen sweeps in S114/S116.)

**Fold the user-facing delta from the per-session CHANGELOG, classifying
ship-vs-internal.** The plan‚Äôs ¬ß6.3 classification predated S112‚ÄìS129,
so the recent sessions had to be folded in. The per-session CHANGELOG
entries are the source: the ship/user-facing ones (S123/S124 data-type
re-exports; S125 `addGenotype` behavior) became their own Minor bullets,
and the many shipped help/data-doc corrections
(S112/S114‚ÄìS117/S120‚ÄìS122/S126/S127) collapsed into ONE omnibus
‚ÄúDocumentation‚Äù Minor bullet rather than 12 lines. The non-shipping
sessions ‚Äî S113 (roxygen tooling), S118/S119 (PR/VCS), S128
(test-fixture cleanup), S129 (website-only article) ‚Äî were correctly
DROPPED. Each handoff‚Äôs own `[news-vs-changelog]` note (‚Äúthis ships ‚Üí
fold into NEWS‚Äù vs ‚Äúwebsite-only ‚Üí no NEWS line‚Äù) is the classification
signal; trust it but confirm against what the change actually touches.

**Bind RED to the rendered artifact, and adversarially verify a
user-facing release artifact before polishing.** As with the man-page
doc fixes (Learning 119), the probe binds to the GENERATED `NEWS.md`
(not just `NEWS.Rmd`), so it also proves the re-render ran. For a
CRAN-facing release note, a 3-lens workflow earned its keep:
completeness compared the draft against the OLD content
(`git show HEAD:NEWS.md`), the plan‚Äôs Major/Minor lists, AND the
CHANGELOG delta; accuracy grepped `NAMESPACE`/`R/` to confirm every
named function exists and is exported and cross-checked each behavior
claim; dedup/style checked the house convention. Run the verify BEFORE
the style-tighten so the tighten can‚Äôt silently drop a fact ‚Äî then a
token-presence sweep confirms it did not.

**`github_document` render litters `NEWS.html`.**
`rmarkdown::render(output_format = "github_document")` has
`html_preview = TRUE` by default, which drops an untracked `NEWS.html`
next to `NEWS.md` (the shipped artifact). `NEWS.html` is not gitignored
‚Äî remove it before commit (or render with `html_preview = FALSE`). Same
family as the `pkgdown` render litter (Learning 122) and the stray
`Rplots.pdf`: a build step can emit artifacts the commit must exclude ‚Äî
snapshot `git status` before, clean after.

**Reflexes:** \[to replace a large contiguous doc region, Write the
replacement to a file and splice by stable heading anchors in R ‚Äî never
hand-match hundreds of lines in one Edit\]\[fold a NEWS/changelog delta
from the per-session CHANGELOG, using each handoff‚Äôs news-vs-changelog
note to classify ship-vs-internal; collapse many small shipped doc fixes
into one omnibus bullet\]\[bind the RED probe to the rendered `NEWS.md`
so it also proves the re-render ran\]\[adversarially verify a
user-facing release artifact (completeness vs `git show HEAD:` + plan +
CHANGELOG; accuracy vs `NAMESPACE`/`R/`; dedup/style) BEFORE tightening
prose, then token-sweep to confirm the tighten dropped no
fact\]\[`rmarkdown::render(github_document)` drops an untracked
`NEWS.html` preview ‚Äî clean it before commit\]. **Apply:** any large
documentation-section rewrite, especially a NEWS/release-notes rewrite ‚Äî
splice deterministically, fold the delta from per-session history,
RED-bind to the rendered file, adversarially verify, then tighten and
clean render litter.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Learning 124 ‚Äî For a version bump / metadata change with NO new behavior, do strict TDD by binding RED to the *version-consistency invariant* (every version-bearing artifact == target; stale strings absent), not to a function‚Äôs behavior. Re-render generated docs with `rmarkdown::render(output_format = <fmt>(html_preview = FALSE))` to avoid the HTML-preview litter outright (improves on Learning 123‚Äôs clean-up-after). `getVersion()` tracks DESCRIPTION under `load_all`, so re-rendering README auto-updates its version line; its *date* is `sessioninfo`-derived (provisional, not literal-today). A version *retitle* in one file leaves *dangling cross-references* in other docs ‚Äî sweep for them. (S131, owner pick B-cont ‚Äî CRAN Phase 3b, the 2.0.0 version bump)

**What happened.** CRAN plan Phase 3 was split (Learning 123): S130 did
the NEWS rewrite (Phase 3a), leaving the version bump as Phase 3b. The
deliverable was purely mechanical ‚Äî `DESCRIPTION:4` Version `1.1.0.9000`
‚Üí `2.0.0`, re-render `README.md`, `CITATION.cff` version `1.0.7` (stale)
‚Üí `2.0.0` + a `date-released` field ‚Äî and the two version-dependent
tests (`test_getVersion.R`, `test_appUI_version.R`) are both *dynamic*
(they read
[`packageVersion()`](https://rdrr.io/r/utils/packageDescription.html)),
so the bump does not make any existing test go red-then-green. There was
no new behavior. Strict TDD still applied: two pre-RED scope gates
(version-consistency only; defer the carried NEWS Minor‚ÜíMajor
promotion) + the three transitions, `GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR` declared N/A. Full
suite stayed 191 files / 0-0. 0 stakeholder corrections.

**The RED surface for a no-new-behavior change is an invariant, not a
behavior.** When the deliverable changes configuration/metadata rather
than logic, bind RED to the *property the change must establish*. Here
that property is the version-string inventory being consistent: a
file-based probe parsed `DESCRIPTION`
(`read.dcf(..., fields="Version")`), `CITATION.cff` (the `version:`
line), and `README.md` (the rendered version line) and asserted all
three equal the target and that the stale strings (`1.1.0.9000`,
`1.0.7`) are gone, plus a `date-released` field exists ‚Äî 7 assertions,
all failing on the current tree, all passing after the edits +
re-render. This is the metadata-bump analog of binding RED to a rendered
`NEWS.md` (Learning 123) or a generated `man/.Rd` (Learning 119): the
probe verifies the *deliverable‚Äôs invariant*, and the existing dynamic
tests verify the *runtime path* (the
[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)
About-tab render = Phase 3E). The probe is transient (removed before
commit); no permanent test is added unless the owner asks (offered,
declined).

**`html_preview = FALSE` beats clean-up-after.** Learning 122/123 note
that `rmarkdown::render(github_document)` drops an untracked
`NEWS.html`/preview that must be cleaned. Passing
`output_format = rmarkdown::github_document(html_preview = FALSE)`
suppresses it at the source ‚Äî the render produced *only* the intended
`README.md` change, no litter to remove. Prefer this over
snapshot-and-clean when re-rendering a github_document.

**[`getVersion()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getVersion.md)
tracks DESCRIPTION under `load_all`; the README date is
`sessioninfo`-derived.** `README.Rmd:10` calls
[`nprcgenekeepr::getVersion()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getVersion.md),
and under
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
`packageVersion("nprcgenekeepr")` returns the *dev DESCRIPTION* version
‚Äî so editing DESCRIPTION then re-rendering README auto-updates the
version line; README.Rmd needs NO edit (it is on the ¬ß3.1 ‚ÄúNO edit ‚Äî
auto-tracks‚Äù list, like the `appUI` About panel). But
[`getVersion()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getVersion.md)‚Äôs
date comes from
[`sessioninfo::package_info()`](https://sessioninfo.r-lib.org/reference/package_info.html),
which for a source package is an environment-derived date (here
`2026-06-17`, not the literal today `2026-06-18`) ‚Äî so the README date
can differ from a hand-set `date-released`. Both are provisional and
reconfirmed at the actual submission (Phase 5); do not force them to
match.

**A version retitle leaves dangling pointers.** S130 retitled the NEWS
`1.1.0.9000` section to `2.0.0`; a closing sweep for the old version
string found `ROADMAP.md:6` still says ‚Äúsee `NEWS.md` 1.1.0.9000‚Äù ‚Äî now
a *dangling* cross-reference to a section that no longer exists. Also
`CLAUDE.md:18` carries stale ‚Äú(Version 1.1.0.9000)‚Äù prose and
`nprcgenekeepr_notes.txt:5` a now-resolved TODO. These are
`.Rbuildignore`d dev docs (not CRAN artifacts) and out of a locked
three-file scope ‚Äî flagged for follow-up, not fixed mid-session (FM
\#8). The lesson: after any version *rename* (not just a numeric bump),
grep the whole tree for the old string and triage hits into bumpable /
historical-keep / dangling-pointer.

**Reflexes:** \[for a config/metadata change with no new behavior, bind
RED to the change‚Äôs invariant (version-string inventory consistent;
stale strings absent), not to a function‚Äôs behavior ‚Äî the metadata-bump
cousin of RED-binding to a rendered artifact\]\[verify the plan‚Äôs
version-string inventory (¬ß3.1) against the live tree with a `git grep`
before editing, and split hits into bumpable vs historical-must-not-bump
vs dangling-pointer\]\[re-render a github_document with
`html_preview = FALSE` to avoid the preview litter
outright\]\[[`getVersion()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getVersion.md)/[`packageVersion()`](https://rdrr.io/r/utils/packageDescription.html)
track DESCRIPTION under `load_all`, so re-rendering README auto-updates
its version line ‚Äî README.Rmd needs no edit; its date is
`sessioninfo`-derived and provisional\]\[after a version *retitle*,
sweep the whole tree for the old string ‚Äî a rename leaves dangling
cross-references in dev docs\]. **Apply:** any version bump or
metadata-only change ‚Äî drive RED off the consistency invariant, verify
the inventory against the tree, re-render with `html_preview=FALSE`, and
triage every lingering old-version hit.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Learning 125 ‚Äî When a plan marks a phase ‚Äúopen,‚Äù a prior session may have already executed it ‚Äî verify against process history (`git log` on the phase‚Äôs target files + the CHANGELOG + the handoff chain) BEFORE re-running; a stale status propagates through many handoffs unreconciled. And a string fixed only at its canonical source can survive in its DOWNSTREAM SINKS ‚Äî a generated file, a child-include rendered into multiple places, and a hand-maintained mirror ‚Äî so sweep the whole tree and scope the invariant probe to git-TRACKED files (an unscoped sweep flags untracked render litter as false offenders). (S132, owner pick B-Phase1 re-scoped ‚Äî finish the `mulatto`‚Üí`mulatta` typo across README/vignette/CITATION)

**What happened.** The owner picked ‚ÄúCRAN Phase 1 static hygiene.‚Äù
Orientation‚Äôs process-history check (memory
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\]) found **Phase 1 was
already executed by S102** (commit `a3cf3623`) ‚Äî the `.Rbuildignore`
cruft lines, the DESCRIPTION `mulatto`‚Üí`mulatta` typo, the renv
`Config/*` reordering, `VignetteBuilder: knitr`, the `@return`/`\value`
docs, and the LICENSE-year reconcile ‚Äî all verified in place against the
live tree. Yet the plan (authored S101) was never marked done, and the
handoff chain S102‚Üí‚Ä¶‚ÜíS131 carried ‚ÄúPhase 1 ‚Ä¶ still open‚Äù forward; S131‚Äôs
gotcha even named `DESCRIPTION:23` as still carrying the typo, which
S102 had fixed. A tree-wide `mulatto` sweep then showed S102 fixed the
typo **only in DESCRIPTION**: it survived in `README.Rmd:48` (‚Üí shipped
`README.md`, twice), the shipped vignette child
`vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd:48`, and
`CITATION.cff:16`. Re-scoped with the owner to finishing the typo
tree-wide; strict TDD throughout (3 transition gates + a pre-RED scope
gate; REFACTOR N/A); RED probe 9 failures ‚Üí 0; full suite 0/0; 0
stakeholder corrections.

**A plan‚Äôs phase *status* is a claim, not ground truth ‚Äî verify it
against process history before executing.** SESSION_RUNNER‚Äôs planning
discipline says a plan‚Äôs ‚Äúfiles to change‚Äù come from search results, not
architectural memory; the dual at *execution* time is that a plan‚Äôs
*completion* status comes from the commit record, not the plan‚Äôs own
text. Before re-running any phase a plan calls ‚Äúopen,‚Äù
`git log --oneline -- <the phase's target files>`, `grep` the CHANGELOG,
and scan the handoff chain for evidence it is already done. Here two
commands (`git log -- .Rbuildignore DESCRIPTION` ‚Üí
`a3cf3623 ‚Ä¶ CRAN Phase 1 static hygiene (S102)`, and
`grep -ni 'phase 1' CHANGELOG.md`) proved Phase 1 was complete and saved
the session from re-doing settled work. A stale status propagates:
S101‚Äôs plan said open, S102 did the work without marking the plan done,
and every handoff since copied ‚ÄúPhase 1 open‚Äù forward. The fix is
twofold ‚Äî verify before executing, AND reconcile the artifacts (the
plan + CHANGELOG) when you find the drift, so the next session inherits
truth instead of re-discovering it.

**A fix at the canonical source can survive in its downstream sinks.**
S102 corrected the typo in DESCRIPTION (the canonical Description field)
and reasonably treated it as handled. But the same species name lives in
**five** independent sinks: (1) a hand-maintained *mirror* ‚Äî
`CITATION.cff`‚Äôs abstract duplicates the Description prose (cffr would
regenerate it from DESCRIPTION, but cffr is absent, so it drifted); (2)
a *generated* file ‚Äî `README.md` is knit from `README.Rmd`; (3) a
*child-include* ‚Äî `README.Rmd` states the reference directly (line 48)
AND `child`-includes `_introduction.Rmd` (line 32), which carries the
same block, so the typo renders into README.md *twice*; (4) that same
child ships into the `a3manual.Rmd` vignette; (5) a *website-config
mirror* ‚Äî `_pkgdown.yml`‚Äôs `description:` field also copies the
Description prose, so the typo would appear on the published pkgdown
site. The plan‚Äôs own location list (‚ÄúDESCRIPTION:23 + CITATION.cff:15‚Äù)
missed the README, vignette, AND `_pkgdown.yml` sinks. Lesson: when
fixing a string that appears in user-facing prose, sweep the *whole
tree* for it and trace generation/include/mirror chains ‚Äî fixing the
source is not fixing the output.

**Scope a tree-wide invariant probe to git-tracked files ‚Äî but ALL of
them, not a curated dir list.** Two opposite scoping errors bit this
session and both were caught. (a) *Too broad:* the RED probe‚Äôs first
sweep flagged untracked, git-ignored local render litter
(`vignettes/a3manual.html|md`, stale builds) as offenders; filtering to
`git ls-files` fixed it ‚Äî the invariant is about *committed/shipped*
content, not the working tree. (b) *Too narrow:* I then scoped the sweep
to a hand-picked dir list (`R/`/`man/`/`vignettes/` + `README.md` +
`DESCRIPTION`) ‚Äî which **missed `_pkgdown.yml`**, the fifth sink. The
miss was caught only by a close-out belt-and-suspenders
`git ls-files | grep mulatto` over **all** tracked files. The lesson: a
curated dir list embeds the same blind spot that let the typo survive in
the first place. Sweep **every** tracked file (`git ls-files`), and
*subtract* only the dev-process-history docs that legitimately
*describe* the fix (CHANGELOG, SESSION_NOTES, PROJECT_LEARNINGS,
`docs/planning/*`, dev notes) ‚Äî don‚Äôt *add back* a curated content
subset.

**Reflexes:** \[before re-running any phase a plan calls ‚Äúopen,‚Äù verify
against process history ‚Äî `git log --oneline -- <phase target files>`,
`grep` the CHANGELOG, scan the handoff chain ‚Äî a stale status propagates
through many handoffs unreconciled\]\[when you find plan/CHANGELOG
drift, reconcile the artifacts (mark the phase done; correct the false
status transparently) so the next session inherits truth, don‚Äôt just
route around it\]\[a typo/string fixed at its canonical source can
survive in downstream sinks ‚Äî a generated file, a child-include rendered
into multiple places, a hand-maintained mirror, a website-config copy ‚Äî
sweep the whole tree and trace generation/include/mirror chains\]\[scope
a tree-wide invariant sweep to `git ls-files` (excludes git-ignored
render litter) but over ALL tracked files minus only the fix-describing
dev docs ‚Äî a curated dir list re-creates the original blind spot; always
run a close-out `git ls-files | grep` belt-and-suspenders pass\].
**Apply:** any ‚Äúresume the plan / do Phase N‚Äù pickup (verify N isn‚Äôt
already done before executing) and any single-location content/typo fix
(sweep ALL tracked files, subtract only the fix-describing docs, run the
close-out grep).

#### Learning 126 ‚Äî A plan‚Äôs named root cause is a *hypothesis*: measure before fixing. The documented cause (‚Äútiming‚Äù) may not reproduce in the current tree, and the profile may instead surface a *different* real defect the owner re-scopes to. Verify a handoff‚Äôs environment/feasibility claim firsthand (the ‚ÄúPhase 2 blocked ‚Äî devtools/renv absent‚Äù premise was stale; base-R `R CMD build`/`check --as-cran` need no devtools). Don‚Äôt invent a fix where the measurement shows no problem. And encode a check‚Äôs invariant with the tool‚Äôs OWN mechanism (R‚Äôs parser `SYMBOL_PACKAGE` tokens for `pkg::`), not a fragile text regex. (S133, owner pick B-Phase2 re-scoped ‚Äî CRAN Phase 2 archival timing root cause)

**What happened.** The owner picked ‚ÄúCRAN Phase 2 ‚Äî archival timing root
cause.‚Äù The plan named example/test/vignette elapsed time as the cause
and listed the gene-drop vignettes + LabKey examples as ‚Äúprime
suspects,‚Äù but it also (dragon \#1) said *measure first; let the profile
name the offender*. Profiling ‚Äî an authoritative
`R CMD check --as-cran --timings` ‚Äî showed the timing defect **does not
reproduce** in the 2.0.0 tree: examples 20s, vignettes 16s, slowest
example `countLoops` 1.43s, zero examples ‚â• 5s; the ‚Äúprime suspect‚Äù
gene-drop vignettes rebuild in 16s total (cheap on the tiny `smallPed`).
What the profile *did* expose was a different, genuinely CRAN-blocking
finding: `Status: 1 WARNING` for an undeclared `withr` test dependency.
Per the plan‚Äôs STOP-and-re-scope rule, the owner re-scoped the session
to fixing that (one `Suggests` line, strict TDD); the re-run `--as-cran`
dropped to 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING. 0 stakeholder corrections.

**A phase‚Äôs *premise* is a claim too ‚Äî measure it before executing, just
as you verify a phase‚Äôs *status* (Learning 125).** Learning 125 said a
plan‚Äôs open/done status is a claim to verify against process history.
Its sibling at execution time: a plan‚Äôs named *root cause* is a
hypothesis to verify against a measurement. A plan that says ‚Äúfix
timing‚Äù can be wrong about *whether timing is still broken* ‚Äî the
archived defect was at 1.0.7/1.0.8, and the post-1.0.8 work (or merely
faster hardware here) may have resolved it. The plan anticipated exactly
this (dragon \#1, ‚Äúlet the profile name the offender‚Äù) and gave a
STOP-and-re-scope boundary; honoring it ‚Äî running the gold-standard
measurement and surfacing the changed premise to the owner with a
grounded re-scope question ‚Äî is what kept the session from inventing a
timing ‚Äúfix‚Äù for a non-problem.

**Verify a handoff‚Äôs environment/feasibility claim firsthand before
accepting a ‚Äúblocked.‚Äù** Both the S131 and S132 handoffs said Phase 2
was ‚Äúblocked here ‚Äî needs devtools +
[`renv::restore()`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/reference/restore.html),
absent in this env.‚Äù A 20-line env probe disproved it: R 4.6.0 with the
renv library already materialized, all 18 Imports present, pandoc
present ‚Äî and crucially `R CMD build`/`R CMD check --as-cran` are
*base-R binaries* that need no devtools. The only real gap was a couple
of missing build-only Suggests (`markdown` for a vignette; installed in
seconds). A ‚Äúblocked‚Äù inherited from a handoff is a hypothesis, not a
fact ‚Äî the cost to test it (one probe script) is far below the cost of
deferring a runnable, critical-path phase.

**Don‚Äôt invent a fix where the measurement shows no problem.** The plan
offered timing mechanisms (`\donttest`, `skip_on_cran()`, reduce
iterations). With the profile showing no unit near the limit, applying
them would *solve a non-problem* ‚Äî and reducing simulation iterations
risks dragon \#2 (a ‚Äúspeed-up‚Äù that silently changes GVA/kinship
numbers). The disciplined output when the measurement is clean is to
*say so with evidence and stop*, not to perform the plan‚Äôs listed
actions for their own sake. (The owner was offered a ‚Äú+guard the
heaviest examples as machine-speed insurance‚Äù option and declined it;
honest framing flagged it as belt-and-suspenders that slightly reduces
CRAN example coverage.)

**Encode a check‚Äôs invariant with the tool‚Äôs own mechanism, not a
hand-rolled text scan.** The RED probe had to mirror `R CMD check`‚Äôs
‚Äúunstated dependencies in tests‚Äù check. A first-draft regex over raw
test text false-flagged two non-deps: `devtools` (appears only in `#'`
comments) and `shinytest2.R` (a filename in a comment). `R CMD check`
ignores both because it parses *code*. Rewriting the probe to use
`getParseData(parse(f))` and collect `SYMBOL_PACKAGE` tokens ‚Äî exactly
what the parser resolves the package half of `pkg::` to ‚Äî made it
comment/string/filename-immune and yielded precisely the one real
offender (`withr`), matching the check. When a probe must reproduce a
tool‚Äôs code-level judgment, reach for the tool‚Äôs own parser/AST, not a
regex approximation of it.

**Reflexes:** \[a plan‚Äôs named root cause is a hypothesis ‚Äî run the
gold-standard measurement (here `R CMD check --as-cran --timings`)
BEFORE applying the plan‚Äôs fix; the documented cause may not
reproduce\]\[honor a plan‚Äôs STOP-and-re-scope boundary: when the profile
contradicts the premise, surface it to the owner with evidence + a
grounded re-scope question ‚Äî don‚Äôt barrel into the listed fix or
silently narrow\]\[verify a handoff‚Äôs ‚Äúblocked / tool absent‚Äù claim
firsthand with a quick env probe ‚Äî base-R
`R CMD build`/`check --as-cran` need no devtools; renv may already be
materialized\]\[when the measurement is clean, say so with evidence and
STOP ‚Äî don‚Äôt apply timing fixes to units that aren‚Äôt slow (non-problem +
dragon \#2 risk)\]\[to reproduce a tool‚Äôs code-level judgment in a
probe, use the tool‚Äôs own parser (`getParseData`/`SYMBOL_PACKAGE`), not
a text regex that scans comments/strings\]. **Apply:** any ‚Äúfix \[named
root cause\]‚Äù pickup (measure that the cause reproduces first) and any
probe that must mirror an `R CMD check` finding (parse, don‚Äôt regex).

#### Learning 127 ‚Äî Verify a spell-flagged word before adding it to WORDLIST: a ‚Äúmisspelling‚Äù can be the *symptom of a real defect*, and adding it masks the defect. A dictionary the plan calls ‚Äúnearly clean‚Äù can be far off ‚Äî reconcile against the live tool output, not the plan‚Äôs guess. And `R CMD check --as-cran` is a base-R gate that needs no devtools wrappers; for a *true* gate, install the missing Suggests so nothing is skipped. (S134, owner pick A ‚Äî CRAN Phase 4 full `--as-cran` gate)

**What happened.** Phase 4‚Äôs gate came back clean on the first run ‚Äî
`Status: 2 NOTEs` = 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING (NOTE 1 = expected
archived/new-submission incoming-feasibility; NOTE 2 = local-only
old-HTML-Tidy/no-V8, CRAN-absent), timings all comfortable, regression
read 0 failed / 0 error. The interesting work was the `inst/WORDLIST`
reconciliation the plan listed almost as an afterthought (‚Äúconfirm only
EHR/Raboin/kinships remain after the `mulatta` fix‚Äù). Reality:
[`spelling::spell_check_package()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/spelling//reference/spell_check_package.html)
flagged **37** words ‚Äî the dictionary had drifted ~35 legitimate code
identifiers behind the S100‚ÄìS133 work (`changedCols`, `femaleSires`,
`qcResult`, ‚Ä¶) ‚Äî i.e.¬Ýthe plan‚Äôs stated expectation was wrong by an
order of magnitude. Verifying each flagged word against its source
location (not blindly running `update_wordlist()`) paid off twice: it
isolated a curly-quote tokenizer artifact (`names'` from the quoted
base-R error `'row.names'` in NEWS ‚Äî a faithful quote, not a word) and,
more importantly, surfaced a **real data defect**.

**A spell flag can be the visible symptom of a defect that has nothing
to do with spelling.** The flagged token `si` traced to a column
literally named **`si.re`** in 5 of the 6 example pedigree datasets
(`pedGood`, `pedDuplicateIds`, `pedFemaleSireMaleDam`,
`pedMissingBirth`, `pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam`); the 6th
(`pedInvalidDates`) correctly uses `sire`. `si.re` is almost certainly a
data defect (should be `sire`/`sire_id`, to pair with `ego_id`/`dam_id`)
‚Äî `spell_check` split it on the period and flagged the `si` fragment.
Had I run `update_wordlist()` (which adds *all* flagged words), `si`
would have been enshrined in the dictionary and the defect masked
forever. Instead I added the 35 clean terms and **deliberately left `si`
flagged** so it stays visible, then documented the `si.re` finding for
its own session. Lesson: before adding a flagged word to a project
dictionary, look at where it occurs ‚Äî if it‚Äôs a fragment of a malformed
identifier, the right fix is upstream, not the WORDLIST.

**The owner-approved scope can be refined by what verification reveals ‚Äî
transparently, and more conservatively.** The owner approved ‚Äúadd the 36
legitimate terms‚Äù before I‚Äôd traced `si` to `si.re`. Discovering `si`
was a defect symptom, I narrowed to 35 (excluding both `names'` and
`si`) ‚Äî *more* conservative than approved, serving the same stated
intent (‚Äúdon‚Äôt enshrine artifacts‚Äù), and reported the deviation
explicitly so the owner could redirect. New information that post-dates
an approval should refine the action toward the approval‚Äôs intent, with
the change surfaced ‚Äî not silently, and not by rigidly executing the
now-outdated literal instruction.

**A ‚Äútrue‚Äù `--as-cran` gate installs the Suggests; the forced check is a
weaker proxy.** S133‚Äôs check used `_R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false`
because `covr`/`shinytest2`/`shinyWidgets`/`spelling` were absent (a
real but partial gate). S134 installed all four (+ `urlchecker`) into
the renv library ‚Äî installing the *package* `shinytest2` does **not**
install Chrome (chromote fetches it lazily, and the e2e tests
`skip_on_cran` so the check never launches a browser), so it was safe ‚Äî
and ran with no forcing flag, the condition CRAN itself checks under.
Pre-gate hygiene that the single `R CMD check` subsumes anyway is still
worth running explicitly for the captured evidence: `roxygenise()` (zero
diff ‚Üí docs in sync), `urlchecker::url_check()` (17/17 correct),
`spell_check_package()` (the WORDLIST reconcile). WORDLIST is
*gate-invariant* (consumed only by the `skip_on_cran`
`tests/spelling.R`), so changing it cannot alter the check result ‚Äî
confirmed by a second rebuild+recheck (identical `2 NOTEs`).

**Reflexes:** \[before adding a spell-flagged word to `inst/WORDLIST`,
read its source location ‚Äî a fragment of a malformed identifier (`si` ‚Üê
`si.re`) is a defect symptom; `update_wordlist()` would mask it, so add
the clean terms by hand and leave the symptom flagged\]\[treat a plan‚Äôs
‚Äúthe dictionary should be nearly clean‚Äù as a guess ‚Äî reconcile against
the live `spell_check_package()` output; drift accumulates silently as
code adds identifiers\]\[installing the R package `shinytest2` does not
install Chrome ‚Äî safe to add for a true `--as-cran` gate when the e2e
tests `skip_on_cran`; install the missing Suggests rather than
`_R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false`\]\[WORDLIST is gate-invariant (only the
`skip_on_cran` spell test reads it) ‚Äî don‚Äôt re-run the full check ‚Äúto be
safe‚Äù on a WORDLIST-only change unless the session is itself the
submission gate\]\[the HTML-manual NOTE (`'tidy' not recent enough` /
`package 'V8' unavailable`) is a local-toolchain artifact, CRAN-absent ‚Äî
document it, don‚Äôt chase it\]. **Apply:** any WORDLIST/spell
reconciliation (verify each word‚Äôs source before adding) and any local
`--as-cran` gate (install Suggests for a true gate; know which NOTEs are
CRAN-absent).

#### Learning 128 ‚Äî A guessed future identifier (a PR number, a branch name, a ‚Äúnext‚Äù version) is a claim that propagates through handoffs unverified ‚Äî confirm branch/PR/remote state firsthand (`gh pr view N`, `gh pr list --state all`, `git show origin/<branch>:DESCRIPTION`, `git merge-base --is-ancestor`) before citing it in a runbook or handoff. And adversarially verify an external-facing submission document (a CRAN cover note) with a skeptical fresh-eyes pass ‚Äî the author, close to the work, misses implied-passes framing, ‚Äúdelete-before-pasting‚Äù foot-guns, and over-scoped quantitative claims. (S135, owner pick B-Phase5 scope A ‚Äî CRAN Phase 5 cover note + runbook)

**What happened.** Phase 5‚Äôs local half (rewrite `cran-comments.md`;
write the win-builder/R-hub runbook) was authored from verified repo
facts, then run through a 3-lens adversarial verification (repo
fact-check + runbook-command/API + skeptical-CRAN-reviewer). Every
*factual* claim in the cover note confirmed against the tree, but the
pass caught three classes of author-blind defect ‚Äî and one was a
long-propagated false premise.

**A guessed identifier rides the handoff chain as if it were a fact.**
Every handoff since ~S112 said the branch ‚Äúrides a future **PR \#53**
(Learning 112).‚Äù It does not: `gh pr view 53` ‚Üí ‚ÄúCould not resolve to a
PullRequest,‚Äù because GitHub shares one number space between issues and
PRs and issues \#45/#46 consumed that range ‚Äî the next PR will be some
later number, not 53. Worse, `gh pr list --state all` showed **PR \#52
is already merged** but carried only S101-S117, and
`git show origin/master:DESCRIPTION` is still `Version: 1.1.0.9000` with
`git merge-base --is-ancestor e24a53a2 origin/master` = NO ‚Äî so `master`
has **none** of the 2.0.0 commits. The runbook‚Äôs first draft repeated
the ‚ÄúPR \#53 / merge to master‚Äù framing and miscast the branch risk as
‚Äúan older tree, not 2.0.0‚Äù when `origin/add-methodology` is in fact
already 2.0.0 but missing S133‚Äôs `withr` fix (so R-hub on the unpushed
tree would re-report the WARNING win-builder won‚Äôt). Lesson: a future PR
number, a ‚Äúrides PR \#N‚Äù note, or ‚Äúmerge to master‚Äù is a *prediction*;
verify it against `gh`/`git` the moment you depend on it, and never let
a guessed number sit in a handoff as settled fact (Learning 125/126 ‚Äî
verify inherited status/premises ‚Äî applied to repository plumbing).

**Adversarially verify the external-facing artifact, not just the
code.** A CRAN cover note is read by a skeptical human reviewer; its
failure modes are rhetorical, not compilable, so the build-equivalent
(it‚Äôs valid markdown) tells you nothing. The reviewer-lens agent caught
what I, close to the draft, did not: (1) listing win-builder/R-hub under
‚Äú## Test environments‚Äù with no results reads as *implied passes* for a
twice-archived package ‚Äî fixed by explicit ‚Äúto be run before submission‚Äù
markers; (2) a ‚ÄúNOTE TO MAINTAINER (delete before pasting)‚Äù block with
placeholder result numbers *inside the file pasted to CRAN* is a
foot-gun (if left in, you ship fake numbers + an internal path) ‚Äî moved
entirely into the runbook so the cover note is CRAN-facing-only; (3)
‚Äú3-5x headroom‚Äù is true per-example (~1.4s vs ~5s) but false per-phase
(the tests phase ~43s is ~1.4x under a minute) ‚Äî scoped the claim; (4)
wording that implies a deliberate timing *fix* when the measured truth
is ‚Äúthe cause does not reproduce‚Äù overclaims for a timing-archived
package ‚Äî reworded to state what‚Äôs true and point at win-builder/R-hub
(independent, slower hardware) as the confirming evidence. None were
factual errors; all were framing/honesty risks a fresh adversarial
reader surfaces and the author misses.

**Reflexes:** \[before citing a PR number / ‚Äúmerge to master‚Äù / ‚Äúrides
PR \#N‚Äù in a runbook or handoff, run `gh pr view N` and
`gh pr list --state all`, and check the target branch‚Äôs real version
with `git show origin/<branch>:DESCRIPTION` +
`git merge-base --is-ancestor <commit> origin/<branch>`\]\[a guessed
future PR number is unreliable ‚Äî GitHub shares the issue/PR number
space, so intervening issues shift it; don‚Äôt enshrine a specific number
in a handoff\]\[R-hub v2 checks the pushed GitHub tree, win-builder
uploads the local tarball ‚Äî if the local branch is ahead, push before
`rhub_check()` or R-hub re-reports already-fixed problems\]\[keep a CRAN
cover note CRAN-facing-only: no ‚Äúdelete-before-pasting‚Äù blocks, no
placeholder numbers, no internal paths ‚Äî stage those in the
runbook\]\[never list a test platform under ‚ÄúTest environments‚Äù without
a result or an explicit ‚Äúpending‚Äù marker ‚Äî for an archived package it
reads as an implied pass\]\[scope a quantitative headroom/limit claim to
the unit it actually holds for (per-example vs per-phase); don‚Äôt imply a
deliberate fix the measurement shows was never needed\]. **Apply:** any
runbook/handoff referencing branch/PR/remote state (verify firsthand),
and any external-facing submission/correspondence document (run a
skeptical fresh-eyes adversarial pass before it ships).

#### Learning 129 ‚Äî A firsthand-verified remote-state claim still has a shelf life: a branch can be pushed or changed between sessions, so a prior session‚Äôs *correct* ‚Äúpush first / N commits behind‚Äù snapshot can be stale by the time you act on it. `git status` ‚Äúup to date with origin/‚Äù compares only against the LOCAL tracking ref, which updates **only on fetch** ‚Äî so it reads ‚Äúup to date‚Äù both when you already pushed and when your view of the remote is merely stale. Run `git fetch` THEN `git rev-list --left-right --count origin/<branch>...HEAD` to get ground truth before acting on an inherited ‚Äúpush first‚Äù prerequisite. (S136, owner pick Phase5-finish scope ‚Äúprep + hand off‚Äù ‚Äî CRAN Phase 5 local prep)

**What happened.** S135‚Äôs handoff and the runbook both said
`origin/add-methodology` was ‚Äú2 commits behind ‚Äî `git push` before
`rhub_check()` or R-hub re-reports the already-fixed `withr` WARNING.‚Äù
That was *true when S135 verified it* (Learning 128 was about getting
exactly this right). But between S135 and S136 the branch was pushed, so
by the time S136 ran the prerequisite was **already satisfied** ‚Äî
`git fetch` +
`git rev-list --left-right --count origin/add-methodology...HEAD` ‚Üí
`0 / 0`, fully in sync at HEAD `24175785`. The session‚Äôs job flipped
from ‚Äúpush‚Äù to ‚Äúconfirm and supersede the caveat.‚Äù The trap that nearly
hid this: the orientation `git status` said ‚ÄúYour branch is up to date
with ‚Äòorigin/add-methodology‚Äô‚Äù, which I could not safely read as
‚Äúalready pushed‚Äù ‚Äî it equally means ‚Äúyour tracking ref hasn‚Äôt been
fetched.‚Äù Only the explicit `fetch` + `rev-list` disambiguated. This is
Learning 128‚Äôs sibling pointed at *time* rather than *guessing*: 128
says verify an inherited/guessed identifier; 129 says even a
**correctly-verified** remote snapshot expires ‚Äî re-fetch at the moment
you depend on it, and when you retire an inherited caveat, mark it
**superseded** in the doc (with the command + result that retired it) so
the next reader doesn‚Äôt re-run the obsolete step.

**Reflexes:** \[before acting on any inherited ‚Äúpush first / N commits
behind / unpushed commits‚Äù prerequisite, run `git fetch` THEN
`git rev-list --left-right --count origin/<branch>...HEAD` ‚Äî a remote
can change between sessions, and `git status` ‚Äúup to date‚Äù reflects only
the last-fetched tracking ref\]\[distinguish ‚ÄúI already pushed‚Äù from ‚Äúmy
local view of the remote is stale‚Äù ‚Äî both surface as ‚Äúup to date‚Äù; the
fetch is what disambiguates\]\[when superseding a prior caveat in a
runbook/plan, write ‚Äúsuperseded by on ‚Äù with the exact command + result,
not just a silent deletion, so the supersession is auditable\]\[a
documentation/prep deliverable‚Äôs build-equivalent is still real:
`R CMD build .` to confirm the artifact assembles + re-confirm every
external-doc fact firsthand against the current tree, even when ‚Äúnothing
changed since the gate‚Äù ‚Äî verify the *unchanged* claim too
(`git diff --name-only <gate>..HEAD`)\]. **Apply:** any session that
inherits a branch/remote/push prerequisite, or that hands off a ‚Äúdo X to
the remote first‚Äù instruction to a later session.

#### Learning 130 ‚Äî An adversarial verify pass can itself be wrong ‚Äî cross-check ITS corrections firsthand, not just the synthesis it attacks; and when the evidence genuinely splits, FRAME the decision for the owner instead of manufacturing one ‚Äúcorrect‚Äù answer. A fan-out‚Äôs skeptic struck the synthesis‚Äôs recommended target name and argued for a different one, citing the package‚Äôs own `@example` as its evidence ‚Äî but reading that example firsthand showed it used the *opposite* form, undercutting the skeptic‚Äôs own claim. Both the proposer and its adversary were partly right; only the firsthand file read settled the ground truth. Also re-derive inherited magnitudes: the carried ‚Äú`si.re` in 5 of 6 pedigrees‚Äù note undercounted ‚Äî 6 datasets are malformed (a second, space-form variant was missed). (S137, owner pick F ‚Äî investigate + file the `si.re` example-data defect as GitHub issue \#53)

**What happened.** Auditing the `si.re` malformed-sire-column defect for
a GitHub issue, I ran a 4-reader ‚Üí synthesize ‚Üí adversarial-verify
workflow. The synthesis recommended renaming the malformed column to
**`sire_id`** (symmetric with the sibling `ego_id`/`dam_id`). The
adversarial pass *correctly* caught that the synthesis had overstated
its case (its headline ‚Äúthe period header contradicts the package‚Äôs
value-level period-rejection rule‚Äù was false ‚Äî headers and data values
are separate code paths in `qcStudbook`), and it argued the
convention-consistent target was instead the **space form `si re`**,
citing `R/fixColumnNames.R`‚Äôs documented `@example` as evidence the
package‚Äôs own sire exemplar uses a space. I did **not** take that at
face value: reading `R/fixColumnNames.R:16` firsthand, the `@example`
actually uses **`Sire_ID`** (underscore) ‚Äî the opposite of what the
skeptic claimed, and evidence *for* the synthesis‚Äôs underscore idea, not
the skeptic‚Äôs space idea. The real ground truth (from reading the files,
not either agent‚Äôs summary): the repo ships **three** messy-sire
conventions ‚Äî `si re` (space, in `createPedOne` + ~8 local fixtures),
`Sire_ID` (underscore, the `@example`), and `si.re` (period, the five
`.RData` files) ‚Äî and the period form matches none. So neither the
proposer‚Äôs nor the adversary‚Äôs single ‚Äúcorrect name‚Äù was right; the
honest deliverable was to document the defect and **present the
target-name choice (`si re` / `sire_id` / `sire`) with trade-offs as a
maintainer decision** in the issue. Separately, the inherited handoff‚Äôs
‚Äú5 of 6‚Äù framing undercounted: a firsthand
[`names()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/names.html) scan of every dataset
found `pedOne` *also* carries a malformed `si re` (space) sire column,
making it 6 ‚Äî and
[`fixColumnNames()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/fixColumnNames.md)
empirically normalizes all forms to canonical `sire`, so the defect is
internal inconsistency, not a functional break (confirmed not a CRAN
blocker). Filed as issue \#53 with `bug` + `low priority`.

**Reflexes:** \[when an adversarial/verification agent CORRECTS a
synthesis, re-verify the correction‚Äôs *cited evidence* firsthand before
adopting it ‚Äî a skeptic can be confidently wrong, and ‚Äúthe verifier said
so‚Äù is not ground truth\]\[when two passes disagree on a decision and
both have partial evidence, read the primary source yourself; if the
evidence genuinely splits, the deliverable is to frame the choice with
trade-offs for the owner, not to assert a false single answer (FM \#23 ‚Äî
a contested call is the owner‚Äôs)\]\[re-derive any inherited
count/magnitude (‚ÄúN of M‚Äù, ‚Äú5 files‚Äù, ‚Äú~35 words‚Äù) firsthand ‚Äî a carried
characterization drifts in magnitude, not just in truth value\]\[for a
shipped-DATA defect, separate ‚Äúfunctionally broken‚Äù (does
ingestion/mapping still produce the canonical schema?) from ‚Äúinternally
inconsistent / typo-looking‚Äù ‚Äî run the normalizer (`fixColumnNames`) on
each variant to prove which it is before calling it a blocker\]\[an
audit/issue deliverable‚Äôs ‚Äúbuild-equivalent‚Äù is firsthand verification
of every <file:line> claim it makes + running the relevant code path
(here
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html) +
`fixColumnNames`), not just assembling prose\]. **Apply:** any session
that runs a verify/adversarial pass over its own findings (standing
practice under ultracode), any issue/audit that must recommend a
contested decision, and any fix scoping that inherits a count or ‚Äúit‚Äôs
broken‚Äù claim.

#### Learning 131 ‚Äî A data/fixture change can break a test that asserts on the SHAPE of derived output (a count, a row total, a newline tally) without that test ever referencing the changed value literally ‚Äî so a grep-for-the-changed-token pre-flight scan will miss it; the full test suite is the only reliable breakage check. (S138, owner pick ‚Äî the real fix for issue \#53: rename the example datasets‚Äô sire column to `sire.id`)

**What happened.** Before regenerating the 6 example pedigrees
(`si.re`/`si re` ‚Üí `sire.id`), I ran a breakage scan: grep `tests/` for
the literal `si.re`/`si re` and for change-log bucket names. It reported
‚Äúno test breaks‚Äù ‚Äî every `si re` in tests was a self-contained local
fixture, and no test referenced the shipped data‚Äôs sire column by its
literal name. That was *true* yet incomplete: the full-suite VERIFY
surfaced one real failure in `test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R`, which
counts newlines in a qcStudbook summary
(`stri_count_regex(...$txt, "\\n") == 9L`). Renaming the column to
`sire.id` makes normalization fire one EXTRA step (`sireIdToSire`,
because `sire.id`‚Üí`sireid`‚Üí`sire`, whereas `si re`‚Üí`sire` was already
canonical), so the summary gained a line and the count became 10. The
test never mentions `si.re`/`si re`/`sire.id` ‚Äî it asserts on the
*shape* of output derived from the data ‚Äî so no token grep could have
found it. Updated `9L`‚Üí`10L` after confirming the new output is correct
(it reports one more real normalization step). The fix was otherwise
clean (full suite 0/0, `R CMD check` Status: OK).

**Reflexes:** \[a fixture/data change‚Äôs breakage check is the FULL test
suite, not a grep for the changed token ‚Äî tests asserting on counts, row
totals, lengths, or formatted-text shape break invisibly to grep\]\[when
changing example data that flows through a reporting/summary/format
function, expect output-shape assertions (newline counts, ‚ÄúN rows‚Äù,
`nrow(...)`) to shift; re-run them\]\[pin the data CONTRACT in a RED
test (exact column names) rather than trusting a pre-flight grep to
predict breakage ‚Äî the contract test is deterministic, the grep is
heuristic\]\[when an output-shape count changes, verify the NEW value is
correct before updating the literal ‚Äî don‚Äôt just bump the number to make
it pass\]. **Apply:** any session that regenerates shipped/example data
or fixtures consumed by reporting/summary/format functions.

#### Learning 132 ‚Äî A spell/lint flag on a GENERATED file can be a render-time artifact, not a source defect: pandoc‚Äôs smart-quotes turn straight `'...'` in an `.Rmd` into curly `'...'` in the `.md`, and a quote sitting against a word (`'row.names'` ‚Üí `names'`) becomes a bogus token. Fix it at the SOURCE in a way the renderer can‚Äôt undo ‚Äî a code span (backticks) bypasses smart-quotes ‚Äî then RE-RENDER and confirm the regenerated-file diff is confined to the one intended line. Never hand-edit the generated `.md`. (S139, owner directive ‚Äî clear S138‚Äôs punch-list)

**What happened.** S138 flagged a lone `names'` spell hit at
`NEWS.md:51` (‚Äúfix the quote, don‚Äôt whitelist‚Äù). The obvious read ‚Äî
‚Äúthere‚Äôs a curly quote to straighten in the source‚Äù ‚Äî was wrong:
`NEWS.Rmd:53` *already* used straight ASCII quotes
(`"duplicate 'row.names' are not allowed"`). The curl is introduced by
pandoc‚Äôs `smart` extension at render time (github_document); because the
closing `'` sits directly against `names`, the tokenizer emits the bogus
`names'`. So there was nothing to ‚Äústraighten‚Äù at the source. Two
tempting wrong fixes: editing `NEWS.md` directly (it‚Äôs generated from
`NEWS.Rmd` ‚Äî would be overwritten, plan gotcha \#3), or disabling
`smart` globally (would curl-strip every other quote in the 500-line
file ‚Äî a huge unwanted diff). The right fix wraps the literal base-R
message in backticks in `NEWS.Rmd`
(`` `duplicate 'row.names' are not allowed` ``): code spans are exempt
from smart-quotes, the straight quotes survive, and it reads better (a
verbatim error message *is* code). Re-rendered `NEWS.md`
(github_document, `html_preview=FALSE`) ‚Üí exactly a one-line diff;
`spell_check_package()` 1 flag ‚Üí 0. Same discipline on the README
de-dup: edited the `.Rmd` source (removed README.Rmd‚Äôs own hardcoded
citation block, leaving the copy that renders from the shared
`_introduction.Rmd` child so the manual/tutorial vignettes were
untouched), re-rendered via `build_readme()`, and read the `git diff` to
confirm it was confined to the removed block (+ a correct incidental
[`getVersion()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getVersion.md)
date refresh) before committing.

**Reflexes:** \[a spell/URL/lint flag on a generated file (`NEWS.md`,
`README.md`, `man/*.Rd`) ‚Äî trace it to the SOURCE (`.Rmd`/roxygen) and
fix there, then re-render; never hand-edit the generated file (it‚Äôs
overwritten)\]\[a `name'`-style bogus token usually comes from pandoc
smart-quotes curling a straight quote that sits against a word ‚Äî the
source may already be ‚Äúcorrect‚Äù; wrap the literal in a code span
(backticks) so the renderer leaves it alone, rather than disabling
`smart` globally (which rewrites every quote in the file)\]\[after
editing a generated file‚Äôs source, RE-RENDER and `git diff` the
generated file ‚Äî confirm the diff is confined to the intended change; an
incidental-but-correct refresh (a
[`getVersion()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getVersion.md)
date) is fine, a storm of formatting changes means the committed file
had drifted from its source ‚Äî investigate before committing\]\[when a
duplicate renders from a shared child doc + a local block, remove the
LOCAL block to keep blast radius to the one file ‚Äî deleting the shared
child changes every consumer (here `a3manual.Rmd` +
`ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd`)\]\[re-run the checker after the fix
(`spell_check_package()` ‚Üí 0) ‚Äî don‚Äôt assume the edit worked\].
**Apply:** any fix to a flag/warning on a knitr/pandoc/roxygen-generated
artifact, and any edit that requires re-rendering `NEWS.md` /
`README.md`.

#### Learning 133 ‚Äî Before merging a PR, triage its CI checks: a red check is not automatically a merge-blocker. `gh pr view --json mergeStateStatus` distinguishes **BLOCKED** (a *required* check is failing ‚Äî do not merge) from **UNSTABLE** (mergeable; only non-required checks are red). When a check is red, read its log and CLASSIFY it ‚Äî required vs optional, correctness vs style, introduced-by-this-PR vs pre-existing ‚Äî and report that classification; don‚Äôt merge blind, and don‚Äôt refuse a merge the owner directed just because a non-required style check is red. (S140, owner directive ‚Äúmerge \#54‚Äù)

**What happened.** PR \#54 (the 2.0.0 merge) came back
`mergeable: MERGEABLE` but `mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE`.
`gh pr checks 54` showed every R-CMD-check (macOS / Windows / Ubuntu
devel+oldrel+release), test-coverage, codecov, and pkgdown **PASS** ‚Äî
and exactly one red: `lint`. Reading the failed job log, the lint
failure was ~30 `lintr` STYLE warnings (`return_linter` ‚Äúexplicit
return() not needed‚Äù, `seq_linter` ‚Äúuse seq_along‚Äù, `coalesce_linter`
‚Äúuse %\|\|%‚Äù, `nonportable_path_linter`, `boolean_arithmetic_linter`) in
long-standing core files (`qcStudbook.R`, `geneDrop.R`,
`removeDuplicates.R`, `makeFounderStatsTable.R`, the `mod*.R` modules,
‚Ä¶) plus a CI config wart (`cyclocomp` package not installed ‚Üí exit 31).
None were correctness issues, none were introduced by S139 (docs only ‚Äî
zero R code), and `lint` is **non-required** (hence UNSTABLE not BLOCKED
‚Äî GitHub allowed the merge). So the disciplined output was to **surface
the finding** (‚Äúthe only red check is non-required pre-existing style
lint; all correctness checks pass‚Äù) and **proceed with the
owner-directed merge** ‚Äî neither merging silently over a red check nor
