(4) **Extending a bundled data object is low-friction but has a fixed checklist.** Adding `minMaleBreedingAge`/`minFemaleBreedingAge` to `speciesGestation` keeps `getSpeciesGestation` + `test_getSpeciesGestation.R` green (they use `%in%`/`$gestation`, not `setequal`/positional), but you MUST: edit `data-raw/speciesGestation.R` AND re-run it (`Rscript data-raw/speciesGestation.R` rewrites `data/*.RData` ‚Äì the source edit alone does nothing), update the `R/data.R` `\describe{}` block, and `devtools::document()` (regenerates `man/*.Rd`) ‚Äì skip any and `devtools::check` throws a code/doc-mismatch NOTE and the 0/0/0 gate fails. `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge` mirrors `getSpeciesGestation`‚Äôs lookup STRUCTURE but not its default (2 years, not 210 days), and matches sex case/whitespace-insensitively with a non-M/F -\> default branch. (5) **Lint: verify with the package namespace LOADED, because the CI mode (`local::.` install) resolves `object_usage_linter` cross-file false positives that a source-only `lintr::lint()`/`lint_package()` over-reports.** `.lintr` enforces `line_length_linter(80)` + (via the ‚Äústyle‚Äù tag) `implicit_integer_linter`, and `lint.yaml` sets `LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT: true` (ANY lint fails CI; `tests/` is excluded). A bare `lintr::lint_package()` flagged my new cross-file calls to `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`/`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` as ‚Äúno visible global function‚Äù ‚Äì but so was a PRE-EXISTING call in `fillGroupMembers.R:40`, and master‚Äôs lint CI is green, proving CI resolves them; running `pkgload::load_all(".")` THEN `lintr::lint_package()` reproduces CI exactly (the object_usage warnings vanish, leaving only the real `line_length`/`implicit_integer` style lints to fix). Use `min(x, 1.0)` / `x * 0.5` (explicit doubles), not `min(x, 1)` / `x / 2`. Strict TDD throughout (RED 3 units: the accessor, the cohort+orchestrator helpers, the qcPed integration; GREEN minimum; REFACTOR = lint only). Build-equivalent `devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` = 0/0/0. (S178, issue \#9 Slice 2 ‚Äì one-unknown peer-cohort mean-kinship correction)

#### Learning 168 ‚Äì Implementing issue \#9 Slice 3 proved that a ‚Äúcharter‚Äù (the ¬ß8-F expanded-slice description) is NOT a ‚Äúspec‚Äù (the ¬ß8-E authoritative formula Slice 2 had), and that REAL-DATA grounding BEFORE RED is what surfaces the decisions a charter leaves implicit. Five load-bearing findings. **(1) A charter-level slice carries unratified decisions that must be surfaced as owner `AskUserQuestion`s before RED.** ¬ß8-F bundled four concerns and the load-bearing one (‚Äúaddress the `gu` inflation that pins founders at the top‚Äù) was ratified IN-SCOPE (D6) but had NO algorithm. Grounding split it into two readings: **Reading B** (rank AROUND gu ‚Äì make the displayed rank classification-aware so flagged founders sink, gu untouched: implementable now, preserves the documented ‚Äúa living founder still contributes‚Äù stance at `calcGU.R:10-34` and all `test_calcGU`/`test_calcA` golden invariants, faithful to the already-ratified D7) vs **Reading A** (recompute/down-weight founder gu inside `calcGU`/`calcA`/gene-drop: reverses that documented stance, breaks the golden invariants, and is NOT RED-testable until an owner-ratified algorithm + replacement gu values exist ‚Äì the Slice-3 analog of Slice 2‚Äôs ¬ß8-E gate). Owner picked Reading B. WHEN TO APPLY: when a slice‚Äôs charter says ‚Äúaddress X‚Äù with no formula, enumerate the implementable-now interpretation vs the needs-ratification interpretation and let the owner choose ‚Äì do NOT silently pick. **(2) Simulate the proposed fix on REAL data before RED ‚Äì a charter assumption can make the fix a no-op or a 44%-of-colony change.** ¬ß8-F framed the both-unknown founders as ‚Äúmissing-data stubs,‚Äù but on `qcPed` ALL 124 both-unknown founders HAVE offspring (they are the colony‚Äôs real founding breeders, e.g.¬Ý`GHH9LB` gu=50, 1 offspring), and the EXISTING `noParentage` bucket requires `totalOffspring==0` ‚Äì so preserving that gate flags ZERO of them (the visible fix is a NO-OP), while dropping it demotes all 124 (44% of the 280-animal colony). A `readRDS(gv5000.rds)` + simulated demote (both gate variants) revealed this; it was a genuine genetics-policy fork (demote-all-keep-imports vs flag-only) the plan never anticipated -\> second owner `AskUserQuestion`; owner picked demote-all. **(3) The U-prefix unknown-id predicate OVER-matches by construction yet is CORRECT on qcPed ‚Äì verify the data, do not assume a bug.** `isGeneratedUnknownId` = `startsWith(id, getAutoIdPrefix("U%04d"))` = `startsWith(id,"U")`, so it matches ANY id starting ‚ÄúU‚Äù, not just generated ‚ÄúU####‚Äù. `qcPed` has ZERO ‚ÄúU####‚Äù ids; its 43 ‚ÄúU‚Ä¶‚Äù parents looked like real ids that would be misclassified ‚Äì but a 20-line probe showed all 43 are genuine unknown stubs (both parents NA, NO birth date, gen 0; `examplePedigree` carries 1372 ‚ÄúU‚Äù ids -\> ‚ÄúU‚Äù IS the ONPRC unknown convention). So the predicate is right, Slice 2‚Äôs correction of the 43 one-unknown animals is sound, and the counts hold (124 both-unknown / 43 one-unknown / 113 known). A cross-cutting ‚Äúbug‚Äù dissolved under a firsthand data check. **(4) Clean classify-\>reconcile architecture: classify ONCE, mark via `value=="Undetermined"`, demote in path B ‚Äì one source of truth across BOTH rank paths.** New internal `classifyParentage(sire, dam)` (U-id aware; built with `out <- rep("known", n); out[anyU] <- "one unknown parent"; out[bothU] <- "both unknown"` ‚Äì NOT `ifelse()`, which returns `logical(0)` on empty input) `cbind`s a `parentage` column into `reportGV`‚Äôs `finalData` (auto-propagates to the table + both CSVs, Learning 165). `orderReport`, made robust to an ABSENT `origin` column (treat as all-NA so the bucket fires on real data) and keyed on `rpt$parentage` (fallback `getFounders`) with the `totalOffspring==0` gate DROPPED, marks no-origin both-unknown founders as `noParentage`-\>‚ÄúUndetermined‚Äù; genuine imports (origin present) stay in the kept/ranked bucket. Path B (`modGeneticValue.R:204-206`) ranks `value=="Undetermined"` LAST via `report[order(demote, report$rank), ]` then reseq `1..n` ‚Äì preserving EVERY row and the sequential rank, so the existing topN-slice and ‚Äúrank is sequential‚Äù tests stay green; `gu` and its 5-caller blast radius untouched. **(5) Phase-3E without a browser: drive the REAL module server on the shipped data via `testServer`.** A `runModularApp` browser smoke is not feasible headlessly (the `test-e2e-`/shinytest2 harness is baseline noise), but `testServer(modGeneticValueServer, args=list(pedigree=reactive(qcPed)), {...})` exercises the actual `gvResults()` reactive end-to-end on REAL shipped data ‚Äì confirmed the displayed top-20 became 18 known + 2 one-unknown (0 both-unknown), all 45 LIVING both-unknown founders demoted to ranks 45-89 (‚ÄúUndetermined‚Äù) ‚Äì plus `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` builds. The DT table auto-renders the new column (no column spec). D8: replaced `test_orderReport`‚Äôs brittle `countUnk(top100)==34`/`==21` golden counts (the `==21` line even measured the UN-ordered input, never `orderReport`) with named-animal behavior assertions on a deterministic `origin`-bearing fixture. Strict TDD throughout (RED 4 specs failing for the right reason; GREEN minimum; REFACTOR = lint only); full clean regression 0 failed / 0 error; build-equivalent `devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` = 0/0/0. (S180, issue \#9 Slice 3 ‚Äì classify unknown-parent animals + classification-aware displayed rank)

#### Learning 169 ‚Äì Publishing a slice that RESOLVES an issue‚Äôs surface complaint but deliberately DEFERRED a deeper sub-scope: close the original issue AND file the deferred scope as a NEW issue in the SAME publish session ‚Äì do not keep the original open as a vague catch-all, and do not silently drop the deferred work. Issue \#9‚Äôs displayed-ranking complaint was met by Slices 2+3 (Reading B ‚Äì rank AROUND the genome-uniqueness statistic), while Reading A (genuine `gu` de-inflation inside `calcGU`/`calcA`/gene-drop, which reverses the documented `calcGU.R:10-34` stance and breaks the golden invariants, so it needs its own ¬ß8-E-style ratification) was owner-deferred in S180. At publish the close-vs-keep-open is a genuine OWNER decision ‚Äì it changes the PR body‚Äôs `Closes #N` vs `Relates to #N` AND the tracker‚Äôs shape ‚Äì so surface it via `AskUserQuestion` with the reasoning, NOT a silent pick (owner chose `Closes #9`). Then HONOR the close by spinning the deferred scope into a fresh, self-contained issue (#76) that states its own acceptance criteria (ratification-first), names the plan section (¬ß8-A/¬ß8-F, D6) and the code stance it would reverse (`calcGU.R:10-34`), and links the shipped display-side fix (`classifyParentage`/`orderReport`/`modGeneticValue`) ‚Äì so the backlog reflects exactly what remains, the closed issue is honestly closed (its complaint is met), and the deep-genetics work is grab-able cold by a future session. Complements \[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\] (completed work -\> CHANGELOG; the tracker holds OPEN items): a closed-with-explicit-follow-up beats a perpetually-open umbrella issue. The `Closes #N` keyword auto-closes on merge ‚Äì verified firsthand (#9 `closedAt` was one second after the merge commit `5ada4b71`); always re-verify the issue‚Äôs resulting state after the merge rather than trusting the keyword. Publish mechanics otherwise unchanged from the documented convention: push master first per the local-ahead Gotcha (FF-verify with `merge-base --is-ancestor` first), fold NEWS into the SAME PR (157a), do NOT merge blind (fresh non-watch `gh pr checks` re-query, 157), `AskUserQuestion`-gate the irreversible merge with a fresh state re-check (`headRefOid`==local, `origin/master`==expected, OPEN/MERGEABLE/CLEAN), ancestor-gated `reset --hard` + verified-merged-before-delete (146). One verification reflex worth keeping: a `skip_on_cran()`-gated `testServer` test reports as a silent ‚ÄúS‚Äù skip under a default local run ‚Äì re-run the Phase-3E test file with `NOT_CRAN=true` so the demotion assertion actually executes rather than accepting the skip as a pass. (S181, publish issue \#9 Slice 3 + file Reading A follow-up \#76)

#### Learning 170 ‚Äì Issue \#73 Part 1 (generalize the `speciesGestation` lookup from rhesus-only to all 14 common colony NHP species) was a bundled-data + accessor change that combined a TYPE-SYSTEM change with an owner/veterinary methodology component; five reflexes. **(1) Fractional biological values force a column TYPE change that cascades completely through the accessor AND its type-strict tests.** The owner-supplied minimum breeding ages include 2.5 / 3.5 / 1.5 / 1.0, so `minMaleBreedingAge`/`minFemaleBreedingAge` moved integer -\> NUMERIC. The cascade is mechanical but total: `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge()` `as.integer()` -\> `as.numeric()` for BOTH age columns AND the `default` AND the empty-input return `integer(0L)` -\> `numeric(0L)`; the function default literal `2L` -\> `2.0`; and EVERY `expect_identical(..., 4L)` in the test became `..., 4` (expect_identical is type-strict, so a numeric return against an integer literal FAILS ‚Äì even the unchanged-VALUE fallback tests like `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge("UNICORN","F")` flip from `2L` to `2`). The gestation column stayed integer (no fractional gestation) so `getSpeciesGestation` + its structure test were untouched except for the new rows. **(2) The biological VALUES are an owner/veterinary call ‚Äì surface them, never invent them (the charter pattern, cf.¬Ý\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] and Learning 168).** The issue said ‚Äúsupply biologically appropriate values ‚Ä¶ a methodology/veterinary call‚Äù; the owner supplied a 14-species table of breeding-age + gestation RANGES. The range-\>single-value rule was ITSELF a decision surfaced via `AskUserQuestion`: minimum breeding age = LOW end (youngest plausible breeder); gestation = conservative MAXIMUM = HIGH end (a wider potential-parents window never misses a true parent). **(3) Don‚Äôt silently overwrite a deliberate existing value just because the owner re-supplied it.** Rhesus already carried gestation 210 (a documented conservative bound, ~1.27x the ~165 typical, cited Vinson & Raboin 2015); the owner‚Äôs supplied 164-166 was the TYPICAL figure. The issue scopes gestation to ‚Äúwhere missing‚Äù and rhesus is NOT missing -\> surfaced keep-210-vs-change-to-166 as an owner `AskUserQuestion` (owner: keep 210), avoiding a change to `getPotentialParents` behavior + its tests. **(4) Prove blast radius with the DATA before assuming a behavioral change ripples.** Rhesus female moved 3 -\> 2.5, which SOUNDS like it shifts the GVA mean-kinship peer-cohort threshold ‚Äì but a firsthand check confirmed NO shipped pedigree (`qcPed`/`breederPed`/`examplePedigree`) carries a `species` column, so on real data the correction always uses the species-absent default cutoff 2; the rhesus and new-species cutoffs are exercised ONLY by the accessor unit tests + the synthetic species-bearing fixture in `test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R` (which uses the rhesus MALE cutoff 4, unchanged). So the whole change was contained to two test files (`test_getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R`, `test_getSpeciesGestation.R`); `test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R` + `test_reportGV.R` were VERIFIED-unaffected (a grep of all four tests + the full regression), not assumed. Consequence carried from Slice 2/3: the new-species values have unit/fixture coverage but NO real-shipped-data exercise (inherent to the data; \#73 itself ships no species-bearing dataset). **(5) `.lintr` excludes `tests/` but NOT `data-raw/`.** The bare-numeric test literals (`4`, `2.5`) are lint-exempt, but the `data-raw/speciesGestation.R` numeric vectors trip `implicit_integer_linter` and MUST be written as explicit doubles (`4.0`, `12.0`) ‚Äì CI runs `lintr::lint_package()`, which lints `data-raw`. Re-run `Rscript data-raw/speciesGestation.R` after editing (the `.RData` is the artifact; the source edit alone does nothing ‚Äì Learning 167 item 4) and `devtools::document()` (man regen; NAMESPACE stays byte-identical for a data/internal change). Scope discipline: Part 1 (data) shipped as its own vertical slice; Part 2 (user-configurable override path ‚Äì the accessors already accept `breedingTable`/`gestationTable` but nothing threads them through `reportGV`/`modGeneticValue`, and the Settings tab at `appUI.R:223-228` is an empty placeholder) deferred to a separate session, so \#73 stays OPEN. Strict TDD throughout (RED 49 assertions failing for the right reason; GREEN data + coercion minimum; REFACTOR doc + lint). Build-equivalent `devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` = 0/0/0. (S182, issue \#73 Part 1 ‚Äì populate the species breeding-age table)

#### Learning 171 ‚Äì Publishing a slice whose NEWS bullet SUPERSEDES an earlier dev-version statement: append a self-framing bullet (‚Äúpreviously X ‚Ä¶ now Y‚Äù), do NOT rewrite the prior-slice bullets. Issue \#73 Part 1 generalized `speciesGestation` from rhesus-only to 14 species, but the dev-version `NEWS.md` already carried three earlier bullets asserting ‚Äúthe shipped species table currently defines only rhesus macaque (210 days)‚Äù (the `getPotentialParents`, the Potential-Parents-tab, and the `speciesGestation`-dataset bullets from prior slices). A new bullet saying ‚Äúnow 14 species‚Äù DIRECTLY contradicts them within the SAME unreleased section ‚Äì the first real contradiction in this project‚Äôs incremental dev-NEWS (prior slices only ADDED non-conflicting bullets). Resolution that honors coherence AND the append-don‚Äôt-rewrite convention AND scope discipline (the deliverable is ‚Äúpublish Part 1‚Äù, not ‚Äúrewrite Part-1-adjacent NEWS‚Äù): write the new bullet to NARRATE the progression ‚Äì ‚ÄúThe bundled `speciesGestation` table, previously seeded with only rhesus macaque, is now populated for 14 common colony NHP species ‚Ä¶‚Äù ‚Äì so a top-down reader sees the earlier ‚Äúonly rhesus‚Äù bullets as the prior increments and this bullet as the supersede, with NO prior bullet edited (no FM \#20/#22 risk, no history rewrite). The fallback clause (‚Äúspecies not listed fall back to the defaults, so results are unchanged for pedigrees without a `species` column‚Äù) also keeps the earlier bullets‚Äô ‚Äúunchanged on existing data‚Äù claim true, since no shipped pedigree carries a `species` column. Two supporting reflexes. **(a) Keep a NEWS bullet at SUMMARY level to hold `spell_check_package` at 0 delta** ‚Äì enumerating the 14 species common names (cynomolgus, vervet, tamarin, bonobo ‚Ä¶) would have needed `inst/WORDLIST` additions; ‚Äú14 common colony NHP species‚Äù is informative, ASCII-clean, and 0-delta (the species detail lives in the data set + `data-raw`). Code identifiers in backticks are stripped by `spell_check_package`, so backticked accessor names cost nothing (the existing `getSpeciesGestation`/`getPotentialParents` refs already prove the baseline is 0). **(b) pandoc escapes `#NN` as `\#NN` mid-line and this is NORMAL, not a render trap** ‚Äì the rendered `NEWS.md` shows `issue \#73`, exactly matching the existing refs (`\#31`/`\#49`/`\#38` are already in the file at 0-delta); `\#` renders as a literal `#` on GitHub/pkgdown, so it is consistent, not a defect ‚Äì do not ‚Äúfix‚Äù it. Publish mechanics otherwise textbook and unchanged (146/152/155/157/157a/159/161): no push-master-first this cycle because S181 ended the local-ahead drift (\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]) so the branch cut directly off an in-sync `master` (verify `master`==`origin/master` firsthand before relying on this); PR diff Part-1-only (3 commits); did-not-merge-blind (fresh non-watch `gh pr checks` re-query); `AskUserQuestion`-gated merge with a fresh state re-check (`headRefOid`==local, `origin/master`==expected, OPEN/MERGEABLE/CLEAN); ancestor-gated `reset --hard` + verified-merged-before-delete (`gh api` 404). **Apply:** any publish whose user-facing NEWS bullet updates/expands a statement an earlier dev-version bullet already made (the next such case is \#73 Part 2‚Äôs ‚Äúnow user-configurable‚Äù, which will supersede this very bullet‚Äôs ‚ÄúMaking these values user-configurable is the remaining part of issue \#73‚Äù ‚Äì self-frame it again). (S183, publish issue \#73 Part 1)

#### Learning 172 ‚Äì Planning a config-EXTENSION feature in this project: `getSiteInfo` is a FIXED hardcoded-key schema, not a tolerant passthrough ‚Äì so an optional override key follows the `getConfigApiKey` soft-lookup precedent, and a read-only grounding workflow‚Äôs load-bearing claims must be adjudicated FIRSTHAND. Issue \#73 Part 2 makes the species breeding-age / gestation / fallback values user-configurable; the owner chose the config-file mechanism (over the empty Settings-tab UI) for all three. Three reflexes the planning grounding produced. **(1) `getSiteInfo()` returns only ~17 hardcoded keys (7 config + ~10 system), each pulled via `getParamDef()` which STOPS on a missing key (`R/getParamDef.R:11`) ‚Äì there is NO arbitrary-key passthrough.** Adding inline per-species table keys to the config would force changes to `getSiteInfo` + the strict-lookup behavior; the clean path is a single optional key (a CSV path) read by a separate `getConfigApiKey`-style soft-lookup helper (`R/getConfigApiKey.R` returns `""` if absent, is NOT in `getSiteInfo`/`defaultSiteParams`, pinned by `test_setLabKeyDefaults.R`) ‚Äì ZERO `getSiteInfo` changes. The CSV header mirrors the bundled `speciesGestation` columns (`species, gestation, minMaleBreedingAge, minFemaleBreedingAge`), so a colony exports the bundled table, edits a few rows, and points at it ‚Äì self-documenting, no parenthesized-list syntax for tabular data. This is the D1 recommendation. **(2) A grounding workflow (here a 4-mapper read-only fan-out, `wf_c21598f3-932`) accelerates a planning inventory, but its load-bearing claims must be verified firsthand (FM \#11/#20, Learning 6) ‚Äì the mappers can DISAGREE or OVERSTATE.** The four mappers split on D1 (config-subsystem specialist: CSV path; the chain mappers: inline keys); the config specialist had the stronger case on the merits (the fixed-schema fact above), which I adjudicated by reading the parser firsthand, NOT by vote. And the gestation mapper called `modPotentialParents` accepting `gestationTable` but not passing it to `getPotentialParents` (`R/modPotentialParents.R:242`) ‚Äúthe single most load-bearing finding ‚Ä¶ broken at the critical call site‚Äù ‚Äì firsthand reading showed it is a PREFILL-ONLY no-op: the module always forces `maxGestationalPeriod` non-NULL (`:239-240`), and `getPotentialParents` consults `gestationTable` only when that arg is NULL (`R/getPotentialParents.R:63-69`), so passing the table there changes nothing today; the override‚Äôs real live effect is the `gestationDefault` prefill (`:216-219`), and the true gap is that `appServer` passes the module NO `gestationTable` at all (`:307-310`). The workflow found the files fast; the firsthand read corrected the framing. **(3) The override accessors REPLACE, so a config override must MERGE onto the bundled table in the reader (the D4 dragon).** `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`/`getSpeciesGestation` treat a non-NULL table as a FULL replacement ‚Äì a species absent from the supplied table falls to the fallback default, NOT to the bundled value. So a colony CSV listing a few species would silently mis-age every unlisted species (rhesus -\> 2.0 / 210). The reader must merge user rows onto bundled `speciesGestation` (user wins per-species; unlisted keep bundled), leaving the accessors unchanged. Two more carried reflexes: the module-config wiring template already exists (`modInputServer("dataInput", config = reactive(shared$config))` at `R/appServer.R:106`; `modORIPReportingServer(..., siteConfig = reactive(...))` at `:295`) ‚Äì the two target modules (`modGeneticValueServer:266`, `modPotentialParentsServer:307`) receive only `pedigree`, so the gap is precise; and a configurable `default` threaded above the accessor must default to `NULL` (not 2.0/210) so the accessor‚Äôs built-in is respected when no config is present, preserving the no-config == today invariant. PLANNING session: the deliverable is `docs/planning/issue73-part2-user-configurable-plan.md` (evidence inventory + D1-D7 for ratification + two vertical slices: GVA tab end-to-end, then Potential Parents tab); NO implementation (FM \#18/#25); D1/D4 await owner ratification before Slice 1 RED. (S184, plan issue \#73 Part 2)

#### Learning 173 ‚Äì Ratifying a predecessor‚Äôs plan is its own discrete session in the plan -\> ratify -\> implement arc ‚Äì and the ratifier re-verifies the load-bearing claims FIRSTHAND before presenting recommendations, never rubber-stamps. Issue \#73 Part 2‚Äôs arc is plan (S184) -\> ratify (S185) -\> implement-per-slice (future). S185‚Äôs deliverable was NOT code: it was flipping `docs/planning/issue73-part2-user-configurable-plan.md` from `Status: DRAFT` to `Status: RATIFIED -- ready to RED`, recording the owner‚Äôs decisions, and resolving the ¬ß7 checklist ‚Äì the gating step before any Slice 1 RED (the RED tests‚Äô shape depends on D1=CSV-path and D4=merge, so coding before ratification is FM \#19 plan-mode-bypass). **(1) The ratifier surfaces the genuine owner calls via `AskUserQuestion`, not prose (\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]).** S185 asked four: D1 (config format), D4 (override scope), D5 (gestation reach), and a bundle of the four low-stakes details (D2/D3/D6/D7) as ‚Äúaccept all vs review individually‚Äù ‚Äì respecting the 4-question max while still surfacing each load-bearing decision individually. The owner ratified all as recommended. **(2) Before presenting a predecessor‚Äôs recommendation for ratification, read the code that the recommendation rests on ‚Äì the predecessor may be right yet have left a sharp edge (FM \#11/#20, Learning 6).** S184‚Äôs D1/D4/R2 all held when re-read firsthand (`getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R:39-41` substitutes the bundled table only when the override is NULL, then matches against ONLY the supplied table -\> absent species fall to `default`, confirming D4-replace is real; `getParamDef.R:12` STOPs, `getConfigApiKey.R` is the soft-lookup, confirming D1), so this was a confirm, not a correction ‚Äì BUT the firsthand read SHARPENED R2: the accessors have NO `NULL` handling on `default` (`out <- rep(default, n)` -\> `rep(NULL, n)` = `numeric(0)`/`integer(0)`), so an upper layer threading a configurable default must translate ‚Äúno configured default‚Äù into NOT passing `default` to the accessor (omit the arg, or compute `x %||% <built-in>` AT the accessor call) ‚Äì threading a bare `NULL` into `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`/`getSpeciesGestation`‚Äôs `default` silently empties the result. This caveat was added to the plan‚Äôs R2 (the Slice 1 implementer reads it there) so the discovery survives. **(3) Mark the ratification at every place an executor reads:** a top ‚ÄúRatification record‚Äù + a `Status: RATIFIED` banner + the ¬ß3 header note (‚Äúinline ‚Äò*Ratify ‚Ä¶*‚Äô notes are now settled‚Äù) + the ¬ß7 checklist flipped to `[x]` with the chosen option ‚Äì so an executor jumping to any section sees the decision is closed, not open. Ratification is a docs-only deliverable (no R/test change -\> TDD code-phases N/A, Phase-3E N/A) committed direct to local `master` then pushed to origin (\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]); do NOT bundle it with the Slice 1 implement (FM \#18/#25). (S185, ratify issue \#73 Part 2 plan)

#### Learning 174 ‚Äì An optional config-file key whose example is documented as a commented `# key = value` line MUST have its soft-lookup strip `#` comment lines before `getTokenList`, or every user who ships the example config silently ACTIVATES the placeholder. Discovered implementing issue \#73 Part 2 Slice 1 (the user-configurable species overrides). `getTokenList` (`R/getTokenList.R`) collapses ALL config lines into one string and splits on whitespace/commas/`=`; it does NOT strip `#` comments. It detects a parameter as ‚Äúthe token immediately before an `=`‚Äù, so a commented line `# speciesOverridesPath = "/home/you/overrides.csv"` yields tokens `[#, speciesOverridesPath, =, /home/you/...]` and `speciesOverridesPath` IS detected as an active param. `getSiteInfo` tolerates this because it only LOOKS UP specific hardcoded keys (center/baseUrl/‚Ä¶) which never appear as `key =` inside comments ‚Äì spurious comment-derived params are simply never queried. But a `getConfigApiKey`-style soft-lookup takes the FIRST match by name (`tokenList$tokenVec[idx][[1L]][[1L]]`), so when a commented example line exists and no active line does, it returns the COMMENTED placeholder. The Phase-3E boot smoke caught it: `appServer` -\> `loadSpeciesOverrides()` read the example config‚Äôs commented `# speciesOverridesPath = "/home/you/colony_species_overrides.csv"` as active and warned ‚Äúfile does not exist‚Äù at boot. **Fix:** strip full-line comments in the soft-lookup before tokenizing ‚Äì `lines <- lines[!grepl("^\\s*#", lines)]` (in the shared `getOptionalConfigToken` helper, used by `getSpeciesOverridesPath` and the fallback-key lookups). Added two regression tests (a commented key is inactive; a commented config yields bundled behavior with no warning). NOTE: the SAME latent bug exists in `R/getConfigApiKey.R` (the example‚Äôs commented `# apiKey = "paste-your-..."` line would be read as the API key) ‚Äì left UNFIXED as out of Slice 1 scope, but flag it if apiKey-from-example ever misbehaves. Three carried reflexes that worked cleanly this session. **(a) R2 omit-when-NULL:** a configurable `default` threaded above `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`/`getSpeciesGestation` must NOT be passed as a bare `NULL` (`rep(NULL, n)` empties the result, Learning 173); the clean pattern is an `if (is.null(default)) accessor(...) else accessor(..., default = default)` at the call site, so the accessor‚Äôs built-in 2.0/210 applies for no-config. **(b) D4 merge-in-the-reader:** the accessors REPLACE, so `loadSpeciesOverrides` merges the user CSV onto bundled `speciesGestation` (user wins per-species; unlisted keep bundled) and returns the full merged table for BOTH `breedingTable` and `gestationTable` (the accessors pick their own columns). **(c) Verify every RED numeric target with standalone R BEFORE writing the test** (FM \#11/Learning 6): I recomputed every cohort/correction expected value (B1-B4 unit fixtures, C1-C5 on qcPed via the real correction fn) with independent base-R before writing the assertions ‚Äì zero targets were wrong at GREEN, and the species-augmented qcPed trick (add `species="RHESUS"`, custom table male 2 vs bundled 4) let the `breedingTable`/`gestationTable` params be exercised through `reportGV` on the otherwise species-less qcPed. IMPLEMENTATION session: code + tests + close-out docs on branch `issue-73-part2-slice1-gva-overrides`; NOT published (PR is a separate session, FM \#18/#25); the heavy slice (reader + 3 signatures + module + appServer) fit one session. (S186, implement issue \#73 Part 2 Slice 1)

#### Learning 175 ‚Äì A clean `devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` (0/0/0) does NOT guarantee `spelling::spell_check_package(".")` = 0: the package‚Äôs spelling check (`tests/spelling.R` -\> `spell_check_test(vignettes = TRUE, error = FALSE, skip_on_cran = TRUE)`) is SKIPPED under `--as-cran` (which `devtools::check` runs), so an unrecognized PROSE word in an `.Rd`/man page or NEWS ships clean through the implementing session‚Äôs build-equivalent. The publish session‚Äôs independent `spell_check_package(".")` run (Learning 159) is the safety net ‚Äì run it, do not infer spelling-clean from a 0/0/0 check. Publishing issue \#73 Part 2 Slice 1 (S187): S186‚Äôs close-out reported the build-equivalent 0/0/0 but did NOT run `spell_check_package`, so the noun `fallbacks` (introduced in S186‚Äôs `man/loadSpeciesOverrides.Rd` roxygen) shipped as an unrecognized word ‚Äì the publish session‚Äôs baseline `spell_check_package(".")` flagged exactly it. Fixed within publish scope by adding `fallbacks` to `inst/WORDLIST` (the only lever ‚Äì there is no `tests/spelling.Rout.save`, Learning 159), then re-running to confirm 0. **Two reflexes that kept the NEWS edit 0-delta:** (a) write NEWS prose in the project‚Äôs existing CLEAN idiom ‚Äì the verb form ‚Äúfall back‚Äù / ‚Äúthe values used for species not in the table‚Äù rather than the noun ‚Äúfallback(s)‚Äù the dictionary lacks ‚Äì and backtick every identifier (`spell_check_package` strips code spans), so the two new bullets added ZERO new flagged words (verified before AND after); (b) the WORDLIST addition is test/check infrastructure, not a user-facing package change, so it earns a CHANGELOG line, NOT a NEWS line (\[\[news-vs-changelog\]\], Learning 155). **Learning 171‚Äôs self-frame-supersede prediction held exactly:** 171 named ‚Äúthe next such case is \#73 Part 2‚Äôs ‚Äònow user-configurable‚Äô, which will supersede this very bullet‚Äôs ‚ÄòMaking these values user-configurable is the remaining part of issue \#73‚Äô.‚Äù The Slice 1 *Changes* bullet self-framed (‚Äú‚Ä¶are now user-configurable through the configuration file ‚Ä¶ This makes the Genetic Value Analysis configurable; making the Potential Parents tab configurable is the remaining part of issue \#73‚Äù) ‚Äì narrowing the prior ‚Äúremaining part‚Äù to the Potential Parents tab ‚Äì with the Part 1 bullet left UNEDITED (no FM \#20/#22, no history rewrite). The rendered `\#73` escape is normal pandoc, not a render trap (Learning 171b). Publish mechanics otherwise textbook (146/152/155/157/157a/159/161): `master`==`origin/master`==`32155f3a` verified FIRSTHAND so no push-master-first; PR diff Slice-1-only (3 commits: S186 feat `2ec4c329` + S186 close-out `81319c10` + S187 NEWS `7e4404e8`); all 10 CI checks PASS re-queried FRESH non-watch (Learning 157b ‚Äì never trust the `--watch` exit); `AskUserQuestion`-gated merge with a fresh OPEN/MERGEABLE/CLEAN + `headRefOid`==local re-check; ancestor-gated `reset --hard` (both old-master AND branch tip asserted ancestors of merge commit `54b11740`, Learning 146); verified-merged-before-delete (`gh api` 404); the S187 1B stub stash-carried OUT of the PR and popped clean onto master. **Issue \#73 stays OPEN** ‚Äì Slice 1 (GVA tab) is live; Slice 2 (Potential Parents tab) remains, and its PR will use ‚ÄúCloses \#73‚Äù. (S187, publish issue \#73 Part 2 Slice 1)

#### Learning 176 ‚Äì When you add an argument to a Shiny `moduleServer` whose name collides with an existing local reactive in that module, the local binding SHADOWS the argument inside every closure created after it, so references resolve to the reactive object (a function), not the argument value ‚Äì a SILENT bug (no error; the value just goes wrong). Implementing issue \#73 Part 2 Slice 2 (the user-configurable gestation override for the Potential Parents tab): `modPotentialParentsServer` already had a local `gestationDefault <- reactive({...})` that is ALSO its public returned key (`list(..., gestationDefault = gestationDefault)`, pinned by `test_modPotentialParents.R`). Adding a same-named `gestationDefault = NULL` argument would have made `pedigreeGestationDefault(ped, gestationDefault = gestationDefault)` inside that reactive pass the REACTIVE OBJECT, not the argument ‚Äì `getSpeciesGestation` would receive a function as `default`. **Fix:** rename the INTERNAL reactive (`gestationDefaultReactive`) and keep the public returned key `gestationDefault = gestationDefaultReactive`; the argument is now unshadowed and resolves correctly. **Do NOT instead dodge the shadow by capturing the arg into a local at module setup** (`gd <- gestationDefault` at the top of the moduleServer body): that EAGERLY forces the argument promise during module CONSTRUCTION, and for a boot-loaded override (`appServer` passes `shared$speciesOverrides$gestationDefault`, populated by an `observe` that has NOT run when the module is built) the forced value is the pre-boot `NULL`. The argument MUST stay a lazily-forced promise ‚Äì first forced inside the prefill reactive, which fires on pedigree load (`observeEvent(pedigree(), ...)` has `ignoreNULL = TRUE`, so it does not fire at boot), well after the boot `observe` has populated `shared$speciesOverrides`. So: rename the reactive, do not pre-capture the arg. R2 reused unchanged ‚Äì thread the override `default` to `getSpeciesGestation` via omit-when-NULL (`if (is.null(gestationDefault)) accessor(species, gestationTable = ...) else accessor(species, gestationTable = ..., default = gestationDefault)`), never a bare `NULL`. **Phase-3E config-completeness gotcha (cost me one smoke run):** a config file that EXISTS but lacks the fixed site-config keys (center/baseUrl/‚Ä¶) crashes `appServer` boot ‚Äì NOT via the Slice 2 wiring but via the PRE-EXISTING ORIP-tab line `getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE)` (`appServer.R:294`), which strict-parses the file and STOPs on a missing `center` (the tolerant `loadSiteConfig()` at boot `:70` is a separate, `tryCatch`-wrapped call). An override-only config is NOT a supported file; a real config is the full bundled example site config PLUS the appended override keys (exactly how S186 ran Slice 1‚Äôs boot smoke). Run the appServer Phase-3E boot with `file.copy(system.file("extdata","example_nprcgenekeepr_config",package="nprcgenekeepr"))` + appended `speciesOverridesPath`/`gestationDefault`, not a minimal stub. The plain-param wiring (owner-chosen over a Slice-1-style reactive param, to match the existing `gestationTable` seam and preserve the \#46 tests) works precisely because the override arg is forced lazily AFTER boot: verified firsthand ‚Äì `shared$speciesOverrides` populated at boot (RHESUS gestation 999, gestationDefault 300, unlisted CYNOMOLGUS kept bundled 170), module prefill 999 (override) / 300 (species-less fallback), and no-config boot 210 (byte-identical to today). D5 prefill-only scope was already structurally true (the module forces `maxGestationalPeriod` non-NULL, so `getPotentialParents` never consults the table for the computed window) ‚Äì Slice 2 touched only the prefill, never `R/modPotentialParents.R:242`. (S188, implement issue \#73 Part 2 Slice 2)

#### Learning 177 ‚Äì `codecov/patch` is PATCH-COMPOSITION-sensitive: a small publish patch can FAIL `codecov/patch` even when the identical wiring PASSED in a larger earlier slice, because patch coverage is the percentage of *changed executable lines* hit ‚Äì a handful of structurally-uncovered glue lines dominates a small diff but is diluted in a large one. Publishing issue \#73 Part 2 Slice 2 (S189): the FRESH non-watch `gh pr checks 79` (Learning 157b) returned exit 1 with `codecov/patch` red ‚Äì **76.93% of diff hit vs auto target 90.01%** ‚Äì while all 9 `R CMD check`/lint/pkgdown/test-coverage jobs passed and `codecov/project` passed (89.99%, -0.02%, flat). The shortfall was the 2 new `appServer` boot-wiring lines (`gestationTable`/`gestationDefault` passed to `modPotentialParentsServer`), which `covr` CANNOT execute ‚Äì `appServer` is never run under coverage; S188 verified that wiring with a `deparse`-match test (source inspection, not execution) + the Phase-3E runtime smoke, neither of which `covr` counts. The module LOGIC (both `pedigreeGestationDefault` branches, the `gestationDefaultReactive`) IS covered by the executing module tests. Slice 1‚Äôs PR \#78 had the SAME appServer-wiring blind spot yet `codecov/patch` passed at **94.24%** ‚Äì because Slice 1‚Äôs large, reader-heavy patch (19-test `loadSpeciesOverrides` reader) diluted the few uncovered lines; Slice 2‚Äôs ~13-line patch could not. So a red `codecov/patch` on a small publish patch is a composition artifact, NOT necessarily a deliverable defect ‚Äì but PROVE it: get the codecov check-run summary (`gh api repos/<o>/<r>/commits/<sha>/check-runs -q '.check_runs[]|select(.name|test("codecov"))|.output.summary'` gives the ‚ÄúX% of diff hit (target Y%)‚Äù line), read the R diff, and attribute every uncovered line to either structural-non-execution (boot wiring, verified by other means) or a real test gap. THREE handling rules. **(a) `codecov/patch` is not a merge gate here** ‚Äì `master` has no branch protection, so `mergeStateStatus` is UNSTABLE (not BLOCKED) and `mergeable` stays MERGEABLE; UNSTABLE = mergeable-with-non-passing-non-required-checks. Confirm via `gh api repos/<o>/<r>/branches/master/protection/required_status_checks` (404 = no required checks). **(b) A publish session must NOT fix a red patch by adding test code** ‚Äì writing a test that boots `appServer` under coverage is implementation work (and reopens the fiddly Phase-3E `getSiteInfo` boot trap, Learning 176); bundling it into a publish is FM \#18/#25. The right response is to SURFACE the red check + its diagnosed cause to the owner via `AskUserQuestion` (never merge a red check silently, never paper it over) and let them decide merge-now vs defer-for-coverage. **(c)** `gh pr view` has NO `baseRefOid` or (for issues) `stateReason` field ‚Äì an invalid `--json` field makes the WHOLE query return empty, which a guard reads as a failure; this actually HELPED (my guarded merge refused to run on the empty values), but use only valid fields (`state`/`mergeable`/`headRefOid` for PRs; verify `origin/master` via `git rev-parse` separately) and verify a closed issue with `state`/`closed`/`closedAt`. Publish mechanics otherwise textbook (146/155/157/157a/171): NEWS *Changes* bullet self-framed to supersede Slice 1‚Äôs ‚Äúremaining part of issue \#73‚Äù line (Learning 171‚Äôs prediction, now fulfilled ‚Äì ‚ÄúThis completes issue \#73‚Äù), 0-delta spelling, confined pure-insertion; `master`==`origin/master`==`c512771a` firsthand (no push-master-first); PR diff Slice-2-only 3 commits (S188 feat `e012e6ad` + S188 close-out `866b0a7f` + S189 NEWS `8ce3f937`); `AskUserQuestion`-gated merge `gh pr merge 79 --merge` -\> `0980a028`; **issue \#73 verified CLOSED** (`closedAt` == merge time, ‚ÄúCloses \#73‚Äù worked); ancestor-gated `reset --hard` (old-master `c512771a` AND tip `8ce3f937` both ancestors of `0980a028`); verified-merged-before-delete (`gh api` 404). **Issue \#73 is now CLOSED** ‚Äì Part 1 (data) + Slice 1 (GVA tab) + Slice 2 (Potential Parents tab) all live on `master`. (S189, publish issue \#73 Part 2 Slice 2)

#### Learning 178 ‚Äì A backlog item / handoff that asserts a change is invasive (‚Äúreverses the documented stance‚Äù, ‚Äúbreaks golden invariants‚Äù, ‚Äúneeds replacement golden values‚Äù) states a HYPOTHESIS about blast radius, not a fact ‚Äì verify it firsthand during ratification, because the clean realization of the *ratified behavior* is often far shallower than the issue assumed. Ratifying issue \#76 Reading A (de-inflate genome uniqueness `gu` for both-unknown founders): the issue body AND the S189 handoff both said Reading A must change `calcGU`/`calcA`/gene-drop, reverse the `R/calcGU.R:10-34` stance, and supply replacement golden values (issue acceptance criterion \#1). A 10-agent research + adversarial-critique workflow (`wf_67d4c94a-691`) plus firsthand pipeline reads showed the owner-ratified ‚Äúdecline-to-credit‚Äù (report unknown-origin founders‚Äô `gu` as 0) has a REPORT-LAYER realization in `reportGV` (~3-5 lines after `classifyParentage` at `reportGV.R:184`, before the `cbind`/`$gu`) that changes NONE of those functions or their golden tests and does NOT reverse the documented stance ‚Äì same displayed numbers, far lower risk. The ratification deliverable legitimately concluded the issue OVER-SCOPED the change: criterion \#1‚Äôs ‚Äúreplacement gene-drop goldens‚Äù became ‚Äúzero gene-drop changes + new report-layer tests.‚Äù Treat ‚Äúthis is invasive‚Äù as the question the ratification answers, not a given. **(a) The targeting trap, generalized ‚Äì the classification set is NOT the gene-drop founder set.** For unknown-parent work the U-id-aware ‚Äúboth unknown‚Äù classification set (`classifyParentage.R:21`: `is.na(x) | isGeneratedUnknownId(x)`) differs from the gene-drop `isFounder`/`getFounders` set: after `addParents` synthesizes U-stub rows with `NA` parents, a U-id-parented proband is ‚Äúboth unknown‚Äù by classification but a NON-founder at the gene-drop level (it inherits from its U-stub parents), while a literal-`NA` animal IS a gene-drop founder. Target the classification/report set where `origin` is known (= `orderReport`‚Äôs `noParentage` bucket, `orderReport.R:38-62`), NOT the gene-drop set. Mis-targeting the gene-drop set was the fatal flaw that made candidate A2 (tag phantom alleles at the mint point) unsound ‚Äì it marks EVERY founder, not the Undetermined subset. **(b) Decline-to-credit resolves to a single value.** Because the target animals have BOTH parents unknown, all their gene-drop alleles trace to phantom mints (`assignAlleles.R:33-36`), so ‚Äúexclude the artifact alleles‚Äù = `gu` -\> 0 (no formula to tune). Apply to BOTH the report `gu` column and the `$gu` element for one coherent reported statistic; never write `NA` (it poisons `rank()`/`order()`); the Slice-3 demotion (flag-driven on `value=="Undetermined"`, `rankSubjects.R:37-44`) composes on top unchanged, and imports (both-unknown WITH a recorded `origin`) are preserved. **(c) Adversarial-candidate review earns its keep on a stance-reversal ratification.** The workflow killed 3 of 4 candidates with VERIFIED fatal flaws ‚Äì A4 rescales the wrong term (the inflation is in the numerator `rowSums(rare)` at `calcGU.R:91`, not the `/(2L*iterations)` denominator); A3 injects per-iteration randomness and breaks the seeded gene-drop goldens; A2 mis-targets all founders ‚Äì and refined the survivor A1 (target `noParentage`, not the full ‚Äúboth unknown‚Äù set). Ratification is a docs-only DESIGN deliverable -\> TDD code-phases N/A, Phase-3E N/A; recorded in `docs/planning/issue76-gu-deinflation-ratification.md` in the ¬ß8-E house style (provenance/authority header, load-bearing finding, authoritative rule, RED/GREEN charter, rejected candidates, firsthand anchors), with a one-line cross-ref added to the issue-9 plan ¬ß8-F; committed direct to local `master` then pushed to origin (\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]); do NOT bundle with the implementation (FM \#18/#25 ‚Äì the ratify -\> implement -\> publish arc is 3 sessions min). (S190, ratify issue \#76 Reading A)

#### Learning 179 ‚Äì A gene-drop NON-founder‚Äôs genome uniqueness (`gu`) is structurally 0 at the default `guThresh = 1`, because every allele it carries is inherited from a parent that is ALSO in the analysed population, so no allele it holds is held by only one animal. This matters when writing a RED test that a U-id-PARENTED both-unknown animal (sire/dam are generated U-ids like ‚ÄúU0001‚Äù, not literal `NA`) gets de-inflated to `gu = 0` (issue \#76 Reading A ‚Äì proving the predicate‚Äôs U-id-awareness): such a proband is ‚Äúboth unknown‚Äù by `classifyParentage` (`is.na(x) | isGeneratedUnknownId(x)`) but a NON-founder at the gene-drop level (Learning 178a ‚Äì after `addParents` it inherits from its U-stub parents), so at `guThresh = 1` its `gu` is ALREADY 0 and de-inflating it is INVISIBLE ‚Äì the assertion `expect_equal(gu["P1"], 0)` would pass on the UNCHANGED code (not a real RED). Use `guThresh = 2` (an allele held by \<= 2 animals counts as rare): each of the proband‚Äôs two inherited alleles is then held by exactly {proband, parent} = 2 animals, so it counts as rare and the proband carries a strictly-positive `gu` (verified firsthand = 100 on the pre-change `reportGV`), making the de-inflation observable and the test RED-meaningful. Verified the threshold firsthand by running `reportGV(fixture, guIter = 1000L, guThresh = thr)` for thr in 1/2/3 BEFORE writing assertions (FM \#11/Learning 6): at thr=1 the U-id proband is 0 (literal-NA founders 26-50), at thr=2 founders AND the U-id proband are all \> 0 (the sweet spot), at thr=3 everything saturates to 100. **General test-design rule:** when a RED test must observe a value being DRIVEN to 0, first confirm the value is NON-zero on the unchanged code under the exact fixture+params, or the ‚Äúfailing‚Äù assertion silently passes. **Robustness:** assert only the DISCRIMINATING properties ‚Äì `== 0` for de-inflation targets (RNG-independent), `> 0` for preserved imports/known animals (the guard that proves targeting), and `$report` gu == `$gu` element (same-run internal consistency) ‚Äì NEVER exact seeded gu magnitudes, so the test survives RNG / R-version differences; a fixture WITH an `origin` column is REQUIRED to exercise the import-preservation branch (qcPed and `makeValidTestPed` both lack `origin`, so they only cover the no-origin -\> all-Undetermined path). The GREEN realization was exactly the ratified ~12 lines (origin if/else mirroring `orderReport.R:43-47`, the `undetermined` mask, `gu$gu[undetermined] <- 0.0`) after `reportGV.R:184` before the `cbind`; mutating the ONE `gu` data.frame covered BOTH the report column and `$gu`; all existing goldens held UNMODIFIED exactly as S190 predicted (verified: full regression 1225/0/0, `devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` 0/0/0, `calcGU`/`calcA`/`geneDrop` untouched). Re-read `modGeneticValue.R:224-231` firsthand (S190 gotcha \#8) and confirmed the Slice-3 demotion keys on `value == "Undetermined"` NOT on `gu` magnitude, so de-inflating `gu` to 0 leaves the displayed ranking unchanged ‚Äì the two corrections COMPOSE (Phase-3E: Undetermined ranked last 6-10/10 with gu 0, imports preserved at top). REFACTOR fixed only the 2 style lints GREEN introduced (line-wrap; `0` -\> `0.0` for the double `gu` column, `implicit_integer_linter`); the 2 `object_usage_linter` warnings on `classifyParentage`/`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` are pre-existing single-file false positives that resolve under `lint_package`. (S191, implement issue \#76 Reading A)

#### Learning 180 ‚Äì To clear a Shiny `fileInput` (reset BOTH the re-read behavior AND the displayed file name) WITHOUT adding a client-side dependency (`shinyjs`), combine three server-side moves: **(1)** render the file input dynamically ‚Äì `output$xUI <- renderUI({ key(); fileInput(session$ns("x"), ...) })` keyed by a `reactiveVal` counter, and bump the counter on ‚Äúclear‚Äù so a FRESH empty widget replaces the old one (this is what clears the displayed file name ‚Äì base shiny has no `updateFileInput`; the input id MUST be `session$ns()`-namespaced because `renderUI` content is not auto-namespaced inside a module); **(2)** blank companion text inputs with `updateTextAreaInput(session, ..., value = "")`; **(3)** hold a server-side ‚Äúcleared‚Äù guard ‚Äì `clearedFilePath`/`clearedText` `reactiveVal`s set on clear, and skip a read when the current value is `identical()` to the cleared one (a newly chosen file gets a new temp `datapath`, and any edited text differs, so genuine new input still loads; the one accepted edge is re-entering the EXACT cleared string, which the guard blocks). Implementing issue \#1 (Pedigree Browser ‚ÄúClear Focal Animals‚Äù left a loaded CSV that silently re-read on the next Update and kept showing the file name; the list-clearing checkbox itself had worked since 2020). The guard (moves 2/3) is what makes the behavior UNIT-testable via `shiny::testServer` ‚Äì assert through the module‚Äôs returned reactive that a cleared file/text is not re-read and that NEW input still loads (load a tempfile CSV, Update, clear+Update, then a 3rd Update with the SAME `focalAnimalFile`/`focalAnimalIds` still set = the browser-not-reset state -\> stays empty); a UI-structure test (`as.character(modPedigreeUI("id"))` no longer contains `type="file"` but DOES contain the `uiOutput` container id) covers move 1 RED-first. **What `testServer` canNOT verify is exactly the client-side half** ‚Äì the file name actually disappearing from the widget and the `updateTextAreaInput` round-trip ‚Äì **so that half needs an E2E (shinytest2 + chromote) assertion.** **Running this project‚Äôs opt-in E2E suite locally for real-browser Phase-3E evidence needs BOTH env vars:** `NPRC_RUN_E2E=true` (the `create_test_app()` opt-in gate, helper-shinytest2.R) AND `NOT_CRAN=true` (else `skip_on_cran()` skips EVERY e2e test ‚Äì a plain `Rscript ... testthat::test_file(...)` run silently skipped 9/9 until `NOT_CRAN` was set; this is the same `NOT_CRAN` condition the `[regression-read]` glossary names, just as critical for the e2e suite). chromote + Chrome ARE installed on this machine, so the browser tests DO run when both vars are set (verified: 9 pedigree e2e tests, 13 assertions, all pass; the new test reads the file-name display via `app$get_js` on the `.input-group input[type=text]` value and the textarea via `app$get_value(input = "pedigree-focalAnimalIds")`, confirming the raw `tags$textarea(id = ns("focalAnimalIds"))` binds as a normal shiny text input). E2E added to a behavior ALREADY implemented is a VERIFICATION test (passes on first run), not strict RED-first ‚Äì be explicit about that; the RED-first coverage of the reset MECHANISM is the testServer UI-structure test (move 1). REFACTOR was correctly SKIPPED (owner-gated) ‚Äì `lintr` clean on the changed R + test files, no duplication. Implement session: code + tests + close-out on branch `issue-1-clear-focal-animals`, NOT published (FM \#18/#25; publish PR uses ‚ÄúCloses \#1‚Äù + a NEWS *Changes* bullet, Learning 157a). (S193, implement issue \#1 ‚Äì clear focal animals)

#### Learning 181 ‚Äì In a PLANNING session whose design rests on non-trivial statistics or empirical claims, an adversarial-critique workflow phase that ACTUALLY runs checks against bundled data is high-ROI: it catches plausible-but-wrong claims BEFORE they enter the plan and misdirect the implementer. Planning issue \#2 (evidence-based GVA gene-drop iteration-count advice). A 7-agent investigate/design/critique workflow (`wf_fdb7c410-95f`, ultracode) produced a first design pass with two confident-but-wrong claims that the adversarial critic ‚Äì prompted to REFUTE and to probe `qcPed` empirically ‚Äì overturned: **(1)** the ‚Äúhomozygous-rare animals inflate the per-animal Monte Carlo SE by up to sqrt(2), so multiply needed N by (1+rho)‚Äù claim is FALSE at the default `guThresh = 1` ‚Äì a rare allele has population frequency \<= 1 (a single copy in the whole population), so a homozygote can never carry one; per-column rare counts are effectively {0,1} and the Bernoulli SE `sqrt(p(1-p)/(2K))` is adequate (slightly conservative), so guidance that would have ~doubled iteration counts was unfounded; **(2)** the rank-stability machinery was motivated by rank churn that does not measurably exist on the only bundled real pedigree ‚Äì `qcPed` is degenerate-bimodal (~56% of probands at gu=0 incl.¬Ýthe issue \#76 de-inflated set, ~43% near gu=50, ~2/280 in the mid-range; Spearman = 1.0 at every N \>= 125), so a TDD test of ‚Äúrecommend a small N‚Äù on qcPed passes TAUTOLOGICALLY ‚Äì a purpose-built dense-mid-range fixture must be built in RED FIRST or the test proves nothing. The critic also corrected a code mis-description (the GVA displayed rank is the bucketed `orderReport`/`rankSubjects` pipeline ‚Äì `gu > 10` cutoff, `-trunc(gu)`, `z <= 0.25` ‚Äì NOT `rank(indivMeanKin - gu)`), which moved the convergence diagnostic from generic Spearman to boundary-density counts. **General rule:** when a plan‚Äôs load-bearing claims are quantitative, add a verification pass that EXECUTES against real bundled data, not one that only reasons; and re-verify the most load-bearing CODE citations firsthand before authoring (FM \#11/#20, Learning 6) ‚Äì here `calcGU`/`calcA`/`reportGV`/`orderReport`/`modGeneticValue` were read directly, confirming `gu = rowSums(rare)/(2*iterations)` (`calcGU.R:97`) and the de-inflation at `reportGV.R:210-211`. **Caveat that must survive into implementation:** the critic‚Äôs empirical `qcPed` numbers are themselves subagent-sourced and are NOT to be trusted as settled ‚Äì they are re-established in the implementation session‚Äôs RED (the dense fixture + a golden-master `gu` before/after the `calcA` refactor are the verification step). **Design corollary (owner-steer handling):** when an owner steer is literally infeasible ‚Äì here ‚Äúreport convergence DURING the calculation,‚Äù blocked because rare-allele classification needs the whole-population frequency table per iteration column (`alleleFreq.R:30`/`calcA.R:33-36`) while `geneDrop` accumulates all-iterations-per-animal not all-animals-per-iteration ‚Äì deliver the realization that PRESERVES THE INTENT (compute the convergence curve from the single completed run via nested column-prefixes `V1..Vk`, since the columns are i.i.d. replicates) rather than implementing the letter or rejecting it. (S195, plan issue \#2)

#### Learning 182 ‚Äì The ratification gate is where subagent-sourced empirical claims must become FIRSTHAND, and the firsthand check must be against the authoritative function (ground truth), not just a re-derivation by the same method. Ratifying ¬ß8 of the issue \#2 plan. Learning 181 had explicitly flagged that the plan‚Äôs `qcPed` numbers were subagent-sourced and ‚ÄúNOT to be trusted as settled‚Äù ‚Äì S196 re-ran them firsthand and found 181‚Äôs OWN degeneracy description was wrong, proving the flag was load-bearing: (1) the `qcPed` Undetermined count is **124/280, not 156** (156 is the *rankable* count ‚Äì the two were swapped); (2) `qcPed` is NOT ‚Äúbimodal 56%/43%/2-mid-range‚Äù ‚Äì firsthand `{gu>0}` == `{both-unknown founders}` == `{the issue #76 Undetermined set}` are the **identical 124 animals** (`identical()` TRUE), every rankable animal is at `gu=0`, and **after the \#76 de-inflation all 280 are at `gu=0`**; (3) `pedWithGenotype`/`rhesusPedigree` are the same, and `examplePedigree` is the ONLY bundled pedigree with rankable `gu` signal (294/2322) but its gu-bearing animals are structurally fixed founder alleles (gu=100/50), so **selection order is identical at N=5 and N=2000** (top-20 overlap 1.0, max rank change 0). Consequence: **NO bundled pedigree can validate a `gu`-based ranking/convergence test** ‚Äì a dense-mid-range fixture built in Slice 2 RED is mandatory, not optional (strengthens Dragon \#2). The error mattered because the plan‚Äôs D3 justification (‚Äú`qcPed` converges by N~250-500‚Äù) was VACUOUS (no signal to converge); the grounded D3 evidence had to come from `examplePedigree` instead (order stable `<<1000`; per-animal SE `~1/sqrt(N)`: <0.79pp@1000> -\> <0.56pp@2000> -\> ~<0.35pp@5000>), which still supports aligning the default to 1000 but on honest footing. **Method that caught it:** replicate `reportGV`‚Äôs deterministic body once, gene-drop ONE `Nmax` run, recompute `gu` on nested column-prefixes (the i.i.d.-columns trick ‚Äì verified: prefix `gu` == `calcGU` to 0 abs diff), and **cross-check the headline counts against `reportGV(qcPed)` itself** ‚Äì the ground-truth call is what exposed ‚Äúall 280 at `gu=0`‚Äù, which a hand-replication alone (sharing the same conceptual error) might have reproduced. **Claims that DID hold firsthand** (so the critique was not all wrong): SE `~1/sqrt(N)`; `SE_exact/SE_approx` ~0.82-0.85 at `guThresh>=2`, ~1.0 at `guThresh=1` (Correction 1); the column-prefix trick; and `byID` is a no-op at `guThresh=1` (`TRUE`/`FALSE` identical). **General rule:** ‚Äúan adversarial critic ran checks‚Äù (Learning 181) is necessary but NOT sufficient ‚Äì before owner decisions are recorded against quantitative claims, the ratifying session re-establishes them personally and validates against the real function, because a wrong number that survives the critique will otherwise be ratified into the plan and mislead the implementer. (S196, ratify ¬ß8 of the issue \#2 plan)

#### Learning 183 ‚Äì To unit-test that a Monte Carlo standard error obeys the `1/sqrt(K)` (more-iterations -\> smaller-SE) law WITHOUT RNG flakiness, exploit that the SE is a deterministic function of a FIXED allele table: duplicate every gene-drop iteration column of a bundled `AlleleTable` (e.g.¬Ý`ped1Alleles`) so `K -> 2K` with identical per-column values, and the sample variance-of-the-mean SE then scales by the EXACT closed factor `sqrt((K-1)/(2K-1))` ‚Äì a strict-equality assertion, no seed, no tolerance band. Implementing issue \#2 Slice 1 (`calcGUSE()`: per-animal genome-uniqueness SE `100 * sqrt(var(rare[i,]/2)/K)`). Pair it with an **exact golden test that recomputes the SE straight from `calcA()`** (`100*sqrt(apply(rare/2,1,stats::var)/K)`) rather than re-running the helper, so the test proves the formula independently of the implementation; both are deterministic because they read a fixed table, not a fresh gene drop. **Three concrete gotchas this slice surfaced:** (1) **`calcGU`/`calcGUSE` rely on `as.data.frame(<named numeric>)` taking the COLUMN NAME from the local variable name and the rownames from the vector names** ‚Äì so the local must literally be named `guSE` to yield a `guSE` column; mirror `calcGU` exactly when adding a parallel helper. (2) **`test_reportGV.R` pins the EXACT report column list via `expect_named` (two of them)** ‚Äì adding any `reportGV` output column is therefore a RED edit to those lists (they fail until GREEN adds the column), in addition to the new feature tests; grep `expect_named` before assuming an additive column is invisible to existing tests. (3) **Column carry-through is free but must be confirmed:** `orderReport()`/`rankSubjects()` subset rows and `rbind`/add-columns by NAME, so a new column threaded into the `cbind` at `reportGV.R:213-215` flows into `$report` automatically ‚Äì verified, not assumed. **Scope discipline (FM \#18/#25):** `calcGUSE()` recomputes the rare matrix (a second `calcA` per `reportGV`); the obvious dedup (factor `rare` out of `calcA` so `gu` and `guSE` share one build) is deliberately Slice 2‚Äôs job (plan Finding 7, where the column-prefix convergence tool needs it) and was NOT pulled forward ‚Äì a real but bounded, planned inefficiency, flagged in code and handoff rather than silently ‚Äúimproved.‚Äù The same column-duplication + golden-from-`calcA` technique will test Slice 2‚Äôs `gvaConvergence()`. (S197, implement Slice 1 of issue \#2 ‚Äì guSE column)

#### Learning 184 ‚Äì GitHub‚Äôs issue auto-close parser IGNORES English negation: a PR body or commit message that contains the literal token sequence `close[s]/closed | fix[es]/fixed | resolve[s]/resolved` immediately followed by `#N` will close issue N on merge to the default branch EVEN inside a negated clause like ‚Äúdoes not close \#N‚Äù. Publishing Slice 1 of issue \#2 (PR \#83). The S197 handoff‚Äôs explicit ‚Äúdo NOT use ‚ÄòCloses \#2‚Äô‚Äù warning WAS followed ‚Äì the PR used ‚ÄúPart of \#2‚Äù in its lead ‚Äì but the body ALSO contained the sentence **‚ÄúThis is Slice 1 of 3 and does not close \#2‚Äù**, and the substring ‚Äúclose \#2‚Äù auto-closed the issue on merge (`gh pr merge 83 --merge` -\> \#2 went CLOSED). The intent was the exact opposite (Slice 1 of 3; Slice 3 closes \#2). **Rule:** for an issue you intend to keep OPEN, the PR body AND every commit message that lands on the default branch must contain NO `<closing-keyword> #N` substring at all ‚Äì not even negated (‚Äúdoes not close \#N‚Äù, ‚Äúwon‚Äôt fix \#N‚Äù, ‚Äúnot resolving \#N‚Äù all still fire). Refer to the issue only with a non-keyword form: ‚ÄúPart of \#N‚Äù, ‚ÄúAddresses part of \#N‚Äù, ‚ÄúSlice 1 of N, \#N‚Äù, or ‚Äú#N (stays open)‚Äù. To state that it does NOT close, restructure so a closing keyword never abuts the number, e.g.¬Ý‚Äú#2 remains open; Slice 3 will close it.‚Äù (The same trap applies to the three S197/S196/S195 commit *messages* on the branch ‚Äì they used ‚ÄúSlice 1 of issue \#2‚Äù, which is safe because ‚Äúissue‚Äù sits between any keyword and the number; ‚Äúof issue \#2‚Äù does not match.) **Detection (standing post-merge assertion):** immediately after `gh pr merge`, re-query the issue state ‚Äì `gh issue view N --json state` ‚Äì whenever the issue is meant to SURVIVE the merge; this session caught it within seconds because the merge step re-queried \#2. Note `closingIssuesReferences` is NOT a field in this repo‚Äôs `gh` version, so you cannot pre-check the GraphQL closing-links before merge ‚Äì query the issue‚Äôs `state` directly, before and after. **Recovery (fast, non-destructive):** `gh issue reopen N`, then `gh issue comment N` documenting that the auto-close was an unintended side effect of the PR wording and why the issue stays open ‚Äì so the issue history is not misleading. **RECURRENCE (same session, the sharpest part of this lesson):** the trap fired a SECOND time because the S198 close-out *commit message* QUOTED the offending keyword-plus-number token while DOCUMENTING the trap, and pushing that commit to the default branch (`master`) re-fired the parser ‚Äì \#2 auto-closed again, reopened again. So the rule is not ‚Äúwatch the PR description‚Äù ‚Äì it is ‚Äúthe literal `<closing-keyword> #N` token must not appear in ANY text GitHub parses for the default branch: the PR body OR any commit message landing on it, INCLUDING a message that merely quotes or explains the trap.‚Äù When you must write about it, break the token (`close #<N>`, ‚Äúthe close-keyword + number‚Äù, name the issue in a separate clause) and re-query `gh issue view N --json state` after EVERY push that could carry such a message, not just after the PR merge. File CONTENTS are safe ‚Äì GitHub parses commit messages and PR descriptions, not the diff ‚Äì so the same words are fine inside `SESSION_NOTES.md`/`CHANGELOG.md`/this learning; only commit messages and the PR body are live. (S198, publish Slice 1 of issue \#2)

#### Learning 185 ‚Äì Building the ‚Äúiteration count actually moves the selection order‚Äù fixture that Dragon \#2 demands, plus three techniques that made Slice 2 (`gvaConvergence()`) a low-risk, purely-additive slice. Implementing issue \#2 Slice 2. **(1) THE FIXTURE (the load-bearing dragon).** No bundled pedigree can exercise a `gu`-based ranking/convergence tool (Learning 182: every rankable bundled animal is at `gu=0` after the issue \#76 de-inflation; `examplePedigree`‚Äôs gu-bearers are structurally-fixed founder alleles, order-stable at `N=5`). The MECHANISM that produces rankable MID-RANGE `gu`: genome uniqueness concentrates at founders (a founder‚Äôs private gene-drop allele is ‚Äúrare‚Äù ‚Äì freq 1 among probands ‚Äì in every iteration it is NOT inherited), and the \#76 policy zeroes exactly those both-unknown founders, so signal vanishes. BREAK it by **EXCLUDING founders from the proband population** (`pop = descendants only`): then a founder‚Äôs private allele among probands is carried only by descendants, and a descendant who is intermittently the SOLE proband carrier of a rare founder allele gets intermediate `gu` AND ‚Äì having known parents ‚Äì is not zeroed by \#76. This is realistic (a colony ‚Äúpopulation‚Äù is living focal animals; founders are historical/dead). Tuning levers: more offspring per founder-pair -\> lower `gu` (roughly `0.5^k`); and the order CONVERGES at a finite `N` only under MODERATE crowding ‚Äì too crowded (many animals at IDENTICAL true `gu` = permanent ties) never converges (Kendall tau-b plateaus below the 0.95 threshold forever), too separated (distinct `k`-levels) converges trivially at the grid floor (top-`k` stable at `N=5`). The winning fixture is a half-sib WEB (14 founder sires x overlapping wrapping windows of 5 founder dams over 15 dams, one offspring per pair, founders excluded -\> 70 probands): the window overlap gives probands distinct-but-close true `gu` straddling the `gu=10` cutoff, so at low `N` the gene-drop estimate randomly crosses the cutoff (a highGu\<-\>lowMk bucket flip is a large rank jump) and settles as `N` grows. Found by a 7-strategy parallel parameter-search workflow + a judge stage, then **RE-VALIDATED FIRSTHAND** (seed 11: unstable at `N=25`, top-20 overlap 0.75; converges by `N~800`; monotone curve; `recommendedIter` varies 200-800 across seeds 11/22/33). **(2) THE COLUMN-PREFIX TRICK NEEDS NO `calcA`/`reportGV` REFACTOR** (corrects the plan‚Äôs Finding 7 framing). `calcA` ALREADY returns the per-column rare matrix and classifies each column‚Äôs rareness from that column ALONE (`apply(alleles, 2L, countRare)`), so `rowSums(calcA(full)[, 1:k]) / (2k) * 100 == calcGU(prefix)$gu` EXACTLY (verified golden across `k` and `byID`/`threshold`). So `gvaConvergence` calls `calcA` ONCE on the `nMax` gene drop and aggregates nested prefixes ‚Äì Finding 7‚Äôs ‚Äúfactor `rare` out of `calcA`‚Äù is satisfied by calling `calcA` once INSIDE the new function, not by changing `calcA`‚Äôs signature. The deterministic ranking scaffold (`kinship` -\> `meanKinship` -\> `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` -\> `scale`/zScores, `classifyParentage`, demographics, the \#76 predicate) is reproduced by ORCHESTRATING the same building blocks `reportGV` calls + reusing `orderReport` ‚Äì `reportGV`/`calcA`/`orderReport`/`geneDrop` themselves UNCHANGED (SAFEGUARDS: do not refactor the central function for a feature). The adversarial review verified `buildOrder`‚Äôs order is IDENTICAL to a `reportGV`-style ranking on the same `gu`, so the measured ‚Äúselection order‚Äù is the REAL one. Net: a purely ADDITIVE slice (new `R/` file + new test file + export + `man`), zero regression risk. **(3) RED TESTS FOR A SEED-VARYING DIAGNOSTIC ASSERT ROBUST INEQUALITIES, NOT THE EXACT VALUE.** `recommendedIter` varies 200-800 by seed, so pinning `recN == 800` would be cross-R-version-flaky (violates the TDD ‚Äúdeterministic‚Äù rule). The load-bearing assertion is DISCRIMINATION (the anti-tautology guarantee): fixture `recommendedIter` is FINITE and `>` qcPed‚Äôs grid floor, the fixture‚Äôs smallest-`N` order is unstable (`topOverlap < oMin`), and qcPed is `overlap == 1` AND `tau == 1` at every `N` (no rankable `gu` signal -\> deterministic mean-kinship order). Pair with determinism (same seed -\> byte-identical curve) and the recommendation-logic test (smallest `N` meeting BOTH criteria; every earlier `N` fails one). **(4) A POST-GREEN ADVERSARIAL REVIEW (ultracode) EARNS ITS KEEP ON THE UNSPECIFIED INPUT SPACE.** A fresh-agent skeptic, told to RUN R and find bugs, confirmed the primary path correct (half-split math, row alignment, `buildOrder == reportGV` order, `nRankable`/`nUndetermined`, determinism, `k>nRankable` clamp, empty-grid guard) but caught one real latent defect the strict-TDD suite missed: an UPPER-bound-only `grid` filter let a user-supplied `N=0` through -\> `colsB = (0+1L):(2L*0) = 1:0 = c(1, 0)` (R‚Äôs `:` REVERSAL, which is NOT an empty range) plus a `2*0` divide-by-zero -\> a silent `NaN` ‚Äúgarbage row‚Äù in the curve. The `(N+1):(2N)` reversal at `N=0` is the classic R `:` trap; guard with a positive-integer lower bound (`grid[grid >= 1L & 2L * grid <= nMax]`). Fixed via a gated hardening MICRO-CYCLE (one RED test for the guard -\> one-line GREEN -\> re-verify), which is the protocol-clean way to fold a verification-found defect into an in-flight slice. General rule: a TDD suite proves the SPECIFIED behavior; an adversarial review of the UNSPECIFIED input space (0 / negative / empty / oversize params) is complementary and cheap, and finding bugs in YOUR OWN deliverable during verification is correct, not scope creep. (S199, implement Slice 2 of issue \#2 ‚Äì gvaConvergence)

#### Learning 186 ‚Äì The Learning-184 GitHub auto-close trap is fully PREVENTABLE with a one-line proactive grep guard run BEFORE `gh pr create` (and before any commit message that lands on the default branch), turning Learning 184 from a reactive ‚Äúcatch-and-reopen after merge‚Äù control into a ‚Äúit never fires‚Äù preventive one. Publishing Slice 2 of issue \#2 (PR \#84). S198 auto-closed \#2 TWICE (a negated clause in the PR body, then a close-out commit message that quoted the token); S200 published the very NEXT slice with ZERO auto-close incidents by **writing the PR body to a scratch file and scanning it first**: `grep -niE '(clos|fix|resolv)[a-z]*[[:space:]]+#[0-9]' pr_body.md` ‚Äì expect NO match before creating the PR; the same scan applies to any commit message destined for `master`. Concrete practices that made this a clean publish: (1) **author the PR body in a file, not an inline `--body` string**, so it is greppable and reviewable before it goes outward (and pass it with `--body-file`); (2) **keep BOTH controls** ‚Äì the preventive grep AND the standing immediate post-merge `gh issue view N --json state` re-query (the backstop confirmed \#2 stayed OPEN; do not drop it just because the grep passed); (3) **to say a PR does NOT close an issue, restructure so a closing keyword never abuts a number** ‚Äì S200‚Äôs body referenced the issue only as ‚ÄúPart of \#2‚Äù and described Slice 3 as ‚Äúthe remaining slice‚Äù, never ‚Äúcloses \#2‚Äù; even `#76` appeared only after the non-keyword word ‚Äúissue‚Äù. Also reaffirmed for a clean publish session (a faithful repeat of S198‚Äôs recipe, minus the trap): a publish/docs session is **SOLO** (a serial irreversible git sequence ‚Äì a workflow adds risk, not coverage; held under ultracode), TDD code-phases are **N/A** (the code was written + tested under TDD in the prior implementation session), the build-equivalent for publish is **the CI `R CMD check` matrix** (√ó5 OS/version) PLUS a **firsthand on-`master`-after-reset smoke** that the merged symbol is exported AND runs (not just ‚ÄúCI was green‚Äù), and the close-out is **pushed to origin/master FF** (\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]). (S200, publish Slice 2 of issue \#2 ‚Äì gvaConvergence)

#### Learning 187 ‚Äì A ‚Äúfix EVERY stale X‚Äù deliverable‚Äôs completeness check must grep ALL tracked file extensions (use `git grep` with NO `--include` filter), because even a RATIFIED plan‚Äôs own evidence inventory can have a blind spot ‚Äì and an adversarial completeness workflow catches multi-surface inconsistencies a single author misses. Implementing issue \#2 Slice 3 (align the gene-drop default `5000 -> 1000L` + reconcile every stale ‚Äú5000‚Äù doc + the `gvaConvergence()` vignette). The plan‚Äôs mandated ‚Äú84-hit 2D inventory‚Äù (the evidence grep that planning sessions are required to run) had **omitted `*.qmd`**, so a stale `| guIter | 5000L |` default claim in the tracked pkgdown article `vignettes/articles/genetic-value-analysis.qmd:193` was NOT in the inventory ‚Äì and my OWN first-pass grep repeated the omission (I used an explicit `--include=*.R,*.Rmd,*.Rd,*.html,*.md,*.yml` list). It was caught only by the DONE-criterion completeness grep run over `R/ man/ inst/ vignettes/` with no extension filter, then re-confirmed by a 3-agent adversarial completeness audit. **Four load-bearing techniques:**

**(1) Completeness grep = `git grep` over ALL tracked files, no
`--include`.** For ‚Äúreconcile every stale X,‚Äù an `--include` allow-list
is a foot-gun: the one extension you forget (`.qmd` in `articles/`,
`.yml`) is exactly where a stale claim hides. `git grep` (tracked-only)
also auto-excludes untracked local build artifacts
(`vignettes/*.html`/`*.md` renders are NOT tracked here ‚Äì they showed
stale ‚Äú5000‚Äù but are regenerated, not shipped). Then CLASSIFY every hit
into {must-change / factual-leave / out-of-scope}; a hit is not
automatically a fix. **(2) The classification needs an OWNER scope gate,
because ‚Äústale 5000‚Äù has a boundary.** Pre-RED, split the 5000 sites
into: the reportGV/geneDrop DEFAULT (change to 1000); factual DATA
PROVENANCE (`lacy1989PedAlleles` genuinely HAS 5000 columns; editing it
would FALSIFY the data description ‚Äì leave); and deferred-`fg` machinery
(`calcFG`/`calcFEFG`/`assignAlleles`; `fg` SE is issue \#82 ‚Äì leave,
incl.¬Ý`assignAlleles`‚Äôs pre-existing ‚ÄúDefault is 5000‚Äù doc bug where `n`
has NO default at all). Posed this as an `AskUserQuestion` (‚Äúhow wide
should the net go?‚Äù); owner chose ‚ÄúCore + reconcile guidance.‚Äù A
factual-provenance number is NOT a stale default and must not be swept
up. **(3) A default-VALUE change is cleanly strict-TDD-able and
low-blast-radius.** RED =
`expect_identical(eval(formals(reportGV)$guIter), 1000L)` (the contract)
PLUS a behavioral EFFECT test (a default-`n`
[`geneDrop()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/geneDrop.md)
run yields exactly 1000 `^V[0-9]+$` columns) ‚Äì both deterministic, both
fail at 5000 for the right reason. GREEN = the two-char value change +
the coupled ‚ÄúDefault is 5000‚Äù roxygen + `document()` (man regen is
REQUIRED or `R CMD check` flags a usage/signature mismatch ‚Äì it is part
of GREEN, not optional). Blast radius is bounded by grepping FIRST that
every caller passes the value explicitly: here every
`reportGV`/`geneDrop` test call passes an explicit `guIter`/`n`, the
only internal callers (`reportGV.R:142`, `gvaConvergence.R:158`) pass
`n` explicitly, and the shipped example data is STORED at its build-time
count (not regenerated by a default change) ‚Äì so the change broke zero
value-pinned tests. Mock-signature stubs that mirror the real signature
(`local_mocked_bindings(reportGV = function(..., guIter = 5000L, ...))`)
are doc-sync, not behavior ‚Äì update for consistency, but they assert
nothing. **(4) An adversarial completeness workflow earns its keep on a
multi-SURFACE doc reconciliation.** A 3-agent audit (exhaustive
iteration-grep / doc-surface consistency / new-vignette correctness)
caught what the single-author pass missed: the `.qmd` default site; a
RANGE contradiction (‚ÄúA range of 2 to 100,000 simulations‚Äù in
`gvAndBgDesc.html` + `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd` vs the modular UI‚Äôs
actual 100-10,000 at `modGeneticValue.R:38` ‚Äì a different inconsistency
AXIS than the default, but squarely within the DONE criterion ‚Äútell one
consistent story,‚Äù and code-grounded to fix); and a drift-prone vignette
claim (prose tied to a MARGINAL build-time result ‚Äì
`recommendedIter=800` only because `rankAgreement` there was 0.952,
barely over the 0.95 threshold). **The vignette robustness rule:** a
worked-example vignette whose numbers come from a seeded RNG must keep
its PROSE qualitative (let the build-time table show the specifics) AND
give the illustrated phenomenon a safety MARGIN ‚Äì here, widen the grid
(nMax=3000, N up to 1500) so convergence is SUSTAINED across the top
cells, so a future RNG shift that nudges one cell below threshold cannot
flip `converged` to FALSE and contradict the text. Finding these in your
OWN in-flight deliverable during verification is correct, not scope
creep (cf.¬ÝLearning 185(4)). (S201, implement Slice 3 of issue \#2 ‚Äì
default 5000-\>1000 + doc reconciliation + vignette)

#### Learning 188 ‚Äì When publishing a change whose new value ALREADY appears in a RELEASED NEWS section but at a NARROWER scope, do NOT edit the released line ‚Äì add a NEW dev-version bullet, and let the owner decide whether to document it at all. Publishing issue \#2 Slice 3, which changed the `reportGV()`/`geneDrop()` FUNCTION default `5000 -> 1000`. The only existing ‚Äú1000‚Äù mention in NEWS sat at `NEWS.Rmd:275` INSIDE the released `2.0.0 (20260618)` section, and it documented the Shiny **GVA tab** default (UI parity), NOT the function signature ‚Äì a narrower scope. Two rules. **(1) Released NEWS sections are immutable history** ‚Äì you never rewrite a shipped release‚Äôs notes to retro-document a later change; the function-default change is unreleased work, so it gets a NEW bullet under ‚Äú# nprcgenekeepr (development version)‚Äù -\> Changes (append-don‚Äôt-rewrite, Learning 171). **(2) The UI-tab claim and the function-signature claim are genuinely distinct user-facing facts** ‚Äì a caller of `reportGV(ped)` got 5000 iterations before this slice and 1000 after, so the new bullet is NOT redundant with line 275; the released line stays untouched. Whether to add the bullet at all is the OWNER‚Äôs call (user-facing release-notes content), posed as a single `AskUserQuestion` (Changes-bullet-only / +vignette-doc-bullet / none); owner chose Changes-bullet-only. Everything else was a clean repeat of the S200 publish recipe: the Learning-186 grep guard confirmed `Closes #2` was the SOLE closing reference (other refs `#82`/`#84` carried no keyword), the GraphQL `closingIssuesReferences` query independently confirmed the PR would close ONLY \#2, the post-merge `gh issue view 2` re-query confirmed it CLOSED, watch + fresh re-query (157b), owner-gated merge, ancestor-gated `reset --hard` + verified-merged-before-delete (146), close-out pushed FF. **General rule:** before editing any NEWS line that already mentions your value, check WHICH version section it lives in and WHAT scope it claims ‚Äì a released-section / narrower-scope match means a new dev bullet, not an edit. (S202, publish Slice 3 of issue \#2 ‚Äì the merge that CLOSED \#2)

#### Learning 189 ‚Äì When PLANNING a ‚Äúreport the sampling SE of a NONLINEAR statistic‚Äù task, the math derivation is the load-bearing risk, and the highest-ROI grounding is an independent derivation panel + an adversarial reconciler that actually RUNS the language (finite-difference gradient check against the authoritative function + an empirical multi-seed validation) ‚Äì it both settles the math AND surfaces adjacent defects a desk derivation never would. Planning issue \#82 (`fg` = founder genome equivalents sampling SE; `FG = 1/sum(p^2/r)`, `p` deterministic, only `r` = gene-drop retention mean stochastic).
