The S203 workflow (`wf_8672ccdd-2bf`, 8 agents: inventory + `calcGUSE` wiring trace + tests/fixtures + current-surfacing + a 3-WAY INDEPENDENT derivation panel + a reconciler) produced, with high confidence: the gradient `dFG/dr_f = FG^2*p_f^2/r_f^2` (the reconciler verified it by finite differences vs `calcFG` on `lacy1989Ped`, agreeing to 5 decimals) and the recommended **influence/score-form** delta estimator `fgSE = sd(crossprod(g, R))/sqrt(K)` (algebraically the full-covariance sandwich but `O(K*F)`, never forms the F x F matrix, folds in the required within-iteration founder covariance), empirically validated (agreement ratio 1.020, coverage 0.953, 1/sqrt(K) scaling 1.94). **Five findings the math work surfaced that a single-author desk pass would likely have missed:** (1) a **latent silent-collapse BUG** in `calcFG`/`calcFEFG` ‚Äì a contributing founder with `r_f=0,p_f>0` makes `p^2/0 = +Inf`, and `na.rm=TRUE` strips only `NaN` NOT `Inf`, so `sum(...)=Inf` and `FG` silently returns 0; materially likelier at the new K=1000 default than the old 5000 (so a planning session can DISCOVER a correctness bug in code adjacent to its feature ‚Äì record it as a ratification decision, do not drive-by fix it); (2) a naive **per-iteration `FG_k` is degenerate** (a single lost founder allele per column -\> `Inf` -\> `FG_k=0`, collapsing essentially every iteration) ‚Äì NEVER batch single-column FGs; the linearization works because it divides by the K-mean `r-bar_f`, bounded away from 0; (3) the within-iteration founder covariance is **MATERIAL** (~46% on a deep 20-founder pedigree vs only ~3% on the 3-founder `lacy1989`) ‚Äì so the diagonal/independence approximation is not defensible AND `lacy1989` is too small to validate it; the validation slice MUST use a real deep pedigree (the same ‚Äúno bundled fixture can validate this‚Äù lesson as Learning 182/185, now for `fg`); (4) `FG` is a colony-level **SCALAR**, so its SE is ONE number ‚Äì the `guSE` wiring mirror DROPS several integration points (no `orderReport` ride-through, no per-animal report column, no issue-#76 zeroing, no `max()` reduction) ‚Äì mirror a precedent in SPIRIT, not blindly point-for-point; (5) the degeneracy path (the `1/r_f^2` pole) has **no fast deterministic fixture** in the package -\> the plan must CRAFT a tiny allele table directly (the `calcGUSE`/`ped1Alleles` pattern), not rely on a slow seeded `geneDrop` of `examplePedigree`. **Concrete reusable gotcha:** `git grep -niE '\bfg\b'` returns ZERO hits because POSIX ERE treats `\b` as a backspace, not a word boundary ‚Äì for any 2-letter token use `git grep -niwE 'fg'` (the `-w` word flag); a planning inventory that trusts the bare `\b` result would be empty and wrong (compounds Learning 187‚Äôs ‚Äúgrep ALL extensions‚Äù with ‚Äúuse the right boundary flag‚Äù). **Process discipline applied:** orient -\> owner picks ‚Äú#82‚Äù -\> light read-only grounding -\> a SEPARATE pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion` (planning vs implement-now; owner chose planning) -\> 1B stub BEFORE technical work -\> the plan IS the deliverable, decomposed as VERTICAL slices (compute -\> validate -\> surface, with validation as an explicit owner GATE before exposure, which is exactly the issue‚Äôs ‚Äúvalidate before exposing‚Äù ask) each with completion criteria + verification commands + a STOP, an evidence-based inventory with <file:line> (all surfacing line numbers re-verified FIRSTHAND before commit, FM \#11/#20), a ‚Äúhere be dragons‚Äù list, and an owner-ratification checklist (D1-D6) ‚Äì then CLOSE OUT; do not start Slice 1 (FM \#18, planning-to-implementation bleed). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (the ARCHITECTURE/planning workstream + the ratified `issue2-...-plan.md` format), \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (the \#2 plan D6 was the spec ‚Äì planned the delta), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] / \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\] (the scope gate + ASCII-only plan math), Learning 182 (validate against the authoritative function + no-bundled-fixture-can-validate), Learning 183 (the column-doubling K-\>2K deterministic shrink test reused for `fgSE`). **General rule:** for a planning session whose core is a derivation, do not just describe the approach ‚Äì spend agents to derive it three ways, reconcile adversarially, and EXECUTE the verification (run the math), because a wrong derivation ratified into the plan misleads every implementer after you, and the act of running it is what finds the adjacent bugs and the fixture gaps. (S203, plan issue \#82 ‚Äì `fg` sampling SE)

#### Learning 190 ‚Äì A ratification session is not a rubber stamp: adversarially re-verify the recommendations against source BEFORE posing them, then fold each chosen option back into the plan ‚Äì and reconcile any text-vs-pseudocode contradiction the choice exposes. Ratifying the issue \#82 plan (`docs/planning/issue82-fg-se-plan.md`, D1-D6 + slice plan). The plan was already strong (S203 did a 3-way derivation panel + an adversarial reconciler that ran R), so the temptation is to just forward its six recommendations to the owner and collect picks. Instead: (1) **re-grounded the load-bearing facts FIRSTHAND** (FM \#11/#20) ‚Äì read `calcFG.R:61`/`calcFEFG.R:52` and CONFIRMED the silent-collapse bug is real (`p^2/0=Inf`; `na.rm=TRUE` strips `NaN` not `Inf` -\> `S=Inf` -\> `FG=1/Inf=0`), read `calcRetention.R:43` and confirmed the id-sorted-`r` vs position-`/` misalignment, read `calcGUSE.R` as the precedent; (2) ran a focused **3-agent adversarial re-verification** (one pair of decisions each, agentType `Explore`, structured verdicts) instructed to TRY to find a flaw or a tradeoff the owner is not being shown ‚Äì all six verdicts came back ‚Äúsound,‚Äù but two genuinely useful catches surfaced. **The key catch:** D4‚Äôs recommendation text said HARD-FAIL (return `NA`+warning when `any(p>0 & r==0)`) but the Section 2.5 recipe PSEUDOCODE (`keep <- rhat > 0`) silently implemented SOFT-SUCCESS (drop the unretained founder, report `FG`/SE on the rest) ‚Äì a material behavior difference (false-confidence protection vs information recovery, and the reported FG refers to a DIFFERENT founder set) that the plan never surfaced as a decision. So I posed it as the explicit owner choice; owner chose hard-fail; I then **reconciled the recipe** (inserted an explicit pre-`keep` guard `if (any(p>0 & rhat==0)) { warning(...); return(NA_real_) }`) so the pseudocode the Slice 1 executor follows now MATCHES the ratified intent and Section 2.6 case 3 ‚Äì otherwise the executor would have coded soft-success from the recipe while believing they implemented the ratified hard-fail. The second catch: D6 ‚Äúreconcile the parallel surfaces‚Äù was scope-ambiguous (does it include the longer-form `gvAndBgDesc.html` + the GVA vignette?) ‚Äì posed as a 3-way scope choice; owner chose broadest. **Recording the deliverable:** the ratification IS the deliverable, so it gets written INTO the plan, not just stated in chat ‚Äì a new ‚Äú### 5.1 Ratified outcomes (Session 204)‚Äù block with each decision‚Äôs chosen option, the Section 9 checklist boxes checked with the choices, the Status line flipped to RATIFIED, and the affected slice text (the recipe + the Slice 3 doc list) edited to the ratified behavior ‚Äì so the plan is now self-consistent and the executor reads choices, not open questions. **Question design:** 4 `AskUserQuestion`s covered all 6 decisions + the slice-count confirmation by bundling the pure-accept items (D1+D5+slices) into one and reserving slots for the genuine forks (D2/D4 coupled, D3 format, D6 scope); recommended-option-first, plain ASCII (`+/-` not the unicode sign, `->` not arrows), plain language (\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] ‚Äì decisions are the owner‚Äôs, I present + verify, owner picks). Close-out pushed FF (\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]). NOT an implementation session (FM \#18) ‚Äì Slice 1 is separate; Phase-3E runtime smoke is N/A (markdown plan, no runtime change). **General rule:** when the deliverable is ‚Äúratify a plan,‚Äù the value you add over forwarding the recommendations is (a) re-verifying them against source so the owner ratifies sound defaults, and (b) catching where the owner‚Äôs chosen option now contradicts something ELSE in the plan (a recipe, a downstream slice, a scope list) and editing those to match ‚Äì a ratified plan that still contains the pre-ratification contradiction will mislead the executor exactly as a wrong derivation would. (S204, ratify the issue \#82 plan D1-D6)

#### Learning 191 ‚Äì When a code slice adds a name-aligned correctness guard ALONGSIDE an existing positional computation, an adversarial review of the IMPLEMENTATION (not just the math) must check the two for CONSISTENCY ‚Äì the mismatch is where a latent, already-shipped bug hides; and any ‚Äúcorrect‚Äù reference value you computed during PLANNING must be checked against what the SHIPPED function actually returns, because a divergence is a bug, not a fixture footnote. Implementing Slice 1 of issue \#82 (`R/calcFGSE.R` = the `fg` sampling SE; plus the ratified D2 zero-retention guard folded into `calcFG`/`calcFEFG` via a shared `@noRd` `checkFgDegeneracy()`). Strict TDD held cleanly (RED 11 assertions failing for the right reason -\> GREEN -\> REFACTOR extract-the-guard; every transition `AskUserQuestion`-gated; check 0/0/0, suite 0/0, spell 0, golden master `lacy1989` FG=2.180096626 unchanged). The math core was correct and well-tested (an INDEPENDENT sandwich-form oracle `sqrt(g' (cov(t(R))/K) g)` agreeing with the implementation‚Äôs `O(K*F)` influence form `sd(crossprod(g,R))/sqrt(K)`; the oracle caught injected position/scaling bugs when probed). **The payoff was the ultracode adversarial review of the IMPLEMENTATION** (a general-purpose agent told to TRY to break the code, not re-derive the math): it confirmed `calcFGSE` correct, then found the real defect ‚Äì my new guard is name-aligned but `calcFG`/`calcFEFG`‚Äôs point-estimate formula `sum((fc$p^2)/r)` is still POSITIONAL, and `p` (getFounders/row order) and `r` (id-sorted via `tapply`/`rowsum`) are in DIFFERENT orders, so FG is silently WRONG on any unsorted-founder pedigree (Dragon D-3, but in the SHIPPED point estimate, not just the new code). Probed firsthand: `qcPed` ships **39.92** vs the correct **52.79**, and the wrong value is baked into `pedWithGenotypeReport`/`qcPedGvReport` (`fg=39.916464`); `examplePedigree`/`rhesusPedigree`/`pedWithGenotype` founders are all unsorted (affected), only `lacy1989`/`smallPed` are sorted (why golden master held and the suite ‚Äì which pins `fg` only as a column NAME, not a value, on the unsorted fixtures ‚Äì never caught it). **The meta-catch:** the issue \#82 PLAN itself recorded ‚ÄúqcPed FG~52.8‚Äù (the reconciler computed it via an ALIGNED recipe) while the shipped `calcFG` returns 39.92 ‚Äì the plan‚Äôs own correct value, sitting next to the shipped wrong value, was the unread smoking gun; nobody had run shipped-`calcFG`-vs-aligned-recipe side by side. **Scope discipline:** this is a pre-existing, MATERIAL bug whose fix needs regenerating shipped data objects (the bundled GV reports) + updating pins ‚Äì a blast radius outside Slice 1‚Äôs ratified scope (the D2 guard was narrowly the `p>0 & r==0` zero-retention path). So I did NOT fold it in (FM \#18/#25): posed the owner an `AskUserQuestion` (file-new-issue-and-finish vs expand-now), owner chose file-new-issue; filed **\#86** (with the qcPed evidence and the dependency that it MUST land before \#82 Slice 3 surfaces `FG +/- SE`, else a wrong FG pairs with a correct SE), fixed only the one doc-level item the review flagged (a misleading test comment), and closed out Slice 1. Slice 2‚Äôs validation gate (a real deep pedigree) would independently re-surface the `calcFG`-vs-`calcFGSE` inconsistency before any exposure. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT workstream + the `calcGUSE`/issue-#2 Slice-1 wiring precedent ‚Äì mirrored in SPIRIT: FG is a scalar so no per-animal column; note `calcGUSE` was itself never added to `_pkgdown.yml`, a pre-existing gap left as-is), \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (the ratified plan Section 5.1/6 was the spec ‚Äì implemented the delta), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] / \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\] (the phase gates + the scope-decision question), Learning 183 (the column-doubling `K->2K` deterministic shrink test `sqrt((K-1)/(2K-1))`, reused for `fgSE`), Learning 189 (the silent-collapse bug it predicted ‚Äì now guarded; AND its `git grep -niwE` 2-letter-token gotcha). **General rule:** an adversarial review earns its keep most when it checks not ‚Äúis the new thing correct‚Äù (the tests already do that) but ‚Äúis the new thing CONSISTENT with the old thing it sits beside‚Äù ‚Äì a correct new estimator bolted next to a quietly-wrong old estimator is a worse user experience than either alone, and the inconsistency is exactly what a test suite that pins the old value (or only its column name) will not see. (S205, implement issue \#82 Slice 1)

#### Learning 192 ‚Äì When a bug fix entails regenerating bundled/frozen GOLDEN DATA, the load-bearing pre-implementation work is the data-blast-radius + reproducibility audit, and the regenerated golden value must come from running the ACTUAL documented production recipe firsthand ‚Äì not a planning estimate or a sub-agent‚Äôs manual replica. Fixing issue \#86 (`calcFG`/`calcFEFG` divided `p` in getFounders/row order by `r` id-sorted, BY POSITION -\> wrong FG on unsorted-founder pedigrees; the one-line fix is `r <- r[names(fc$p)]`, the same alignment `calcFGSE`/`checkFgDegeneracy` already use). The CODE fix was trivial (two one-liners; golden master `lacy1989` FG=2.180096626 byte-unchanged; FE has no retention term, unaffected); the real work was everything around the bundled DATA. **(1) Blast-radius first (ultracode 4-agent workflow + firsthand spot-checks):** enumerate every bundled object embedding the wrong value (exactly 2 ‚Äì `qcPedGvReport`, `pedWithGenotypeReport`, both `$fg=39.9164644`), every test/doc that pins it (NONE in source ‚Äì only process-doc narrative; 0 tests break ‚Äì `test_reportGV.R` already pins the current full structure on FRESH output), and whether the frozen objects are STALE (they were ‚Äì predated `guSE`/`nMaleFounders`/`parentage`, so a documented-recipe regen refreshes structure too ‚Äì a scope fork I posed to the owner, who chose full regen). **(2) The near-miss that became the lesson:** the investigation agent computed the corrected `fg` via a MANUAL replica of `reportGV` and got 52.7546854; I baked that into the RED value-pin. But running the ACTUAL documented recipe (`set_seed(10); reportGV(qcPed, guIter=10000)`) under the fixed code gave **52.7641277** ‚Äì the agent‚Äôs replica had not perfectly reproduced reportGV‚Äôs RNG prefix (and `fg` also varies with `guIter`: ~52.778 at K=200, converging to 52.764 at K=10000; the issue‚Äôs ‚Äú52.79‚Äù was an illustrative K=1000 seed-1 figure, not the report‚Äôs K=10000 value). I caught it at GREEN (the regen produced a different number than my pin) and corrected the pin to ground truth. **General rule:** a frozen golden value belongs to whatever the documented production recipe ACTUALLY emits, run firsthand; never pin a planning estimate or a sub-agent reconstruction. Structure the test so the **bug-discriminating** assertion (`expect_gt(fg, 50)` ‚Äì fails on the buggy 39.92, passes on any correct value) is SEPARATE from the **exact-value documentation pin** (`expect_equal(fg, 52.7641277, tol=1e-6)`); the discriminator proves the fix even if the pin needs a number you only learn at GREEN. **(3) Verify the recipe‚Äôs seeding, don‚Äôt assume:** the recipe doc said `set_seed(10)` ‚Äì a real package wrapper (`R/set_seed.R`, `sample.kind="Rounding"` on R\>=3.6), NOT base `set.seed(10)` (`Rejection`); I almost ‚Äúfixed a typo.‚Äù Verified firsthand they yield the identical draw in this pipeline before trusting that my base-`set.seed` regen matched the doc. **(4) The fix closed a SECOND degeneracy path:** on the unsorted fixture the positional pairing landed a `p>0` contributor against a phantom `r=0` -\> FG silently 0 with NO warning ‚Äì which the name-aligned \#82 guard does NOT catch (it sees the truly-unretained founder has `p=0`); aligning `r` closes it. Strict TDD held (RED 6 assertions failing for the right reason -\> GREEN; no REFACTOR ‚Äì minimal one-liners; scope-decision + PRE-RED-\>RED + RED-\>GREEN all `AskUserQuestion`-gated). Scope discipline: did ONLY \#86 (no \#82 Slice 2/3, no drive-by cleanups ‚Äì FM \#18/#25). On branch `issue-82-fgse`, committed locally, not pushed (publishes with \#82). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT workstream; the documented `reportGV` data recipe in `R/data.R`), \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (S205‚Äôs handoff + the issue \#86 body were the spec ‚Äì did the delta, not a re-investigation), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] / \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\] (the gates + the two scope questions); Learning 191 (the bug this fixes ‚Äì S205‚Äôs review predicted it), Learning 189 (the silent-collapse family). (S206, fix issue \#86)

#### Learning 193 ‚Äì A ‚Äúvalidate a Monte Carlo SE before exposing it‚Äù gate (issue \#82 Slice 2) decomposes cleanly into strict-TDD-able PURE harness scorers (tested on tiny synthetic inputs with hand-computed answers) + a ONE-TIME recorded study (a build-ignored `data-raw/` runner) + a STATIC report; the load-bearing design choice is picking the validation pedigree by MEASURING which one actually exercises the failure mode, not by description ‚Äì and the harness‚Äôs SE form must be tied to the SHIPPED estimator by an equality test so the study validates what users actually get. Implementing Slice 2 of issue \#82 (the ‚Äúvalidate before expose‚Äù gate for `calcFGSE()`, the `fg` sampling SE). The gate PASSED on both pedigrees (all seven checks), clearing Slice 3 to surface `FG +/- SE`. **(1) The shape that makes a slow stochastic validation strict-TDD-able.** Split the work into three layers: PURE SCORERS (`fgSEAgreementRatio = mean(SE)/sd(FG)`, `fgSECoverage`, `fgSEScalingRatio`, `fgSEFullFromMatrix`/`fgSEDiagFromMatrix`, `fgSEBootstrapFromMatrix`, `fgSEVerdict`) ‚Äì each pure/deterministic and unit-tested on crafted inputs whose answer is hand-computed (e.g.¬Ýtwo orthogonal-centered retention rows give `cov=0` so full SE == diagonal SE == `sqrt(1/6)`); an ORCHESTRATOR (`fgSEValidate`) tested ONLY for shape + determinism on `lacy1989` at a tiny budget (it runs gene drops, so the full bands are NOT asserted in the suite ‚Äì that would be slow and flaky); and the SLOW B\>=300-seed study, which runs ONCE via a `data-raw/` runner (build-ignored, never in CI) and whose numbers are embedded STATICALLY in a pkgdown `vignettes/articles/*.qmd` report (renders without re-running). The harness functions live in `tests/testthat/helper-fgSEValidation.R` so the suite unit-tests them AND the runner sources them (one definition, DRY). Net: zero new `R/` source, zero API/golden-master change ‚Äì a purely additive validation-infra slice. The verdict LOGIC is tested by feeding `fgSEVerdict` synthetic in-band/out-of-band summaries (PASS vs FAIL per check), so the ratified bands are exercised without the slow run. **(2) MEASURE which pedigree exercises the failure mode ‚Äì do not trust the plan‚Äôs menu.** The plan offered ‚ÄúexamplePedigree OR qcPed‚Äù as the real deep pedigree. A 3-line firsthand probe settled it decisively: per-seed cost (lacy 0.03s / qcPed 0.12s / examplePed-trimmed 0.40s -\> B=300 at K=1000+4K is ~11 min, feasible in-session) AND ‚Äì the load-bearing signature ‚Äì the off-diagonal/bottleneck profile: `examplePedigree` full/diag SE = 0.692 with min `r` = 0.013 (5 founders r\<0.10) vs `qcPed` full/diag = 1.013 with min `r` = 0.50 (none low). qcPed is WELL-RETAINED with negligible founder covariance, so it CANNOT validate the very thing the gate exists for (Dragon D-5: validating on a well-retained pedigree falsely blesses the diagonal/independence approximation). So the real-ped choice is settled by evidence, and I did NOT pose it as an owner question ‚Äì the data dictates `examplePedigree` (the deep bottlenecked one) + `lacy1989` (the fast deterministic anchor). **The headline result confirms it:** on `examplePedigree` the diagonal approximation OVERESTIMATES the SE by ~45% (full/diag 0.692; `0.0945/0.0654`) ‚Äì exactly Learning 189‚Äôs ~46% prediction ‚Äì vs 0.1% on lacy; lacy alone would have shipped the diagonal shortcut and overstated colony uncertainty by nearly half. The full influence form is mandatory, and a real deep pedigree is mandatory to prove it. **(3) Tie the harness‚Äôs SE to the SHIPPED estimator with an equality test.** `fgSEFullFromMatrix(R, p)` must equal `calcFGSE(ped, alleles)` to machine precision (a RED assertion on `lacy1989`), so the study measures what the package actually reports, not a re-implementation that could silently diverge ‚Äì the same ‚Äúvalidate against the authoritative function‚Äù discipline as Learning 182/191. **(4) Two FP/tooling gotchas this slice surfaced.** (a) A coverage-boundary test literal `fgSECoverage(11.96, 1, ref=10, z=1.96)` FAILS because `11.96 - 10` is `1.9600000000000009` in floating point, just outside `1.96` ‚Äì the inclusive-`<=` implementation is correct, the TEST picked a non-representable boundary; fix the test to exactly-representable values (`11.5`, `z=1.5`) that still discriminate `<=` from `<` (a test-construction bug corrected during GREEN, intent and implementation unchanged ‚Äì cf.¬ÝS206‚Äôs golden-value-at-GREEN). Separate the bug-discriminating assertion from the exact pin, as always. (b) In a Quarto `.qmd`, a backtick span beginning with `r` (e.g.¬Ýa backtick-r-space-`0.75` span) is parsed as INLINE R CODE and aborts the render ‚Äì put a bare `r` in its own code span; only `r` triggers this (a `K = 1000` span is safe). And edit `inst/WORDLIST` with `sort(x, method = "radix")` (C-locale/ASCII byte order, matching the file) NOT bare `sort()` (locale-aware collation) ‚Äì the latter re-orders the whole file into a 187-line diff instead of +3. **(5) Process:** the ~11-min study was run in the BACKGROUND (harness-tracked -\> re-invokes on completion, or sooner on error), so the session never blocked; the runner + its `.rds` + the article are all `.Rbuildignore`‚Äôd, so the slow study never touches `R CMD check`. Strict TDD held (RED 8 tests / 45 assertions failing for the right reason -\> GREEN; no REFACTOR ‚Äì the harness is cohesive; PRE-RED scope + PRE-RED-\>RED + RED-\>GREEN all `AskUserQuestion`-gated). Scope discipline: did ONLY Slice 2 (no Slice 3 surfacing ‚Äì FM \#18/#25). On branch `issue-82-fgse`, committed locally, not pushed (publishes with the whole \#82 branch). Verify: full suite 0/0, `devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0, `spell_check_package` 0, lint 0, article renders; Phase-3E runtime smoke N/A (no runtime/UI change ‚Äì `calcFGSE` is surfaced in Slice 3). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT workstream; the ratified plan Section 6 Slice 2 was the spec), \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (implemented the planned delta, did not re-derive), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] / \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\] (the scope + phase gates), Learning 189 (the ~46% off-diagonal materiality it predicted ‚Äì now MEASURED at ~45% on the real ped; the ‚Äúno bundled fixture / use a real deep pedigree‚Äù mandate), Learning 191 (validate-against-the-shipped-estimator), Learning 183 (the deterministic-SE testing mindset). **General rule:** for a ‚Äúvalidate before expose‚Äù gate, the validation is only as good as the pedigree it runs on ‚Äì measure the candidate‚Äôs failure-mode signature (here: off-diagonal covariance + thin-retention founders) and reject a fixture that looks adequate but structurally cannot exercise the thing under test; and make the harness measure the shipped function by construction, or the green report proves the wrong thing. (S207, implement issue \#82 Slice 2 ‚Äì fgSE validation gate)

#### Learning 194 ‚Äì When surfacing an additive companion value (`fgSE` beside `fg`) across N display surfaces, the load-bearing decisions are (a) guard EVERY surface on present-AND-finite so old/bundled objects and degenerate (NA) results degrade to the bare value, and (b) RESIST extracting a shared formatter when the surfaces‚Äô BARE-value formats already differ ‚Äì the dedup would force a behavior change. Plus: an additive return element breaks exact-name-pin structure tests (update them, they are the contract, not discriminators); and a UI ‚Äúruntime smoke‚Äù without a browser is faithfully done by driving the real render code paths (`testServer` `output$...`, the pure HTML/text functions) on a REAL `reportGV()` result. Implementing Slice 3 of issue \#82 ‚Äì surface `FG +/- SE` in `reportGV()` + the GV-tab summary, Summary-Statistics founder table, text `summary()`, and `makeFounderStatsTable` HTML, with the broad D6 guidance reconciliation. The `guSE` wiring (issue \#2 Slice 3) was the near-exact precedent and made this low-risk: thread `fgSE <- calcFGSE(ped, alleles)` from the SAME gene drop next to `fg = feFg$FG` (a colony SCALAR ‚Äì no per-animal `$report`/`$gu` column, plan F2), then display inline. **(1) The recurring shape: guard on present-AND-finite, degrade to bare.** Each of the four display sites is `if (!is.null(fgSE) && is.finite(fgSE)) sprintf("%.2f +/- %.2f", fg, fgSE) else <bare FG>`. The `&&` short-circuits NULL (older objects / the re-export of a NULL `fr$fgSE`) before `is.finite` (which is `logical(0)` on NULL and would error in `if`); `is.finite` also catches the ratified hard-fail `NA` (degenerate FG) -\> show bare FG, never `+/- NA`. The bundled `pedWithGenotypeReport`/`qcPedGvReport` (left UNCHANGED per owner ‚Äì additive, not regenerated) are exactly the absent-`fgSE` path, so the backward-compat tests run against real shipped objects. **(2) Do NOT extract a shared `fgWithSE()` helper here ‚Äì the bare formats diverge.** The `+/-` (with-SE) form is identical everywhere, but the BARE form is NOT: gvSummary/modSummaryStats use `sprintf("%.2f", fg)`, `summary()` uses `as.character(round(fg, 2L))` (e.g.¬Ý`52.7`, no trailing zero), and `makeFounderStatsTable` uses `as.character(round(fg,2))` AND a prior `is.na(fg) -> "N/A"` branch. A single sprintf-bare helper would change `summary()`/`makeFounderStatsTable` bare output from `22.3` to `22.30` (a behavior change, and it breaks the `grepl(">22.3<")` backward-compat pin). So the ~3-line guard is duplicated 4x ON PURPOSE; the cohesion is in the precedent, not a helper (cf.¬ÝS207‚Äôs same call). Without a helper, the `+/-` format-string trips `nonportable_path_linter` (the `/` looks like a path) at each site ‚Äì suppress with the project‚Äôs `# nolint: nonportable_path_linter.` (inline where the line stays \<=80; `# nolint start/end:` block on its own lines where indentation pushes the inline comment over 80, matching `getProductionStatus.R`). **(3) An additive return element breaks exact-name-pin tests ‚Äì update them as the contract.** Adding `fgSE` to `reportGV()`‚Äôs return made two `expect_named(gvReport, c("report",...,"fg","maleFounders",...))` blocks in `test_reportGV.R` fail; insert `"fgSE"` after `"fg"` to match the new (intended) structure. This is NOT weakening a discriminator ‚Äì it is recording the additive contract change (and `expect_named` is order-sensitive, so position matters). The integration test asserts PROPERTIES (`fgSE` present/numeric/length-1/finite/\>0 on `qcPed`; ABSENT from `$report`/`$gu`) not an exact value, because reportGV‚Äôs INTERNAL gene drop is not externally reconstructible (an external `set.seed(1); geneDrop(...)` gives DIFFERENT alleles ‚Äì reportGV consumes RNG before its gene drop ‚Äì so an external `calcFGSE` exact-match is impossible; `fg` finite+healthy on qcPed already implies `fgSE` finite+\>0 since retention varies). **(4) Headless UI runtime smoke = drive the real render paths on real data.** Phase-3E ‚Äúlaunch the app‚Äù is faithfully satisfied without a browser by running the ACTUAL render code: `testServer(modGeneticValueServer, ...)` with a real pedigree -\> `output$gvSummary` contains `+/-`; `testServer(modSummaryStatsServer, founderStats=reactive(list(...fgSE=...)))` -\> `output$summaryStats` founder cell contains `+/-`; and the pure `summary()`/`makeFounderStatsTable()` on a real `reportGV(qcPed)` result print `52.79 +/- 0.04`. That exercises the same code an app launch would, deterministically. **(5) D6 ‚Äúbroadest‚Äù doc scope, verified by reading not by the plan‚Äôs list.** Of the plan‚Äôs named doc surfaces, two (`gvAndBgDesc.html`, `_genetic_value_analysis.Rmd`) turned out to contain NO founder-genome-equivalents description at all (grep-confirmed) ‚Äì so ‚Äúreconcile every FG surface‚Äù meant editing the five that DO describe FG and recording that two have nothing to reconcile, not forcing an edit into them. A guidance-content RED test (`genetic_value.html` must mention ‚Äúfounder genome‚Äù) makes the in-app doc note a tested deliverable, mirroring the issue-#2 gu-guidance test. **(6) Tooling:** `devtools::document()` (run because `@return`/`@param` changed) also re-wrapped `man/calcGUSE.Rd` (pure whitespace ‚Äì the known local-roxygen artifact; `git checkout` it) and CORRECTED two stale data-doc `@format` lines (`pedWithGenotypeReport.Rd`/`qcPedGvReport.Rd`: `GVnprcmanag`-\>`nprcgenekeeprGV`, length `8`-\>`10`) left stale by S206‚Äôs data regen ‚Äì accurate auto-output, kept (reverting just re-dirties on the next `document()`). Quarto render of an article leaves `*.html` + `*_files/` + an auto `.gitignore` ‚Äì delete them (build artifacts, not deliverables). Strict TDD held (RED across 5 files, every new test failing for the right reason ‚Äì `fgSE` length 0 / no `+/-` / `genetic_value.html` lacks ‚Äúfounder genome‚Äù; GREEN; no REFACTOR; PRE-RED scope + PRE-RED-\>RED + RED-\>GREEN all `AskUserQuestion`-gated). Scope discipline: did ONLY Slice 3 (no NEWS bullet ‚Äì folds into the publish session, Learning 157a; no bundled-data regen ‚Äì owner pick; FM \#18/#25). On branch `issue-82-fgse`, committed locally, not pushed (publishes with the whole \#82 branch). Verify: full suite 0/0, `devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0, `spell_check_package` 0, lintr clean on touched lines, GVA article renders, Phase-3E PASSED. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT workstream; the `guSE`/issue-#2 Slice-3 wiring precedent; the ratified plan Section 6 Slice 3 + D3/D6 were the spec), \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (implemented the planned delta), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] / \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\] (the scope + phase gates; ASCII `+/-`), Learning 191 (the `calcFG` order bug \#86, now fixed, so a correct FG pairs with this SE), Learning 193 (Slice 2 calibrated the SE this surfaces). **General rule:** surfacing an additive companion value is mostly the `guSE`-style wiring, but the two judgment calls are (a) guard present-AND-finite at every site so absent/NA degrade to the bare value, and (b) DON‚ÄôT DRY the format when the bare paths already differ ‚Äì the duplication preserves behavior that a shared formatter would silently change. (S208, implement issue \#82 Slice 3 ‚Äì surface FG +/- SE)

#### Learning 195 ‚Äì Publishing a multi-slice branch where the OWNER merges: re-run the FULL gate on the branch TIP (not a stale slice‚Äôs gate), put a closing keyword in the PR BODY for EVERY bundled issue and VERIFY GitHub linked them, and advise a merge commit (not squash) so the slice commits survive. Publishing the whole `issue-82-fgse` branch (Slice 1 `calcFGSE`+guard / \#86 founder name-align / Slice 2 validation gate / Slice 3 surface `FG +/- SE`) as **PR \#87 -\> master**; owner chose ‚Äúopen the PR, you merge it‚Äù + ‚Äúmerge commit, keep S205-S208‚Äù at the publish gate. **(1) The merge gate is the TIP, not the last slice.** Each slice ran its own `devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)`=0/0/0; but this session adds the deferred `NEWS.md` bullets (Learning 157a), which changes the tree, so the AUTHORITATIVE pre-push gate is re-running the full set on the FINAL commit ‚Äì `check` 0/0/0 + clean-regression suite read (`test_dir`, `sum(failed)`/`sum(error)`, true offenders excl. `test-app-`/`test-e2e-` = 0) + `spell_check_package(".")`=0. Don‚Äôt treat S208‚Äôs green as the merge gate. (Ran both in the background while the owner answered the publish-method `AskUserQuestion` ‚Äì gate green by the time they replied.) NEWS.md is NOT covered by the package‚Äôs `spelling.R` test by default (it checks Rd/vignettes/DESCRIPTION), so keep NEWS wording plain and rely on the explicit `spell_check_package` run; here `FG` was already in `inst/WORDLIST` and the run came back 0. **(2) Closing keywords ‚Äì the recurring auto-close trap, closed and VERIFIED.** Every prior slice commit named ‚Äúissue \#82‚Äù/‚Äú#86‚Äù with NO closing keyword on purpose (whole-branch publish, FM \#18), so both issues were still OPEN at publish time. Put one keyword PER issue in the PR BODY (`Closes #82` and `Closes #86` on their own lines ‚Äì a single `Closes #82 #86` only links the first). Then VERIFY GitHub actually parsed them: `gh pr view --json closingIssuesReferences` is NOT a field in the installed gh (it errors with the available-fields list), so confirm via GraphQL ‚Äì `gh api graphql -f query='{repository(owner,name){pullRequest(number:87){closingIssuesReferences(first:10){nodes{number title}}}}}'` returned BOTH \#82 and \#86. A keyword GitHub doesn‚Äôt link leaves the issue open after merge; verifying is cheap insurance against the trap the gotchas have warned about for many sessions. **(3) Owner-merges =\> the PR is the deliverable, the merge is not.** With ‚Äúopen PR, you merge it,‚Äù the session DELIVERABLE is a review-ready PR (build evidence + closing keywords + a ‚Äúuse Create a merge commit, not squash‚Äù note in the body so the four slice commits and their issue refs survive); \#82/#86 stay OPEN until the owner clicks merge, and master stays at `19e4b3d2` == origin/master (no local-ahead drift ‚Äì the close-out docs ride the PR branch to origin, satisfying \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\] without touching master). **(4) Keep the publish commits clean.** Commit only `NEWS.md` in the publish commit; the SESSION_NOTES 1B stub stays in the working tree and is replaced by the full handoff in the separate close-out commit (CHANGELOG + this learning + handoff), which is pushed to update the PR. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (whole-branch publish model FM \#18; the S202 PR-publish precedent; build-equivalent = `check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] (posed the merge-method + history-style choices as one `AskUserQuestion`, ASCII labels), \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **General rule:** a publish session‚Äôs job is to make the merge SAFE and ONE-CLICK for the owner ‚Äì re-gate the tip, wire and verify the closing keywords for every bundled issue, and tell the owner exactly which merge button preserves history. (S209, publish issue \#82 branch via PR \#87)

#### Learning 196 ‚Äì Before overwriting bundled/golden data, VERIFY the documented recipe reproduces the COMMITTED values in-memory FIRST; a ‚Äúpurely additive‚Äù regeneration can mask a pre-existing NON-reproducible save, and a coarse aggregate can hide a difference a continuous statistic reveals. Regenerating the two bundled GV reports (`qcPedGvReport`/`pedWithGenotypeReport`) so they carry `fgSE` (added to `reportGV()` in S208). I had told the owner the regen was ‚Äúpurely additive‚Äù ‚Äì `fg` would reproduce the committed `52.7641277` and only `fgSE` would be added. **It was not, and the verify-before-save guard caught it.** **(1) Compute-and-verify-against-the-committed-value BEFORE `save()`.** The regen script computed both reports in memory and compared `fg`/`fe` to the committed values before writing; `fg` came back `52.7546854`, NOT the committed `52.7641277` (a real 0.0094 gap, not FP noise ‚Äì `fe` matched to 8e-9). The guard REFUSED to save and surfaced the discrepancy instead of silently overwriting a golden value. Never `save()` frozen data first and check later; gate the save on reproducing the committed numbers. **(2) An isolated worktree AT THE SAVING COMMIT decisively assigns the cause.** To rule out ‚Äúa later change (S208‚Äôs additive `fgSE` line) shifted `fg`,‚Äù I ran the clean recipe at S206‚Äôs own code state: `git worktree add --detach <scratch> 83d8640d`, then `RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED=FALSE R_LIBS_USER=<main renv lib> Rscript ...` to borrow the main project‚Äôs library (a fresh worktree‚Äôs renv lib is empty -\> `pkgload` missing). It ALSO produced `52.7546854`. So the committed `52.7641277` was never reproducible from its own recipe ‚Äì a contaminated RNG-state save (the gene drop ran from a different RNG position than `set_seed(10)` immediately-before), not code drift. The worktree-at-the-saving-commit is THE diagnostic for ‚Äúdoes the committed data match the recipe at the time it was saved.‚Äù (Irony: `52.7546854` is the value S206‚Äôs sub-agent originally computed before S206 ‚Äúcorrected‚Äù it to the non-reproducible number. Learning 192‚Äôs ‚Äúrun the documented recipe firsthand‚Äù was right, but the firsthand run was itself contaminated ‚Äì the missing step was verifying the SAVED OBJECT reproduces, which this learning adds.) **(3) A coarse aggregate can hide a gene-drop difference a continuous statistic reveals ‚Äì diff old-vs-new to bound the blast radius.** Loading the OLD object from git (`git show HEAD:data/x.RData > tmp; load(tmp, envir=e)`) and comparing to the new showed `$report$gu`/`$rank`/`$value`, the `$gu` vector, and `$kinship` byte-IDENTICAL; ONLY `fg` changed (+`fgSE`). `gu` is reported as INTEGER percents ‚Äì too coarse to register the small clean-vs-contaminated gene-drop difference ‚Äì while `fg` (continuous) does. This old-vs-new diff is exactly what answered the owner‚Äôs ‚Äúare vignettes or `@examples` affected?‚Äù decisively: NO ‚Äì nothing any vignette/example reads from these objects changed, and no vignette/example reads `fg` from them anyway (the GVA article computes `fg`/`fgSE` LIVE on `examplePedigree`, not the bundled reports). **(4) `tools::resaveRdaFiles(\"data/\")` is a footgun ‚Äì scope it.** On a directory it recompresses EVERY `.RData` (here all 19), dirtying 17 unrelated files; pass the explicit file paths, and `git checkout -- data/` to undo the directory-wide churn before re-running scoped. **(5) A moved value is a PREMISE change ‚Äì re-surface it even mid-GREEN.** Because I had told the owner the regen was additive (`fg` unchanged), discovering `fg` actually moves to `52.7546854` (display `52.75`, breaking the \#86 pin and the `52.76` display patterns) is a scope/premise change, not a detail. I stopped GREEN, reported, and re-gated via `AskUserQuestion` (proceed to the reproducible value / investigate the contamination / abort) rather than quietly editing a \#86-established golden value. Then strict TDD: returned to RED to correct the value pins (`52.7641277 -> 52.7546854`, `52.76 -> 52.75`), confirmed they fail for the right reason (data still old), then GREEN saved. \#86‚Äôs name-alignment fix is INTACT ‚Äì only the precise saved number is corrected to its reproducible form. Verify: `check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0, full suite 0/0, `spell_check_package` 0, only the two `.RData` modified; filed issue \#88 for the incidental stale GVA `_freeze` (frozen output omits `fgSE`, predates S208). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT; build-equivalent `check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] (the owner‚Äôs ‚Äúregenerate‚Äù was given on an additive premise I had supplied and then had to correct ‚Äì re-surfaced the changed premise as a decision, did not assume), \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] / \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], Learning 192 (run the documented recipe firsthand ‚Äì EXTENDED here: also verify the saved object reproduces, since the firsthand run can itself be contaminated), Learning 194 (the additive-companion `fgSE` wiring this completes for the bundled objects). **General rule:** regenerating frozen data is not ‚Äúadditive‚Äù until proven ‚Äì verify the recipe reproduces the committed values in-memory before saving, attribute any mismatch with a worktree at the saving commit, diff old-vs-new to bound the blast radius (and to answer ‚Äúwhat downstream is affected‚Äù), and re-surface a moved value to the owner as a decision rather than silently correcting it. (S210, regenerate bundled GV reports to carry fgSE + correct non-reproducible fg)

#### Learning 197 ‚Äì A ‚Äústale cache / re-render the docs‚Äù issue may have ZERO published impact: verify the LIVE artifact and the CI build model BEFORE treating a local cache as a publication defect. Working issue \#88 (‚Äúthe GVA article‚Äôs Quarto `_freeze` is stale -\> the published article omits `fgSE`‚Äù). The issue premise was FALSE for the published site, and three checks proved it: (1) **curl the live page** ‚Äì `https://rmsharp.github.io/nprcgenekeepr/articles/genetic-value-analysis.html` already rendered `fe 109.67 / fg 47.62 / fgSE 0.29` (all three values); (2) **the CI build model** ‚Äì the freeze lives under gitignored `.quarto/` (`.gitignore:44`) and `pkgdown.yaml` does a clean `checkout@v4` with NO freeze-restore/cache step, so CI never sees a committed freeze and re-executes EVERY article fresh; (3) **the CI history** ‚Äì the pkgdown run on the S210 merge succeeded AFTER `fgSE` shipped (run 28264621260). **Root cause of the stale LOCAL freeze, and why it never reached publication:** the article loads `library(nprcgenekeepr)` (the INSTALLED package), and the local renv library held a stale 2.0.0 that predated `fgSE` (`renv::status` out-of-sync; `calcFGSE` not exported, `reportGV` body has no `fgSE`) -\> `gv$fgSE` was NULL at local freeze time; CI instead installs the current source via `local::.` (setup-r-dependencies) so its render carries `fgSE`. The stale freeze was therefore a purely local, gitignored artifact with no repo or published impact. **The correct "refresh":** regenerating the freeze IN PLACE locally would REPRODUCE the stale output (the installed package still lacks `fgSE`), so the right move is to CLEAR the gitignored cache (commits nothing) and let CI / a post-`install()` build regenerate it correctly ‚Äì not to run a heavy install+render or, worse, re-bake the stale value. **Sibling audit:** all 6 articles publish (HTTP 200); GVA was the only one with a live `fgSE`-dependent value (correct); `fg-se-validation` uses static recorded numbers (`data-raw/fgSEValidation-results.rds`) so it is unaffected by definition. **When to apply:** any "stale cache / re-render" or "the published docs are wrong" issue ‚Äì FIRST determine whether the cache is committed and whether CI consumes it; if CI rebuilds fresh from source, the PUBLISHED artifact is the source of truth ‚Äì verify it directly (curl the live page, check the deploy run) before assuming a defect or spending a session "fixing" a non-defect. Surface a wrong premise to the owner as a disposition decision (close-as-verified vs act) rather than mechanically executing the issue‚Äôs literal ask. Carried as applied: \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] (posed the premise-is-wrong finding + disposition via `AskUserQuestion`, did not silently close or blindly re-render), \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (verified the real published state instead of re-running a "fix"), \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\]. Extends Learning 196 (S210 filed \#88 from the local freeze JSON without checking the live site ‚Äì the inference outran the evidence). **This was NOT a TDD code-phase session** (verification + docs + issue close; phase N/A). (S211, verify+document+close issue \#88)

#### Learning 198 ‚Äì For a recurring inventory issue (one repeatedly re-audited, like \#37), the non-redundant deliverable is the DELTA at the current HEAD, not a fresh full audit ‚Äì and the cheapest reliable way to find the delta is to diff the export/symbol surface between the prior-audit HEAD and now, then re-run the documented reachability recompute and RECONCILE the count by SET, not by total. Auditing \#37 (‚Äúexported functions not used by the Shiny app‚Äù), already audited S65/S78/S97 (S97 declared the actionable surface fully drained). Per \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], started by reading the most recent audit doc (`docs/audits/ISSUE_37_UNUSED_EXPORTS_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md`) + the issue‚Äôs own documented method, then scoped the delta inline: `git diff a5507a35..HEAD -- NAMESPACE` -\> exactly 10 added `export()` lines, 0 removed; and a recompute of the issue‚Äôs reachability method (`codetools::findGlobals(merge = TRUE)` transitive closure seeded at `runModularApp`/`runGeneKeepR`/`appUI`/`appServer`, sourcing all 220 `R/*.R` into a throwaway env ‚Äì renv not materialized in-checkout, so `pkgload::load_all` fails and static sourcing is the correct equivalent). **(1) The headline insight: the composition drifts even when the count holds.** The unused count stayed 39 across the FOURTH re-verification (S65/S78/S97/S212; exports grew 155-\>176), but THIS time 4 exports moved ‚Äì `getPedigree`/`getPedDirectRelatives` graduated keep-as-public-API -\> USED (wired in *for free* by an unrelated file-pedigree-source refactor, the first \#37 export to graduate without a dedicated wire-in), and `gvaConvergence` (new vignette helper) + `makeGrpNum` (the \#29 rename alias, moved used-\>unused BY DESIGN) entered. `39 - 2 + 2 = 39`. So ‚Äúthe count is unchanged‚Äù is NOT evidence ‚Äúnothing moved‚Äù; reconcile the SETS (`setdiff` both directions) and explain every mover, or a real status change hides behind a stable total. The S97 method note flagged this risk; this session is the proof. **(2) Scope the work-list inline first, THEN fan out adversarial verification over JUST the delta.** Rather than re-verify all 176 exports, the NAMESPACE diff + recompute yielded a 13-export delta (10 new + 2 graduated + 1 alias); a 26-agent evidence-then-refute workflow (Explore agents: stage 1 trace the firsthand call path for ‚Äúused‚Äù or gather keep-evidence for ‚Äúunused‚Äù; stage 2 independently TRY to refute) covered exactly those 13. A deterministic static-reachability ‚Äúused‚Äù result is STILL worth one adversarial refute pass: 12/13 agreed, and the one refutation corrected a `loadSpeciesOverrides` downstream-consumer mis-attribution (right status USED ‚Äì it is called directly at `appServer.R:74` ‚Äì wrong claim about which module consumes the result). The recompute classifies; the workflow supplies citable call-path evidence + catches mis-attribution. **(3) Distinguish an intended status change from a regression.** `makeGrpNum` moved used-\>unused, which looks like a regression but is the deliberate \#29 rename (app now calls `makeGroupNum`; `makeGrpNum` is a genuine `.Deprecated("makeGroupNum")` wrapper at `R/makeGroupNum.R:32`, still exported as the soft-deprecated alias ‚Äì keep-as-public-API, future-retire only after its deprecation cycle, NOT a stale export and NOT a defect). Confirm such a mover firsthand (read the definition + grep the live callers) before labeling it. **(4) Read-only audit -\> issue close/update is OWNER judgment, not an auto-close** (S97 precedent): wrote the dated `docs/audits/...` report, recommended close-or-refresh-body, performed 0 issue-state changes. Disposition unchanged: 0 wire-in / 39 keep-as-public-API / 0 retire; `safeExecute` (0 callers ever) + `makeGrpNum` (post-deprecation-cycle) the only conditional future-retire candidates. Carried as applied: \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (did the delta, not a re-run), \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (the issue‚Äôs own documented reachability method is the spec; AUDIT_WORKSTREAM report structure), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] (recommended, did not close the issue), \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] (the deliverable-pick `AskUserQuestion`), \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends the S97 \#37 re-verification (same method, +1 method-confirmation: composition drifts under a stable count). **This was NOT a TDD code-phase session** (read-only audit; phase N/A each response). (S212, delta re-verification audit of issue \#37)

#### Learning 199 ‚Äì For an EMPTY-BODIED feature issue, the planning deliverable is a design doc whose spec is reconstructed from the title + owner Phase-1 decisions (ASKED, not invented), written to the project‚Äôs ACTUAL doc home, and ADVERSARIALLY VERIFIED before commit ‚Äì the verification pass over the freshly-drafted plan catches design flaws that firsthand drafting misses. Owner picked ‚Äú#13‚Äù (‚ÄúAssign kinship coefficients into the kinship coefficient matrix based on outside information‚Äù, filed 2020-11-20, **title only, empty body**). **(1) Empty body -\> ask, do not fabricate a spec.** Posed TWO owner decisions via `AskUserQuestion` ‚Äì deliverable shape (design doc / plan / thin slice) and core semantics (pair-level override / full-matrix replace / both) ‚Äì and the title + answers ARE the spec. Process-history scan (\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\]) found no prior \#13 work beyond the S62/S95 backlog audits‚Äô ‚Äúgenuinely open, large‚Äù tag. **(2) Consult project source of truth for the doc HOME, not just the content.** The base methodology‚Äôs `DESIGN_WORKSTREAM.md` is UI/layout-specific (star component, column balance, mobile views) ‚Äì the WRONG fit for a backend algorithm/feature; the project‚Äôs real convention is `docs/planning/issueN-<slug>-plan.md` (issue9/issue73 house style: status banner, firsthand evidence-based inventory, `Dn` decisions with options+recommendation flagged for ratification, vertical slices with RED/GREEN/DONE/Verify/Session-boundary/Dragons, consolidated dragons, ratification checklist). Drafting and ratification are SEPARATE sessions (FM \#18) ‚Äì this session drafts; a follow-up ratifies before Slice-1 RED. (\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] applies to WHERE a deliverable lives, not only how it reads.) **(3) Find the single chokepoint + reuse the in-repo pattern.** The kinship matrix from `kinship()` (`R/kinship.R:69`, custom Therneau/Lange, id-named base matrix) is the one object every genetic consumer reads, so a pair-level override is mechanically ‚Äúwrite `kmat[id1,id2]` + the symmetric twin‚Äù; the design is a leaf `applyKinshipOverrides` + validator threaded into `reportGV` as a `NULL`-default param ‚Äì the EXACT pattern `reportGV` already carries (`breedingTable`/`gestationTable` from \#73), with `kinship()` left untouched (6 callers; the \#9 blast-radius boundary). Three vertical slices (script core -\> Shiny upload -\> fallback paths + close), not horizontal (FM \#25). **(4) THE META-LEARNING: adversarially verify the PLAN‚Äôs design, not only its <file:line> inventory.** A 3-agent workflow (citation accuracy / consumer-inventory completeness / design red-team) over the DRAFT confirmed the core symmetric-REPLACE mechanism sound but caught FOUR real issues firsthand drafting missed, all folded in PRE-commit: (a) applying before `meanKinship` (`R/reportGV.R:124`) STACKS the override with the issue-#9 `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` `+sexMean/2` correction for the feature‚Äôs OWN primary use case (one-unknown-parent animals) ‚Äì I had framed the placement as a pure benefit (D11, the \#1 dragon, now a genetics-methodology decision for the owner); (b) `filterKinMatrix(probands, ...)` narrows `kmat` to the population subset, so a strict `stop()`-on-unknown-id leaf called directly by `reportGV` ABORTS the run for the canonical sire-dam case (sire/dam are usually ancestors, not probands) -\> soft warn-drop must live in `reportGV`, not only the file loader (D5); (c) a `[0,1]` kinship range is too loose ‚Äì off-diagonal kinship is `<= 0.5` for non-inbred pairs (Cauchy-Schwarz) and the common `r`(relatedness)-vs-`f`(kinship) data-entry confusion (r = 2f) passes `[0,1]` and silently corrupts the matrix (D6); (d) `convertRelationships`‚Äô `relation` label is pedigree-CEPH-derived, not value-derived, so a value override yields self-contradictory rows (`kinship=0.5` next to `relation="No Relation"`) ‚Äì narrow the Slice-3 claim. Re-verified the workflow‚Äôs NEW citations firsthand before writing them (FM \#11). **General rule:** for a feature whose design must reason about a heavily-documented ADJACENT feature (here \#9‚Äôs mean-kinship correction, documented at length in the very session notes I‚Äôd read), that interaction is exactly where firsthand drafting blind-spots hide ‚Äì a red-team pass over the draft is high-ROI and the plan is not ‚Äúdone‚Äù until it is verified, even when every inventory citation is correct. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] (the deliverable+semantics `AskUserQuestion`), \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends the issue9/issue73 planning-doc pattern. **This was a planning/design session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S213, design doc for issue \#13 kinship overrides from outside information)

#### Learning 200 ‚Äì RATIFYING a high-ceremony design plan: batch the low-stakes recommendations via `AskUserQuestion`, but GROUND the `/grill-me` on the \#1 load-bearing (genetics/algorithm) decision in a FIRSTHAND, EXECUTED analysis FIRST ‚Äì the analysis both makes the owner‚Äôs call defensible AND can correct a load-bearing mechanics claim the draft‚Äôs own red-team got wrong. This is the ‚Äúratification is a SEPARATE session‚Äù half of the issue9/issue73 pattern (Learning 199 named the drafting half). Owner picked ‚Äúratify the \#13 design draft,‚Äù then chose ‚Äúbatch recs, grill D11‚Äù at the process gate. **(1) Batch the low-stakes decisions, recommendation-first.** D1, D2, D4-D9 + slice order (10 items across 3 `AskUserQuestion` calls, each recommended option listed first and labeled ‚Äú(Recommended)‚Äù) ‚Äì the owner accepted every one as written (0 overrides). A ratification pass over a well-structured draft (each `Dn` already carries options + a recommendation + a ¬ß7 checklist, the issue9/issue73 house style) is fast precisely because the drafting session did that work; the batch just flips `[ ]` -\> `[x]` with the resolved outcome. **(2) For the \#1 dragon, verify firsthand and QUANTIFY before grilling ‚Äì do not grill on the draft‚Äôs prose.** D11 (override interaction with the issue-#9 `+sexMean/2` unknown-parent mean-kinship correction) is a genetics-methodology call; a 4-agent workflow (`wf_a3c184ee-92b`: correction mechanics + issue-9 S177 ratification rationale + an EXECUTED numeric model on real `qcPed` + a structured-output synthesis) ran BEFORE the grill. It quantified what the draft only asserted (‚Äústacks‚Äù): the stacking term IS `sexMean/2` ~= 1 SD of the colony distribution, ~8.4x the override‚Äôs own effect, flipping a worked headline animal GV rank \#6 -\> \#179 ‚Äì magnitude that makes ‚Äúdocument-and-accept‚Äù indefensible and forces the fix. A `/grill-me` grounded in an executed model lets the owner (geneticist) make a defensible call, exactly as issue-9‚Äôs own D2 was settled (S177). **(3) THE META-LEARNING: a ratification analysis can CORRECT the draft it ratifies ‚Äì even a claim the draft‚Äôs red-team produced.** S213‚Äôs D11 text (and its red-team) claimed the override ‚Äúpropagates into OTHER animals‚Äô corrections via their `sexMean` (uncorrected `original` snapshot).‚Äù FIRSTHAND-FALSE: the spurious `+sexMean/2` is written only to `corrected` (`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:175`) while `sexMean` reads the `original` snapshot (`:174`), so the double-count CANNOT cascade; only the override‚Äôs legitimate raw `Delta/N` (~0.017 SD) reaches peers. That correction de-risked the sub-decision (keep overridden animals as cohort peers ‚Äì nothing to fear). A red-teamed draft is not a verified draft; the ratification session re-verifies the load-bearing mechanics firsthand (FM \#11/#20), and the plan records the correction explicitly rather than silently. **(4) Grill the decision TREE, and offer the premise-REVERSING option.** Walked D11 one question at a time, each with a recommendation: top-level fix-vs-accept; then the supersede-vs-independent crux that determines whether blanket-suppression (A) is even RIGHT (a single off-diagonal override fixes one cell, while `+sexMean/2` corrects the whole-row scalar) ‚Äì offered ‚Äúkeep the prior / accept stacking‚Äù (which REVERSES Q1) so the owner consciously rejects it rather than only picking among ways to implement an assumption; then the cohort sub-decision; then the v1 scope edges (both-unknown promotion, shared-sib-pair coupling -\> document as limitations, FM \#25). Owner ratified blanket-A + keep-cohort-peers + document-edges + track-targeted-option-C-as-follow-up. **(5) Record every answer back INTO the doc (DRAFT -\> RATIFIED).** Status banner + a ratification-record paragraph; per-`Dn` `-> RATIFIED (S214)` tags; D11 fully rewritten (corrected mechanics + magnitude + grilled decision + the 4-part regression-test spec); Slice-1 scope/RED sharpened from ‚Äúthe ratified alternative‚Äù to the SPECIFIC blanket-A behavior so the executor has no interpretation gap; R11 + the ¬ß7 checklist flipped. **General rule:** a ratification session is not a rubber stamp ‚Äì batch the easy calls, but for any load-bearing genetics/algorithm decision verify the draft‚Äôs mechanics firsthand + quantify the magnitude + offer the premise-reversing option BEFORE the owner decides, then write the resolved spec back into the plan precisely enough that Slice-1 RED needs no further interpretation. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (the ratification HOME is the plan‚Äôs own ¬ß3 + ¬ß7, not a new doc), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] (posed every decision; assumed none), \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (grounded the grill in issue-9‚Äôs S177 ratification record), \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 199 (the S213 draft this ratifies). **This was a ratification/design session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S214, ratifying the issue \#13 kinship-overrides design plan; D11 settled via `/grill-me`)

#### Learning 201 ‚Äì IMPLEMENTING a ratified high-ceremony plan slice under strict TDD: an ‚Äúinterpretation-ready‚Äù plan makes RED authoring fast, but still VALIDATE every test fixture firsthand on the real data BEFORE freezing the RED tests, and let an INDEPENDENT recomputation (not the implementation) be the assertion backbone. Owner picked ‚Äúimplement \#13 Slice 1.‚Äù The S214-ratified plan‚Äôs ¬ß4 Slice 1 (scope/RED/GREEN/DONE/Verify/Dragons all reflecting the resolved D1-D11) was genuinely interpretation-ready, so RED was quick to draft ‚Äì but the high-ROI work was de-risking the fixtures and tests against `qcPed` FIRST. **(1) Validate the fixture on the real data before freezing the RED test.** The D11 4-part regression needs an animal that is BOTH a one-unknown-parent override target AND a valid cohort peer of another one-unknown animal. All 43 `qcPed` one-unknown animals are *sire*-missing, so every \#9 cohort is male-only ‚Äì the ‚Äúoverridden animal is still a peer‚Äù part is only exercisable if the override target is MALE. An executed scratchpad exploration found `X=0K7VJN` (male, one-unknown) sitting in `Z=K0ACWS`‚Äòs male cohort, and confirmed all four parts reproduce (X‚Äôs `+sexMean/2` suppressed -\> `corrected == original`; Z still corrected; X in Z‚Äôs cohort; suppress-vs-no-suppress differ ONLY at X), plus the legit raw colMeans flow to peers (Z rises 1.79e-05, the spurious term does NOT cascade). A RED fixture not checked against the data can assert an impossible condition (a female X could never be a male-cohort peer) and pass for the wrong reason. **(2) The independent base-R recomputation is the backbone.** Mirroring the existing issue-#9 Slice-2 test, the new `reportGV` test asserts the WHOLE `indivMeanKin` vector equals a base-R model that applies the override to `kmat`, recomputes `colMeans`, then runs the blanket-A correction ‚Äì so the test rests on the ALGORITHM, not on the implementation, and no-cascade falls out as ‚Äúdiffer-only-at-X.‚Äù **(3) Verify a test‚Äôs control is sound before freezing it.** Confirmed baseline `reportGV(qcPed, guIter=100)` emits ZERO warnings, so the warn-drop test‚Äôs bare `expect_warning()` cannot pass spuriously on some unrelated warning. **(4) When a ratified plan is internally inconsistent, the precise decision wins ‚Äì and say so.** The ¬ß4 RED bullet loosely wrote the validator ‚Äúrejects \> 0.5,‚Äù but the precise D6 text and the ¬ß7 checklist say the standalone validator WARNS (the matrix-aware leaf rejects via the exact `sqrt(diag_ii*diag_jj)` bound). Implemented per D6/¬ß7 (warn) and surfaced the reconciliation at the PRE-RED gate rather than silently choosing. **(5) Reuse the in-repo pattern; each function self-validates.** `checkKinshipOverrides` mirrors `checkGenotypeFile` (stop on structural/domain errors, warn on the soft range); `applyKinshipOverrides` is the strict leaf that also re-validates its own input ‚Äì a deliberate robust-public-API choice (cheap on a tiny frame), so `reportGV` validates once for the warn-drop and the leaf validates again; offered as REFACTOR, owner chose skip (a noted optional tidy, not a defect). `reportGV` threads only the SURVIVING overridden id-set into `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`, whose new `overriddenIds` guard `next`s at the TOP of the one-unknown loop (leaves the value at its override-influenced `colMeans`, never flags it, never removes it from others‚Äô cohorts). `kinship()` stayed untouched (D1). **(6) Close-out mechanics:** `NEWS.md` is GENERATED from `NEWS.Rmd` (edit the `.Rmd` source + `rmarkdown::render`, never hand-edit `NEWS.md`); make a `spell_check_package` flag on a common English word (‚Äúvalidator‚Äù) go away by REWORDING the prose, not by padding the shared `inst/WORDLIST` with a non-domain word. Phase-3E genuinely N/A (Slice 1 is script-level; the Shiny smoke is Slice 2‚Äôs gate) ‚Äì stated, not skipped. **General rule:** even when the plan removes all design ambiguity, the executor‚Äôs firsthand value-add is (a) proving each RED fixture is REALIZABLE on the actual data, (b) anchoring assertions to an implementation-independent model, and (c) reconciling any residual plan inconsistency toward the precise ratified decision, out loud. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] (surfaced the \#13-closed state + the D6 wording rather than acting silently), \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 199/200 (the draft + ratification this implements). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED -\> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S215, issue \#13 Slice 1 ‚Äì outside-information kinship overrides in `reportGV`)

#### Learning 202 ‚Äì WIRING a script-level feature into the Shiny app (the ‚Äúapp delivery‚Äù slice) under strict TDD: the new risks are (a) a soft/optional input ABORTING its mandatory consumer, (b) extending a function‚Äôs signature SILENTLY breaking the test doubles that mock it, and (c) doing an honest Phase-3E for Shiny wiring in a HEADLESS environment. Owner picked ‚Äúmerge PR \#89; reopen \#13 and continue the work‚Äù -\> the single development deliverable was Slice 2 (upload an outside-kinship CSV in the Genetic Value tab; D7). **(0) Do the owner-directed outward-facing admin carefully, not blindly.** ‚ÄúMerge PR \#89‚Äù is explicit authorization, but before merging I confirmed the full R-CMD-check matrix was green and SURFACED that the red `lint` check is the long-standing whole-codebase informational job (red on master through S211-S214; `mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE` not `BLOCKED` -\> non-required), so the merge matched house convention and wasn‚Äôt a blind merge-over-red. Report-faithfully (\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]): name the red check and why it‚Äôs non-blocking; don‚Äôt silently merge or silently refuse. **(1) A nested reactive that uses `req()` ABORTS its caller.** The GV run (`gvResults <- eventReactive(input$runAnalysis, ...)`) must run whether or not an override file was uploaded. A `kinshipOverrideData` reactive written with `req(input$kinshipOverrideFile)` would, when called from inside `gvResults()`, throw the shiny silent-error up through the parent and ABORT the whole analysis whenever no file is present ‚Äì breaking the backward-compat (D10) path. Fix: guard an OPTIONAL input feeding a MANDATORY pipeline with an explicit `if (is.null(input$x)) return(NULL)` and let the consumer treat `NULL` as "no override," reserving `req()` for inputs whose absence really should halt. **(2) Extending a real function‚Äôs signature breaks test DOUBLES that hardcode the old one ‚Äì and only the FULL regression read catches it.** Slice 1 added `kinshipOverrides` to the real `reportGV`, but two issue-#73 tests in `test_modGeneticValue.R` mock `reportGV` via `local_mocked_bindings` with a HAND-COPIED signature; the moment Slice 2‚Äôs module passed the new arg, those mocks errored "unused argument." The two new test files passed in isolation ‚Äì the breakage showed up ONLY in the full clean regression read (`test_dir`, exclude `test-app-`/`test-e2e-`). General rule: when you change the call to a widely-mocked function, run the FULL suite, not just the file you added, and update every test double‚Äôs signature to mirror the real one (the mock is a contract that must track the real signature). **(3) Headless Phase-3E for a Shiny-wiring change (FM \#24 is REQUIRED for app slices).** "Build-clean + unit tests pass" is not enough for runtime wiring, but a headless agent can‚Äôt click a browser file-picker. The defensible substitute: (i) launch the REAL app (`runModularApp(port=..., launch.browser=FALSE)` in the background), poll the port, fetch the page, and `grep` the served HTML for the new namespaced input id (`geneticValue-kinshipOverrideFile`) ‚Äì this proves the module change doesn‚Äôt break app construction AND the new UI element is wired into the live app, not just into the module function in isolation; (ii) lean on a `testServer` integration test that drives the REAL module server with the actual `fileInput` data shape Shiny produces (a data.frame with `name`/`size`/`type`/`datapath` columns, `datapath` pointing at a real temp CSV) -\> `reportGV` -\> assert the ranking moves; (iii) state the residual gap (a true browser click-through needs a GUI/Chrome driver) EXPLICITLY ‚Äì do not let "app starts" masquerade as "feature works end-to-end." Kill the background app afterward (`lsof -ti:PORT | xargs kill`). **(4) Pick a deterministic fixture for an inherently-noisy module.** `reportGV` runs a random gene-drop, but mean kinship (`indivMeanKin <- meanKinship(kmat)`) is read straight off the (deterministic) kinship matrix. Overriding a BOTH-unknown founder pair (`F1,F2` -\> 0.4) raises each one‚Äôs mean kinship deterministically AND sidesteps the issue-#9 one-unknown correction entirely, so `expect_gt(ovrKin, baseKin)` is stable run-to-run with no seed. **(5) Reuse the app‚Äôs reader/validator split and the soft-wrapper-at-the-consumer pattern.** `readKinshipOverrides` mirrors `getGenotypes` (Excel via `readExcelPOSIXToCharacter`, else `read.table`); the module‚Äôs soft `tryCatch(withCallingHandlers(checkKinshipOverrides(read...), warning=notify+muffle), error=notify+NULL)` keeps the app non-fatal (D5) while letting a `>0.5` warning surface yet still apply (D6). The soft wrapper lives in the module (it calls `showNotification`, so it needs session context and can‚Äôt be a pure helper ‚Äì noted at the REFACTOR gate, owner skipped). Stayed strictly in Slice 2: the breeding-group/summary-stats fallback recompute paths (`modBreedingGroups.R:173`, `modSummaryStats.R:357`) are Slice 3 (FM \#18/#25); surfaced the R9 intermediate-state caveat ("run the GV tab first") in NEWS rather than bundling the fallback patch. **General rule:** an "app delivery" slice is where script-level correctness meets reactive plumbing ‚Äì budget the firsthand work for the three wiring-specific failure modes (optional-input-aborts-consumer, signature-change-breaks-mocks, headless-Phase-3E) rather than re-deriving the already-tested core. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 199/200/201 (the draft, ratification, and Slice-1 core this delivers to the app). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED -\> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S216, issue \#13 Slice 2 ‚Äì GV-tab kinship-override upload)

#### Learning 203 ‚Äì WIRING a feature into a DOWNSTREAM consumer‚Äôs FALLBACK path (when the upstream delivery is a Shiny upload) is a plumbing-mapping problem first: read the actual reactive return lists and each consumer‚Äôs prefer-vs-fallback branch FIRSTHAND, apply overrides only where the value is missing, and assert the DETERMINISTIC input of a stochastic consumer rather than its noisy output. Owner picked ‚Äú1‚Äù (Slice 3, the FINAL slice, closes \#13). **(0) Two owner scope-decisions BEFORE declaring RED, surfaced not assumed (a separate `AskUserQuestion`, then the phase gate).** R13 (relation label-vs-value divergence) = ‚Äúnarrow + document‚Äù: the override moves the kinship VALUE everywhere (matrix, relationship-table `kinship` column, CSV export) but the relation LABEL stays pedigree-derived, with NO change to `convertRelationships` (which has other callers ‚Äì narrow avoids a shared-function blast radius). Branching = STACK Slice 3 on the unmerged slice2 branch (PR \#90 open) rather than block the session on a merge. **(1) Verify whether the upstream module EXPOSES the input ‚Äì it usually does NOT.** Slice 2‚Äôs `kinshipOverrideData` reactive was INTERNAL to `modGeneticValueServer`; its return list (`geneticValues`/`topAnimals`/`nAnalyzed`/`kinshipMatrix`/`founderStats`/`maleFounders`/`femaleFounders`) did not include it. Slice 3 had to ADD `kinshipOverrides = kinshipOverrideData` to the return list, then thread `gvResults$kinshipOverrides` through `appServer.R` to both consumer modules (mirroring `founderStats = gvResults$founderStats`). The plan‚Äôs ‚Äúsource from the same input as Slice 2‚Äù line hid this expose+plumb step; reading the actual return list firsthand surfaced it. **(2) Distinguish consumers that get the value TRANSITIVELY from those that ALWAYS recompute ‚Äì they need different reasoning.** Breeding-groups `getKinshipMatrix` PREFERS `geneticValues()$kinship` (already overridden via Slice 1/2) and recomputes `kinship()` only as a fallback -\> apply on the fallback branch only. Summary-stats `getKinshipMatrix` prefers a passed `kinshipMatrix()` but `appServer` passes `kinshipMatrix = NULL`, so it ALWAYS hits the fallback recompute -\> it never saw overrides at all before Slice 3 (NOT ‚Äútransitive then fallback‚Äù as the S216 gotcha framed it ‚Äì a finer firsthand read corrected this). Apply on the fallback branch in both; do NOT re-apply on the preferred branch (the GV-output matrix already carries them; REPLACE is idempotent but double-application is confusing). **(3) D11 does NOT apply to Slice 3.** The issue-#9 `+sexMean/2` correction lives only in `reportGV`‚Äôs mean-kinship SCALAR; the modules consume the raw kinship MATRIX (groupAddAssign threshold / `convertRelationships` / CSV export), so Slice 3 is pure matrix-cell replacement ‚Äì `applyKinshipOverrides` only, no `overriddenIds` threading. **(4) For a STOCHASTIC consumer, assert the DETERMINISTIC input, not the noisy output.** Breeding-group formation (`groupAddAssign`) is RNG-driven; rather than a brittle ‚Äúthese two are not co-housed‚Äù end-to-end assertion, the RED tests call `getKinshipMatrix` directly (testServer exposes the server‚Äôs internal functions/reactives by name) and assert the matrix that FEEDS `groupAddAssign` carries the override (symmetric write) ‚Äì deterministic, isolated, and that matrix is the sole kinship input to formation. Same for summary-stats: assert `getKinshipMatrix()` and `relationshipData()`‚Äôs `kinship` column, not a rendered plot. **(5) Demonstrate R13 ‚Äúnarrow‚Äù with a pedigree-RELATED pair.** `convertRelationships` derives `relation` from pedigree CEPH structure and consults the kinship value only as a last tiebreak (`:87`, `kinship > 0 -> "Other"` for an otherwise-unrelated pair). So for an UNRELATED pair an override flips ‚ÄúNo Relation‚Äù-\>‚ÄúOther‚Äù via the EXISTING tiebreak (not new behavior); but for a pedigree-RELATED pair (F1 is O1‚Äôs sire -\> ‚ÄúParent-Offspring‚Äù) the override moves the value to 0.1 while the label stays ‚ÄúParent-Offspring‚Äù ‚Äì the clean proof that the label is structural, not value-derived. **(6) DRY the soft warn-drop into ONE helper, but stop short of refactoring the working upstream.** The intersect + warn-drop + `applyKinshipOverrides` soft wrapper (D5 ‚Äì never abort on an id absent from the matrix) now lives in a single `@noRd` helper `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix` shared by both modules; `reportGV` keeps its own inline copy because it ALSO needs the surviving `overriddenIds` for the \#9/D11 threading (the helper returns only the matrix), so a clean-DRY there would change the helper‚Äôs contract and touch the working \#9 path ‚Äì offered as REFACTOR, owner skipped. **(7) Close-out mechanics:** NEWS from `NEWS.Rmd` + render (retired the slice-2 ‚Äúrun the GV tab first‚Äù caveat); `spell_check_package` flagged the NOUN ‚Äúrecomputation‚Äù while the VERB ‚Äúrecomputes‚Äù (in roxygen) PASSED -\> reword to the verb, do not pad `WORDLIST`; Phase-3E for a Shiny-wiring change = launch the REAL app, CONNECT a session (which runs `appServer` and instantiates the modules with the new arg, so a wiring error would surface) + grep the served page for the override `fileInput`, and state the browser-click gap explicitly. **General rule:** wiring a feature into a downstream consumer‚Äôs fallback is a plumbing-mapping problem first ‚Äì read the actual reactive return lists + each consumer‚Äôs prefer-vs-fallback branch firsthand, apply only where the value is missing, and assert the deterministic input of any stochastic consumer. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 199/200/201/202 (the draft, ratification, Slice-1 core, and Slice-2 app delivery this completes). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED -\> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S217, issue \#13 Slice 3 ‚Äì breeding-group + summary-stats fallback kinship-override paths; closes \#13)

#### Learning 204 ‚Äì COMPLETING a stacked-PR merge arc is careful outward-facing admin, and a closing keyword ANYWHERE in a PR body fires on merge. Owner picked ‚Äúmerge PR \#90,‚Äù then (after the \#90 merge accidentally closed \#13) ‚ÄúReopen, then merge PR \#91.‚Äù The deliverable was purely outward-facing (two PR merges + issue-state admin); no `R/`/test change ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A, like the S214 docs-only session. **(1) Verify each PR‚Äôs FULL R-CMD-check matrix is GREEN before merging ‚Äì `mergeable: MERGEABLE` is necessary but not sufficient.** `gh pr view <n> --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus` + `gh pr checks <n>`: `UNSTABLE` (not `BLOCKED`) means mergeable despite non-passing NON-REQUIRED checks ‚Äì here the only reds are the long-standing whole-codebase `lint` informational job (red on master since before S211) and `codecov/patch` (a coverage-delta check). Confirm macOS + Windows + all Ubuntu (devel/oldrel-1/release) jobs PASS, then merge ‚Äì the house ‚Äúcareful-admin‚Äù standard (Learning 202 ¬ß0): name the red check and why it‚Äôs non-blocking; never blind-merge-over-red nor blind-refuse. **(2) For a STACK, merge the base PR with a MERGE COMMIT (not squash) and KEEP the base branch.** `gh pr merge <base> --merge` (matching the repo‚Äôs PR \#89 precedent `0438c2d5`) preserves the base PR‚Äôs commit SHAs, so GitHub recognizes them as ancestors of `master` and the dependent PR‚Äôs diff NARROWS to just its own commits; a squash would orphan those SHAs and force a rebase. Keeping the base branch protects the still-open dependent PR. **(3) Merging the base RE-TRIGGERS the dependent PR‚Äôs CI** ‚Äì moving its base (master advanced) reruns its workflow on the new merge commit; two matrix jobs went `pending`. RE-VERIFY the dependent‚Äôs matrix went GREEN on the new base before merging it; wait for `pending` to clear rather than merge over a pending correctness signal (UNSTABLE/non-required would have allowed an immediate merge, but careful-admin waits). A bounded background poll (`while gh pr checks <n> | grep -qi pending; do sleep 60; done`) re-invokes on completion without blocking. **(4) THE GOTCHA: a closing keyword (`closes`/`fixes`/`resolves` `#N`) ANYWHERE in a PR body ‚Äì even inside a descriptive sentence like ‚ÄúSlice 3 closes \#13‚Äù ‚Äì is parsed by GitHub and FIRES on merge to the DEFAULT branch.** PR \#90 (‚ÄúRelates to \#13 ‚Ä¶ Slice 3 closes \#13‚Äù) auto-closed \#13 the moment it merged, even though Slice 3 was not yet on master ‚Äì so the tracking issue read ‚Äúdone‚Äù prematurely.
