Closing keywords fire only on MERGE (that‚Äôs why \#13 stayed open while \#90 sat open). **AUDIT a PR body for stray closing keywords before merging**; for a non-final PR that mentions the issue, phrase it as ‚ÄúSlice 3 WILL close \#13‚Äù / ‚ÄúRelates to \#13‚Äù with NO `closes #13` substring. **(5) To restore an accidentally-closed tracking issue whose final slice isn‚Äôt merged yet: reopen it, then merge the final PR ‚Äì and reopen BEFORE the final merge** so the final PR‚Äôs `closes #N` fires on an OPEN issue and produces the proper ‚Äúclosed via PR \#N‚Äù linkage (firing `closes #N` against an already-closed issue is a no-op with no link). **(6) Issue lifecycle is the OWNER‚Äôs call ‚Äì surface a premature close, don‚Äôt silently reopen.** Posed reopen-and-pause / reopen-then-merge-final / leave-closed as an `AskUserQuestion` (\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], FM \#23) rather than assuming. **(7) Close-out for an admin session:** Phase-3E N/A (no `R/`/runtime code authored ‚Äì merged already-tested, CI-green code; the merged code‚Äôs runtime is covered by the upstream sessions‚Äô Phase-3E + the dependent PR‚Äôs green post-merge matrix, a fuller check than a local `devtools::check`); build-equivalent not run (markdown-only changes don‚Äôt trigger it, per the S214 docs-only precedent); sync local master via `fetch`+`reset` not `pull`; push close-out docs to origin/master (\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]). **The preventable ding:** a pre-merge audit of \#90‚Äôs body for closing keywords would have avoided the close-\>reopen-\>close churn on \#13 ‚Äì prevention beats cure. **General rule:** an outward-facing stacked-PR merge is careful admin ‚Äì green-matrix-before-merge each PR, merge-commit + keep-branch for the base, re-verify the re-triggered dependent CI, audit PR bodies for stray closing keywords, reopen-before-final-merge to restore an accidentally-closed issue, and surface issue-state decisions to the owner. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 202 ¬ß0 (the careful-admin merge practice) and completes the issue \#13 arc begun in Learning 199-203. **This was an admin/merge session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S218, completed the issue \#13 merge arc ‚Äì merged Slices 2+3 to master; \#13 CLOSED via PR \#91)

#### Learning 205 ‚Äì DOCUMENTING a shipped feature‚Äôs behavior + LIMITS in the in-app UI is a real, test-backable deliverable: assert the doc TEXT in the rendered UI and the static guidance assets, put it where the input is SUPPLIED, and mind that `helpText()` renders each argument as a separate text node. Owner asked ‚Äúare item-3‚Äôs \[gvaConvergence / relationship-label / unknown-parent\] implications documented in the app?‚Äù, then ‚ÄúDocumentation should appear at a minimum in the UI where the kinship coefficient(s) are supplied.‚Äù The deliverable was DOCUMENTING the three issue-#13 deferred follow-ups (NOT implementing them) across three in-app surfaces. **(0) An ‚Äúis X documented in-app?‚Äù audit is a FALSE-NEGATIVE risk ‚Äì enumerate ALL in-app surfaces and grep each broadly before concluding ‚Äúabsent.‚Äù** The app‚Äôs user-facing doc surfaces are inline `helpText()`, the per-tab `includeHTML()` guidance assets (`inst/extdata/ui_guidance/*.html`), and the About/Documentation tab (`appUI.R`). The item-3 implications were recorded only DEVELOPER-facing (roxygen `@param` + code comments in `modSummaryStats.R`) and PACKAGE-level (NEWS, planning doc) ‚Äì ZERO in-app coverage; the override-upload helpText covered FILE FORMAT only. A function can be MENTIONED in guidance without its caveat: `gvaConvergence` already appears in `genetic_value.html`/`gvAndBgDesc.html`, but neither said it ignores overrides. **(1) Put the documentation where the input is SUPPLIED (the owner‚Äôs ‚Äúat a minimum‚Äù floor).** The first-class surface is the `helpText` at the override `fileInput` in `modGeneticValueUI`, not just the tab-level guidance HTML ‚Äì a user who never opens the guidance panel still sees the caveats at the upload control. Placement (supply-point only vs + guidance panels) and content depth were ratified via `AskUserQuestion` (\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]): user-facing COPY that ships in the package is the owner‚Äôs editorial call; offered a ‚Äúreview exact wording first‚Äù hold at the GREEN gate. **(2) THE RENDERING GOTCHA: `helpText(a, b, c)` renders each argument as a SEPARATE, newline-separated text node, NOT a space-joined string.** So an asserted multi-word phrase split across arguments (‚Äú‚Ä¶the relationship‚Äù / ‚Äúlabel‚Ä¶‚Äù) renders with a newline between the words and `grepl(fixed = TRUE)` MISSES it. Keep each asserted phrase inside ONE string argument ‚Äì or build it with a single `paste(...)` (whose space-join happens BEFORE it becomes one text node) so source lines stay \<=80 while the rendered phrase is contiguous. The HTML assets have the same constraint: keep an asserted phrase on one line (no mid-phrase line break). **(3) For RED discriminators, the rendered UI INCLUDES the included HTML ‚Äì pick phrases absent from the WHOLE rendered output.** `as.character(modGeneticValueUI("gv"))` embeds `genetic_value.html`, which already contains ‚ÄúgvaConvergence‚Äù ‚Äì so asserting ‚ÄúgvaConvergence‚Äù in the UI test would FALSE-PASS at RED. Chose helpText-EXCLUSIVE phrases (‚Äúchange the kinship value‚Äù, ‚Äúconvergence check ignores‚Äù, ‚Äúrelationship label‚Äù) verified absent from the current 9.3KB rendered UI before freezing; 12/14 assertions failed (the 2 passing = the `nzchar(path)` file-exists guards), 0 errors. **(4) Phase-3E for a UI-TEXT change is still REQUIRED (FM \#24) ‚Äì and read the app‚Äôs ACTUAL port from its startup log.** Launched the real `runModularApp()`, fetched the served page, grepped for all six new caveat strings (all PRESENT; 0 startup errors). `runModularApp` set its OWN port (6013) and ignored my `options(shiny.port = 6099L)`, so the first curl hit a refused port ‚Äì parse ‚ÄúListening on 127.0.0.1:PORT‚Äù from the log rather than assuming the port you requested. **(5) Documenting a known limitation is a legitimate small deliverable.** The three item-3 follow-ups (gvaConvergence-ignores-overrides; relationship label-vs-value divergence; the unknown-parent \#9 edge cases) remain UNIMPLEMENTED, but the gap that users couldn‚Äôt SEE the limits is now closed ‚Äì a cheap, high-value slice distinct from the deeper implementation work, which stays on the backlog. **(6) SESSION-BOUNDARY discipline: a ‚Äúsession‚Äù is a LOGICAL unit (orient -\> one deliverable -\> close out), not a chat window ‚Äì but don‚Äôt claim/gate an implementation while still in Phase-0/1 discussion.** I slid from orientation + the doc-coverage investigation straight into writing the 1B stub and posing the RED gate; the owner flagged it (‚Äúshouldn‚Äôt this be a new session‚Äù). Reverted the premature stub, clarified that the investigation was the front-half (orient/scope) of ONE deliverable with no committed artifact to close out separately, and re-claimed cleanly once the owner chose to continue in-conversation. The investigation feeding the scope is fine; auto-promoting it into a claimed deliverable before the owner sets the deliverable is the miss. **General rule:** documenting a feature‚Äôs limits in-app is test-backable ‚Äì audit every in-app surface to avoid a false ‚Äúundocumented‚Äù negative, write the docs where the input is supplied first, assert the text in the rendered UI + the static assets (each asserted phrase in one text node / one line), pick RED discriminators absent from the WHOLE rendered output, and smoke the real app on its actual port. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 199-204 (the issue \#13 design/implementation/merge arc whose deferred limits this documents). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT (documentation) session (RED -\> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S219, documenting issue \#13 item-3 kinship-override behavior + limits in the in-app UI)

#### Learning 206 ‚Äì WIRING an existing feature into a SIBLING consumer that shares a pipeline STAGE: read the sibling‚Äôs FULL handling firsthand (a shared correction step makes ‚Äúapply to the matrix only‚Äù a SILENT-DIVERGENCE trap, not a style choice); recognize when implementing X FALSIFIES a prior session‚Äôs docs + the doc-tests pinning them; for a diagnostic whose output is INSENSITIVE to the change on the easy fixture, prototype the perturbation on the HARD fixture before freezing RED; and lint your edits (package loaded) before the owner has to ask. Owner picked ‚ÄúItem-3 IMPLEMENTATION follow-ups‚Äù -\> (slice `AskUserQuestion`) `gvaConvergence` override support, the most self-contained of the three deferred item-3 follow-ups S219 documented. **(1) ‚Äúitem-3 follow-ups‚Äù is THREE deliverables ‚Äì surface the slice pick (1-and-done, FM \#18/#25).** A category is not a deliverable; posed the slice choice (`gvaConvergence` / R13 relationship-label / D11) as a pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion` with the self-contained one recommended, then claimed only that slice. **(2) A sibling consumer that shares a pipeline STAGE needs the sibling‚Äôs FULL treatment, not just the obvious half.** `gvaConvergence` mirrors `reportGV`‚Äôs ranking pipeline AND calls the same `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`; `reportGV` both (a) writes overrides into `kmat` AND (b) threads `overriddenIds` into the \#9 correction (skips `+sexMean/2` for an overridden one-unknown animal ‚Äì D11 supersession). Applying only (a) ‚Äì the literal ‚Äúreuse `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix`‚Äù ask ‚Äì would leave a one-unknown overridden animal RANKED differently than `reportGV` ranks it, defeating the diagnostic‚Äôs whole purpose (it exists to characterize `reportGV`‚Äôs selection order). Reading the sibling‚Äôs block firsthand turned ‚Äúmatrix-only vs match-reportGV‚Äù into a pre-RED owner decision (FM \#13 literal-minimum avoided); owner chose Match reportGV ‚Äì a CORRECTNESS call, not a style one. The `overriddenIds` machinery already existed (this is NOT the deferred D11 option-C/both-unknown/sib-pair work). **(3) Implementing a follow-up can FALSIFY a prior session‚Äôs docs AND the doc-tests pinning them ‚Äì fold the correction in.** S219 had documented (in `modGeneticValue` helpText + `genetic_value.html` + a NEWS bullet) that ‚ÄúgvaConvergence ignores overrides / ranks on pedigree kinship,‚Äù and `test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R` asserted those exact phrases (‚Äúconvergence check ignores‚Äù, ‚Äúdoes not use‚Äù). Honoring overrides made all of them FALSE. Surfaced ‚Äúupdate the S219 docs now vs defer‚Äù as a pre-RED scope decision (owner: update now), then corrected the three doc surfaces + the two doc-test assertions as part of GREEN ‚Äì shipping a feature with contradicting docs/tests is incoherent. General: grep for prior in-app/NEWS statements about the behavior you are changing; the doc-test that locked the old statement is part of YOUR change surface. **(4) For a diagnostic whose OUTPUT is insensitive to the change on the easy fixture, prototype the perturbation on the HARD fixture BEFORE freezing RED.** `gvaConvergence`‚Äôs convergence metrics are gene-drop-churn-driven; on a no-gu pedigree (qcPed) both half-splits agree (overlap=1, rankAgreement=1) REGARDLESS of mean kinship, so an override is output-INVISIBLE there. A scratchpad copy of the function body (with the planned override block) run on the gu-bearing convergence fixture found a perturbation (5 offspring pairs @ 0.49) that GUARANTEED a curve change ‚Äì so the differential RED test (`expect_false(isTRUE(all.equal(base, over)))`) fails-then-passes for the RIGHT reason rather than risking a GREEN no-op. Reading-only prototyping of the EXISTING system to design a robust failing test is legitimate RED work. **(5) Honestly scope an assertion the function‚Äôs public surface cannot express.** The \#9-suppression NUMERIC (does an overridden one-unknown animal skip `+sexMean/2`?) is not observable through `gvaConvergence`‚Äôs return (it exposes convergence metrics, no per-animal mean kinship; no-gu pedigrees are output-invariant). Rather than build a brittle bespoke gu+one-unknown fixture (against the test file‚Äôs stated anti-tautology philosophy), test 5 exercises the PATH (runs clean + override honored via the ‚ÄúN kinship override(s) applied‚Äù message on a real one-unknown qcPed animal, `X=0K7VJN`) and the numeric is covered by construction-parity with `reportGV` + `test_reportGV.R`‚Äôs D10/D5/D11 tests (the identical `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship(overriddenIds=)` call). State the residual; do not fake a direct assertion. **(6) Lint changed R files BEFORE the owner has to ask ‚Äì with the package LOADED.** Owner correction mid-session (‚Äúcorrect your coding/comment standards to avoid creating new lints‚Äù): I had introduced two `line_length_linter(80)` violations (the new `@param`; a former-last-arg line pushed to 81 by my added trailing comma). The CI `lint` is long-standing red, but my EDITS must be lint-clean. Establish the real signal by linting with `pkgload::load_all()` first: `object_usage_linter` ‚Äúno visible global function‚Äù warnings drop to 0 once the namespace is present, and `object_name_linter` camelCase warnings appear only when linting a file OUTSIDE the project dir (the project `.lintr` allows `snake_case`/`CamelCase`/`camelCase`; `tests`/`vignettes`/`inst` excluded) ‚Äì so line-length/style are the real signal. New memory \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\]. **(7) Close-out mechanics:** `spell_check_package` flagged the singular ‚Äúproband‚Äù (WORDLIST has only the plural ‚Äúprobands‚Äù) -\> reworded to ‚Äúanalysis set‚Äù (matching `reportGV`‚Äôs own `@param` for the identical concept), did NOT pad WORDLIST; `man/` regenerated via `document()` (no NAMESPACE change ‚Äì already exported, helper `@noRd`); NEWS from `NEWS.Rmd` + render (removed the now-false clause from the S219 bullet, added the item-3 bullet); Phase-3E REQUIRED because the deliverable edits in-app UI text even though the core function is script-level (launched the real app, grepped the served page for the corrected text, read the actual port 6013 from the log). **General rule:** wiring a feature into a sibling that shares a pipeline stage is a ‚Äúmatch the sibling‚Äôs FULL handling‚Äù problem; implementing a follow-up can falsify prior docs + their doc-tests (fold the fix in); prototype the perturbation on the fixture where the change is observable before freezing RED; honestly scope assertions the public surface can‚Äôt express; lint your edits (package loaded) before being asked. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\], \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\]; extends Learning 199-205 (the issue \#13 arc ‚Äì the first of the three item-3 follow-ups S219 documented as limits). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED -\> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S220, issue \#13 item-3 ‚Äì gvaConvergence honors kinship overrides)

#### Learning 207 ‚Äì Completing the push-\>PR-\>CI-\>merge arc for an ALREADY-FINISHED slice as careful outward-facing admin: the new nuggets are (a) confirming the lone CI red is PRE-EXISTING whole-package noise via the check-run ANNOTATIONS API when `gh run view --log` comes back empty, and (b) a git-state trap when syncing local master with an uncommitted 1B stub still in the tree. Owner picked ‚Äú1‚Äù ‚Äì the full admin arc S220 suggested: push the branch, open a PR ‚ÄúRelates to \#13‚Äù with NO closing keyword, watch CI to green, merge. **(0) Verify-then-merge, do not merge-on-`MERGEABLE`.** `MERGEABLE` + the owner‚Äôs explicit ‚Äúmerge‚Äù IS authorization, but the careful-admin bar (Learning 204 / \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]) is: confirm the FULL R-CMD-check matrix (macOS + Windows + Ubuntu devel/oldrel-1/release) + pkgdown + test-coverage + both codecov checks are all green, and SURFACE the one red (`lint`) with evidence it is non-blocking, BEFORE merging ‚Äì not merge the moment GitHub says mergeable. **(1) When `gh run view <run_id> --log` / `--log-failed` returns EMPTY for a failed job, get the per-file findings from the check-run ANNOTATIONS API.** `gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/check-runs/<JOB_ID>/annotations` returned the 12 lint annotations after `--log` came back 0 bytes (a known gh quirk for some failed jobs). They were entirely PRE-EXISTING whole-package noise ‚Äì `data-raw/fgSEValidation.R` (implicit-assignment / line-length / commented-code) plus a Node.js-20 workflow-deprecation warning ‚Äì with NONE in this PR‚Äôs changed files (`gvaConvergence.R`, `modGeneticValue.R`), corroborating S220‚Äôs ‚Äúlint 0 on the changed files (package loaded).‚Äù That annotation evidence is what turns ‚Äúlint is red‚Äù into ‚Äúlint is the documented non-blocking red AND this PR added nothing to it‚Äù ‚Äì the concrete form of ‚Äúsurface the non-blocking red.‚Äù **(2) Syncing local master with an uncommitted 1B stub present is a git-state trap.** `git checkout master` ABORTS when the working tree has uncommitted changes (the Phase-1B stub I had added to `SESSION_NOTES.md`), so a chained `git reset --hard origin/master` reset the CURRENT (feature) branch to the merge commit instead of fast-forwarding master ‚Äì discarding the stub and moving the WRONG pointer. Low harm here (the 1B stub is always superseded by the full handoff; `reset --hard` leaves untracked files like the audit HTML untouched), but the lesson: after the merge, get the tree clean FIRST (the stub is disposable -\> let it go, or commit it), THEN `git switch master` + `git merge --ff-only origin/master`, and VERIFY `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` and local-master == origin/master before trusting the sync. Do not chain `checkout` then `reset --hard` blind to the abort message. **(3) PR-body hygiene for a CLOSED tracking issue.** \#13 is CLOSED; the body says ‚ÄúRelates to \#13,‚Äù and I grep-scanned the RENDERED body for any `clos(e|es|ed)/fix(es|ed)/resolv(e|es|ed)` adjacent to a `#` (none) before merge AND re-verified \#13 stayed CLOSED after ‚Äì a stray closing keyword fires on merge to the default branch even inside prose (Learning 204 / S218 close-\>reopen-\>close churn). **General rule:** an admin/merge session‚Äôs deliverable is a VERIFIED integration ‚Äì full matrix green + the lone red PROVEN pre-existing (via the annotations API if `--log` is empty) + a closing-keyword-free body for a closed issue + a clean, correctly-ordered local sync. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\], \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\]; extends Learning 204 (the careful-admin merge practice) and closes out the S220 gvaConvergence slice (Learning 206). **This was an admin/merge session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S221, issue \#13 item-3 ‚Äì merged PR \#93)

#### Learning 208 ‚Äì Branch-hygiene deletion of MERGED feature branches as careful outward-facing admin: verify each branch firsthand, know that `git branch -d` checks the branch‚Äôs UPSTREAM (not only HEAD), and enumerate the set from `git branch --merged` rather than trusting the handoff prose. Owner picked ‚ÄúAll 5 issue-13 branches‚Äù (one `AskUserQuestion`). **(1) Verify-before-delete is the SAFEGUARDS ‚Äúnever delete without verifying it‚Äôs committed‚Äù rule applied to branches.** Before deleting anything, confirmed all 5 issue-13 branches were merged into BOTH `master` and `origin/master` (`git branch --merged master` + `git branch -r --merged origin/master`) ‚Äì deleting a branch whose commits all live in master loses nothing (recoverable via the merge commits / reflog), which is exactly what makes the deletion safe. **(2) `git branch -d` compares against the branch‚Äôs UPSTREAM, not only HEAD.** Four branches deleted cleanly with the safe `-d`; the fifth, `issue13-item3-gvaconv-overrides`, REFUSED ‚Äì ‚Äúnot yet merged to refs/remotes/origin/‚Ä¶ even though it is merged to HEAD‚Äù ‚Äì because S221‚Äôs `reset --hard` had moved its LOCAL tip to the merge commit `daa7728b` (the ‚Äúahead 1‚Äù quirk, Learning 207), so it is ahead of its remote feature tip while still fully in master. Resolution: prove containment explicitly with `git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> master` (exit 0) THEN `git branch -D` ‚Äì a VERIFIED force-delete, not a blind one. Do NOT reach for `-D` on the first refusal; confirm the ancestor relationship first. **(3) Enumerate the deletion set from git, not the handoff prose.** The S221 handoff named 4 branches but `git branch --merged master` showed 5 issue-13 branches (`issue13-slice1-kinship-overrides` was merged via PR \#89 but never re-listed in the prose) ‚Äì the complete-job set came from the merge query, and slice1‚Äôs omission was SURFACED to the owner rather than silently followed or silently expanded (FM \#13 literal-minimum / \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]). **(4) Remote deletion is outward-facing -\> confirm the exact set first.** `git push origin --delete` modifies origin, so the precise set (5 vs 4; local + remote; whether to also sweep the unrelated stale merged branches `module`/`rlabkey-version-floor`, left alone as out-of-scope) was a single pre-execution `AskUserQuestion`; `dev` is a mainline branch and was never a candidate. **General rule:** branch hygiene = verify-merged-firsthand (BOTH refs) -\> enumerate from `git branch --merged` -\> confirm the outward-facing set with the owner -\> safe `-d` first, and only `-D` after `merge-base --is-ancestor` proves containment. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\]; extends Learning 207 (the ‚Äúahead 1‚Äù quirk this deletion had to handle). **This was an admin/branch-hygiene session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S222, issue \#13 branch hygiene ‚Äì deleted 5 merged branches local+remote)

#### Learning 209 ‚Äì Adding a DISPLAY-LAYER FLAG for a documented divergence as the narrow alternative to a wider core change (issue \#13 item-3 R13): when a prior session shipped the ‚Äúnarrow + document‚Äù fork of a reconcile/flag decision, the remaining work is the OTHER fork (flag), and the owner‚Äôs blast-radius call (display-layer vs touching the shared core function) is a pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion`. Owner picked ‚ÄúItem-3 IMPLEMENTATION follow-ups -\> R13‚Äù -\> recon mapped the relationship display (it is NOT an on-screen table; the per-pair output reaches the user only via the ‚ÄúExport All Relationships‚Äù CSV + the returned `relationships` reactive) and the divergence (`convertRelationships`‚Äôs `relation` LABEL is pedigree-derived at `:51-91`; only the kinship VALUE is overridden, so a pair can self-contradict). **(1) ‚ÄúR13 reconcile/flag‚Äù is two FORKS and two SURFACES ‚Äì surface them, do not assume.** Fork A flag-vs-relabel: a single kinship coefficient does NOT identify a unique relationship (0.25 = parent-offspring OR full-sib; 0.125 = half-sib OR grandparent OR avuncular), so relabel-from-value is genetically UNSOUND ‚Äì flag (mark the pair, infer nothing) is the correct call; surfaced as the owner‚Äôs genetics decision, not picked silently. Fork B blast radius: the display-layer choice (annotate in `modSummaryStats` AFTER `convertRelationships`, using the override frame the module already receives) leaves the shared core function untouched (S217‚Äôs deliberate boundary; `convertRelationships` has other callers + feeds the class table), the narrowest single TDD slice. **(2) D10 byte-identical = add the flag column ONLY when overrides are supplied.** `flagOverriddenRelationships(rel, NULL/zero-row)` returns the input unchanged (`expect_identical`), so the no-override CSV/reactive stays exactly as before; the column appears only when an override is in play. **(3) Test the REAL app path, not the convenient one.** The module applies overrides only in the `kinshipMatrix = NULL` fallback recompute (`:374-376` ‚Äì the path appServer actually uses); the pre-existing relationship tests pass `kinshipMatrix = reactive(...)`, which RETURNS the provided matrix and BYPASSES overrides ‚Äì so an override integration test MUST pass `kinshipMatrix = NULL` + `kinshipOverrides = reactive(frame)` or it silently never exercises the feature. **(4) Match pairs UNORDERED.** `convertRelationships` emits canonical pairs; flag with a sorted-pair key (`pmin`/`pmax`, sep=‚Äú) so an override given as (B,A) still flags the (A,B) row; an override pair absent from the table flags nothing and does not error (D5-consistent). **(5) Fold the doc forward (S219 lesson).** The in-app guidance already explained the divergence; ADD one sentence that overridden pairs are now flagged, pinned by a NEW doc-test asserting a distinct word (‚Äùflagged‚Äù) ‚Äì chosen because ‚Äúoverride‚Äù/‚Äúoverridden‚Äù were already present ‚Äì WITHOUT disturbing the existing asserted phrases (‚Äúoverride‚Äù, ‚Äúrelationship label‚Äù, ‚Äúpedigree-derived‚Äù). **(6) `@noRd` internal helper** -\> no NAMESPACE/man change for the helper itself (only the edited module‚Äôs `.Rd` regenerates), testable directly under `load_all`. **(7) Pre-existing lints are not yours to fix in a tight slice** ‚Äì the 3 `line_length_linter(80)` hits in `modSummaryStats.R` (an `@param` line I preserved verbatim + two `getKinshipMatrix` comment lines) pre-date this session; my added lines are all \<=80 (zero NEW lints, \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\]); left the pre-existing ones alone (scope discipline). RED was clean-for-the-right-reason: 6 helper ERRORS (function absent) + the with-override flag FAILURE + the doc-sentence FAILURE; the no-override D10 guard + `convertRelationships`/`makeRelationsClasses` stayed green (proves display-layer-only). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 205 (documenting item-3 limits in-app) and Learning 206 (the gvaConvergence item-3 slice) ‚Äì this implements the SECOND of the three deferred item-3 follow-ups (R13), leaving D11 (targeted option C / both-unknown / shared-sib-pair). **Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session.**

#### Learning 210 ‚Äì The careful-admin push-\>PR-\>CI-\>merge arc is now ROUTINE (3rd run: S218/S221/S224) ‚Äì follow Learning 207, with one refinement to the local-master sync. Owner picked ‚Äúoutward-facing admin‚Äù -\> integrate the finished S223 R13 slice (PR \#94, merge commit `33b0b09f`; \#13 stayed CLOSED). **(1) Verify-then-merge held again exactly as 207 documents:** the full R-CMD-check matrix (macOS release; Windows release; Ubuntu devel/oldrel-1/release) + pkgdown + test-coverage + both codecov checks all green; the lone `lint` red PROVEN pre-existing via `gh api repos/.../check-runs/<job>/annotations` ‚Äì the same noise set (12 findings: 10 in `data-raw/fgSEValidation.R` ‚Äì implicit-assignment / line-length / commented-code / nonportable-path; 2 `.github` ‚Äì a Node.js-20 workflow-deprecation warning + the exit-code-31 summary), with NONE in this PR‚Äôs changed files (`flagOverriddenRelationships.R`, `modSummaryStats.R`). The annotations API remains the reliable path (`gh run view --log` still returns empty for the failed lint job). Do not merge-on-`MERGEABLE`; surface the lone red with evidence first. **(2) Refinement to the 207 sync trap ‚Äì STASH the stub, do not discard it.** 207‚Äôs trap: an uncommitted 1B stub makes `git checkout master` abort, so a chained `reset --hard` resets the WRONG (feature) branch; 207‚Äôs fix was ‚Äúclean the tree FIRST -\> the stub is disposable, let it go.‚Äù Cleaner and marker-preserving: `git stash push -- SESSION_NOTES.md` (stash ONLY the stub, leaving the standing untracked audit HTML in place) -\> `git fetch` -\> `git switch master` -\> `git merge --ff-only origin/master` -\> `git stash pop`. The pop applies cleanly because after the fast-forward, master‚Äôs `SESSION_NOTES.md` == the merged feature version == the stash‚Äôs base ‚Äì so the trap is avoided AND the ghost-session marker survives the sync (no need to throw it away). Verify `HEAD == origin/master` (== the merge commit) and the merged code is present on master (e.g.¬Ý`R/flagOverriddenRelationships.R` exists; the wiring grep hits) after. **(3) PR-body hygiene for a CLOSED issue unchanged:** ‚ÄúRelates to \#13‚Äù; grep BOTH the commit messages and the rendered body for the GitHub closing-keyword pattern (`clos|fix|resolv` adjacent to a `#N`); confirm \#13 CLOSED before AND after merge. **(4) Branch deletion stays a SEPARATE step (S221-\>S222 split):** merge does not delete the branch ‚Äì left `issue13-item3-r13-flag-overridden` for a future hygiene pass rather than bundling (1-and-done). **General rule:** when the arc is routine the value is in NOT regressing ‚Äì run the same verify-then-merge + annotations-API proof + closing-keyword-free body, and use the stash-the-stub sync to keep the marker; do not invent novelty where the documented arc already applies. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\]; extends Learning 207 (the admin/merge arc) and integrates Learning 209 (the R13 slice it merges). **This was an admin/merge session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S224, issue \#13 item-3 ‚Äì merged PR \#94)

#### Learning 211 ‚Äì ‚ÄúImplement the follow-up‚Äù can resolve to ‚Äúthere is no implementable slice ‚Äì track it instead‚Äù: recon a deferred-work thread BEFORE declaring RED, and when every remaining sub-item is blocked on a design/genetics decision, FILE A TRACKING ISSUE as the deliverable. Owner picked ‚ÄúBranch hygiene; Item-3 follow-up‚Äù. The carried thread framed the remaining issue-#13 item-3 work as ‚ÄúD11 IMPLEMENTATION follow-up (1 of 3 left)‚Äù, which reads like a ready TDD slice. **It was not.** A 5-agent read-only recon workflow (`wf_c1143706-b45`) + the plan doc showed the D11 CORE (blanket supersession, option A) was ALREADY shipped + 4-part-tested (`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:164-171` guard; `test_reportGV.R:539-610`), and the three things called ‚Äúfollow-up‚Äù are deferred v1 LIMITATIONS, each blocked: **option C** (targeted suppression) needs a NEW override-schema column because the missing-parent side CANNOT be derived (the parent is unrecorded -\> no pedigree edges; a kinship value is path-agnostic) -\> plan-mode; **both-unknown-\>one-unknown promotion** and **shared-parent sib-pair coupling** are genetics-methodology calls (#9 defers both-unknown; the `for (i in which(oneU))` loop at `:162` is per-animal independent) -\> `/grill-me`-style ratification. **The right move:** do NOT default to writing RED tests against unratified behavior (that would invent a spec ‚Äì FM \#13/#19 in spirit); surface ‚Äúno ready slice -\> here are the options‚Äù via a pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion` (\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]), and when the owner picks tracking, file ONE well-structured issue (#95, `enhancement`) that captures each follow-up‚Äôs blocker + blast-radius <file:line> inventory + disposition, so the expensive recon is not lost (the follow-ups had been prose-only in `plan.md:153,212,230` / CHANGELOG S214 with NO issue number ‚Äì \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] confirmed no existing issue and that \#76 was a different, already-done deferral). Outward-facing hygiene: ‚ÄúFollow-up from \#13 (D11)‚Äù with NO closing keyword; confirm \#13 AND \#9 CLOSED before and after. **Branch-hygiene half (Learning 208 reused):** the merged branch‚Äôs tip `68db38c9` was the ORIGINAL feature tip (NOT moved to the merge commit ‚Äì S224 fast-forwarded master rather than `reset --hard`-ing the feature branch as S221 did to gvaconv), so it was a clean ancestor of master AND origin/master and safe `-d` deleted it ‚Äì no `-D` force needed (the ‚Äúahead 1‚Äù quirk did NOT bite this time; verify which case you are in, do not assume `-D`). **Workflow caveat:** 2 of the 5 recon agents hit the StructuredOutput retry cap (5) and returned nothing ‚Äì I backfilled by hand (a `gh issue list --state all` delta scan + a direct read of `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:140-191`), no lost coverage; a structured-output schema with several REQUIRED free-text fields raises the retry-cap risk, so keep recon schemas lean (fewer required fields / allow short answers) or expect to backfill. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 208 (branch hygiene) and the issue-#13 item-3 arc (Learning 205/206/209/210). **This was an admin/disposition session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S225, issue \#13 item-3 -\> filed \#95)

#### Learning 212 ‚Äì For a SCHEMA-EXTENSION / ARCHITECTURE plan, run the adversarial verification BEFORE the plan is ‚Äúdone‚Äù, and re-verify a recon subagent‚Äôs DIRECTIONAL/SEMANTIC claims firsthand even when its line numbers are right. Owner picked ‚Äúplan option C design for issue \#95‚Äù; deliverable = `docs/planning/issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md` (the 4th-override-column + targeted-suppression design that \#95/D11 deferred). **(1) The two-workflow shape worked well:** a 9-agent read-only INVENTORY workflow (`wf_4012d83a-551`) captured firsthand current `file:line` across the ~12 affected files (correcting the parent plan‚Äôs drifted citations ‚Äì the `+sexMean/2` add is `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:190`, not the plan‚Äôs stale `:175`), then a 3-agent VERIFICATION workflow (`wf_38c97263-a2e`: citation accuracy / design red-team / consumer completeness) ‚Äì mirroring the parent plan‚Äôs S213 verification, which is THIS project‚Äôs bar for a planning deliverable. The verification earned its keep: it caught a **HIGH-severity backward-compat contradiction** (the draft‚Äôs C1 absent-column default said ‚Äúkeep the prior‚Äù, but D10 requires absent-column =\> blanket-A =\> SUPPRESS ‚Äì opposite outcomes for any pre-existing override on a one-unknown animal; the fix is TWO opposite defaults ‚Äì whole-file-absent =\> blanket-A, per-row-blank =\> known-side) and a **factually WRONG directional claim**. **(2) The meta-lesson (the wrong claim‚Äôs root cause):** my FIRST (inventory) workflow‚Äôs agent asserted ‚ÄúgvaConvergence computes parentage BEFORE its correction call (`:169`)‚Äù ‚Äì the LINE NUMBER `:169` was correct but the BEFORE/AFTER direction was WRONG (`:169` is AFTER the correction at `:157-163`, same as reportGV‚Äôs `:258`). I propagated it into the draft‚Äôs load-bearing ‚Äúasymmetry‚Äù framing. The verification‚Äôs citation agent flagged it and I CONFIRMED firsthand. **Rule: a subagent‚Äôs directional / ordering / ‚Äúalready-has-X‚Äù / depends-on claims are NOT verified just because its `file:line` checks out ‚Äì re-read them firsthand (code wins; FM \#11/#20 applied to subagent output).** Bonus correction from the same finding: the side classification needs `isU(ped$sire/dam)` (the missing SIDE), NOT `classifyParentage` (which returns only the parentage CLASS) ‚Äì so there was no hoist needed at all. **(3) Red-teaming improves the DESIGN, not just catches errors:** the draft proposed ‚Äúadd a NULL-default `overrideMissingSideIds` param‚Äù; the red-team showed that creates a NULL-vs-`character(0L)` opposite-semantics trap on the main path. The better design that emerged ‚Äì ‚Äúcaller computes the suppress set, redefine `overriddenIds`‚Äôs contract, function stays dumb/unchanged‚Äù ‚Äì has no new param, no ambiguity, no signature change. Let the adversary push you to a simpler design. **(4) Completeness grep + a completeness agent found doc surfaces the file-scoped readers AND the issue‚Äôs own inventory missed:** `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/genetic_value.html:50-61` (a parallel user-facing limitation copy) and `tests/testthat/test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R:21-78` (a doc-consistency test that asserts the exact ‚Äúcurrent limitation‚Äù phrasing and BREAKS when the text is rewritten). A schema/UI change‚Äôs blast radius includes phrase-pinning doc-consistency tests, which symbol-greps do not surface. **(5) The plan is a DRAFT; do NOT ratify the owner‚Äôs calls in it (FM \#19).** Option C‚Äôs two prerequisites ‚Äì C1 (the irreversible 4th-column schema encoding) and C2 (the residual genetics-modeling rule: full-drop-on-any-missing-side vs partial-residual) ‚Äì are owner/geneticist gates settled via `/grill-me` against a firsthand numeric check, exactly as D11 was; the plan RECOMMENDS for each but marks the ¬ß7 checklist UNRATIFIED. Be honest in the recommendation: rule (i) is a JUDGMENT not a slam-dunk (it trades the case-(b) over-credit for a smaller SCOPED missing-side over-credit). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 211 (which filed \#95) and the issue-13 plan‚Äôs D11/S213-verification precedent. **This was a planning/architecture session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A (the plan is the deliverable; implementation is a SEPARATE later session, gated on Phase-0 ratification).** (S226, issue \#95 option C)

#### Learning 213 ‚Äì A Phase-0 genetics-ratification grill: do the firsthand numeric check FIRST and PRESERVE its evidence IN the plan, lead with the upstream domain judgment, and reframe a ‚Äútargeted-suppression‚Äù rule as a strict SUBSET of the blanket it refines. Owner picked ‚Äúratify C1 + C2 via `/grill-me`‚Äù (Phase 0 for issue \#95 option C, the gate S226‚Äôs plan left UNRATIFIED). Deliverable = the ratified ¬ß7 checklist + a new ¬ß8 evidence record in `docs/planning/issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md`; **0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides** (every recommendation ratified as written). **(1) Numeric check BEFORE the grill (the D11/S214 bar), grounded firsthand on real `qcPed`:** replicated the `reportGV.R:118-180` mean-kinship path (`getGVPopulation` -\> `kinship` -\> `filterKinMatrix` -\> `meanKinship` -\> `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`) and measured the `+ sexMean/2` prior = **median ~1.34 SD** of the mean-kinship spread, a single half-sib override‚Äôs raw effect = **~10%** of that prior (prior/raw ~9.9x), rank swings 49-121 of 280. **These independently reproduced S214‚Äôs recorded ‚Äú~1 SD‚Äù / ‚Äú8.4x‚Äù numbers (`issue13-kinship-overrides-plan.md:151-152`) ‚Äì cross-validating a firsthand computation against the prior session‚Äôs recorded numbers IS the adversarial verification for a load-bearing number** (\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\]: I did the C2 DELTA, not a re-run). **(2) PRESERVE the evidence in the plan, not scratchpad.** S214‚Äôs D11 evidence lived only in a scratchpad workflow output and was LOST; I wrote the numbers + a re-runnable reproduction recipe into plan ¬ß8 so the next session need not re-derive them. A genetics decision‚Äôs evidence belongs in the durable artifact, not the ephemeral workflow log. **(3) The decisive REFRAMING the evidence surfaced (verified logically, not numerically):** rule (i) suppresses the prior iff an override informs the focal‚Äôs MISSING side; blanket-A suppresses for ANY override -\> rule (i) suppresses a **strict subset** of blanket-A‚Äôs suppressions, so it is a **Pareto improvement over today** (it newly fixes case b ‚Äì known-side overrides ‚Äì and leaves case a EXACTLY as today). The large ~1.2 SD case-(a) residual is therefore **pre-existing, not introduced by option C** ‚Äì which is WHY shipping the genetically-incomplete rule (i) for v1 is safe and rule (ii) is a deferrable follow-up. **Separate ‚Äúis the narrow rule worth shipping‚Äù (yes ‚Äì it strictly improves) from ‚Äúis it genetically complete‚Äù (no ‚Äì needs the harder model); a numeric residual that looks alarming in isolation can be a no-op delta vs the status quo.** **(4) Grill order = walk the dependency tree, lead with the upstream domain judgment that gates the whole feature.** Q1 was NOT a technical call but the geneticist‚Äôs judgment ‚Äúare real overrides mostly known-side, missing-side, or a mix?‚Äù ‚Äì because option C/rule (i) only delivers value if known-side (case b) overrides actually occur; ‚Äúmix‚Äù confirmed the feature is worth building before grilling HOW. Then C2 (genetics) -\> C1 (schema, depends on C2) -\> C1 sub-decisions -\> technical confirmations. **(5) Reserve `AskUserQuestion` for the genuinely-open calls; confirm invariant-forced sub-decisions in prose.** C1.1 (two opposite absent-column defaults) and C1.3 (optional column) are FORCED by the D10 byte-identical invariant ‚Äì I stated them as derived-and-settled (owner may object) rather than asking, and grilled only the genuinely-open C1.2 (both/diffuse encoding) and the genetics gates C1/C2. Batched the architecture/test confirmations (C3/C4/C5/C6 + slice order + D10) into ONE consolidated confirm-or-amend question since none is a genetics call and S226‚Äôs verification already hardened them. Grounded the one test-design rec (C4) firsthand first (`test_reportGV.R:545-555`: X is sire-missing, so pinning the `(X,Y)=0.25` fixture as case (a) keeps the blanket-A regression additive). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\], \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 212 (which drafted the plan) / 211 (which filed \#95) and the D11/S214 ratification precedent (Learning 200). **This was a planning / Phase-0 decision session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A (the deliverable is the ratified decisions; implementation is a SEPARATE later session).** (S227, issue \#95 option C Phase 0)

#### Learning 214 ‚Äì A targeted-suppression slice where the genetically load-bearing function is UNCHANGED: the behavior change lives in the CALLER + a new classifier, so the discriminating RED is on the case the refinement FLIPS, not the function itself. Owner picked ‚ÄúSlice 1 of option C‚Äù; deliverable = the script-core of issue \#95 option C per the RATIFIED plan (RED `5448a1a8` -\> GREEN `4cd3e509`; REFACTOR offered, owner chose skip; all 3 gates via `AskUserQuestion`; **0 corrections / 0 overrides**). **(1) The load-bearing function did not change ‚Äì only its contract did.** `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`‚Äôs `:164` guard (`if (focalId %in% overriddenIds) next`) ALREADY suppresses whatever id-set it is passed, so option C needed NO code change there ‚Äì only a docstring redefinition of `overriddenIds` from ‚Äúids carrying an override‚Äù to ‚Äúthe set to suppress‚Äù (C3), and the CALLER (`reportGV`) computing a narrower set. Consequence for TDD: the function‚Äôs own ‚Äútests‚Äù (strict subset suppresses only those; full set = blanket-A) PASS at HEAD ‚Äì they are stays-green CHARACTERIZATION, not RED. **The genuine RED lived in the new pieces: the new classifier (`classifyOverrideMissingSide`, 7 cases error ‚Äì absent), the validator‚Äôs new structural check (a `missingSideFor` not naming id1/id2 -\> stop, fails at HEAD), and the caller‚Äôs flipped case.** When refining a blanket rule to a targeted one, look for where the NEW code is (caller + classifier), not the unchanged core. **(2) Design the discriminating RED on the case the refinement FLIPS (\[discriminating-RED\]).** Option C changes behavior ONLY for case b (known-side override now KEEPS the prior); case a (missing-side) is unchanged from blanket-A. So the case-b `reportGV` test (blank `missingSideFor` -\> `expect_gt(imk[[X]], original[[X]])`) FAILS at HEAD (blanket-A drops the prior; equal) ‚Äì 1/280 animals differ, surgical ‚Äì while the case-a test PASSES at HEAD (still suppressed) and is only a regression guard. Testing only case a would have been a non-discriminating RED that passes on the buggy build. **(3) The independent oracle for an option-C refinement is the EXISTING blanket-A oracle called with the refined suppress set** ‚Äì `i13_correctOptionC <- i13_correctBlanketA(ped, original, probands, <suppress set>)`, where the suppress set is computed in the test itself (not via the package helper) for true independence (\[identical-proof\] spirit). No new oracle algorithm needed. **(4) Reconstruct RED/GREEN/close-out as separate commits even when implemented in one working-tree pass:** stage selectively ‚Äì tests -\> RED commit, R/+man -\> GREEN commit, NEWS/CHANGELOG/learnings/handoff -\> close-out commit ‚Äì to keep the strict-TDD history (matches S223). **(5) Net-zero lint MUST use the in-place stash, never the /tmp-copy method (\[stale-namespace\]/\[lint-net-zero\]).** Linting `git show HEAD:R/x.R > /tmp/x.R` loses the project `.lintr` -\> shows default object_name/indentation lints (HEAD-in-/tmp reportGV = 31 lints vs 4 in-place) ‚Äì NOT comparable. Correct: `git stash push -- <tracked R files>` -\> lint in-place (so `.lintr` applies) -\> `stash pop`; proven net-zero (HEAD 6/4/2 vs current 6/4/1; the new file 0; my docstring rewrite even REMOVED one 81-char line). Match the lint-clean idiom: `toString(x)` not `paste(x, collapse=", ")`, and reword code-like comments (`+sexMean/2`) that trip `commented_code_linter`. **(6) Phase-3E N/A for a script-core slice (stated, not skipped):** the change is to a pure analytical function‚Äôs (`reportGV`) logic with NO Shiny wiring/dispatch/startup change (the in-app upload is Slice 2), so FM \#24 (build-passes-ship-it, about runtime wiring) does not apply ‚Äì the `reportGV` behavior is fully exercised by the test suite. Carried as applied: \[discriminating-RED\], \[regression-read\], \[lint-net-zero\], \[stale-namespace\], \[right-sized-orchestration\]; \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\], \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 213/212 (the ratified plan) and Learning 209 (the R13 display slice ‚Äì the prior issue-13 override slice). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session.** (S228, issue \#95 option C Slice 1)

#### Learning 215 ‚Äì The careful-admin push-\>PR-\>CI-\>merge arc bites when you treat it as ROUTINE and skip the documented verification steps: two pre-warned regressions (a closing-keyword NEGATION that auto-closed the tracking issue, and a `reset --hard` that discarded the owner‚Äôs external edit). 4th run of the arc (S218/S221/S224/S229); owner picked ‚Äú1‚Äù then ‚Äúmerge it once devel passes‚Äù. The merge itself was correct (verify-then-merge held: full matrix green, lone `lint` red surfaced as the documented non-blocking noise, merged with a merge commit only after green). But I read SESSION_NOTES and NOT Learning 207/210 before acting, and regressed on both procedures those learnings exist to prevent. **(1) A NEGATED closing keyword STILL fires ‚Äì ‚Äúdoes not close \#95‚Äù auto-closed \#95 on merge.** GitHub‚Äôs closing-keyword parser does pattern-matching, not sentiment: the substring `close #95` triggers regardless of a preceding ‚Äúnot‚Äù. S228‚Äôs gotcha said ‚ÄúNO closing keyword‚Äù and Learning 207(3)/210(3) prescribe the mitigation I skipped: BEFORE merge, grep BOTH the rendered PR body AND the commit messages for `clos(e|es|ed)|fix(es|ed)?|resolv(e|es|ed)` adjacent to a `#N`. **Run that grep every time, and keep ANY such word away from a `#N` even in prose/negations** ‚Äì if you must mention closing, write ‚Äústays open‚Äù / ‚Äúremains open‚Äù, never ‚Äúdoes not close \#N‚Äù. **It fired a THIRD time the same session ‚Äì in my close-out COMMIT MESSAGE** (‚Äúa negated closing keyword auto-closed \#95‚Äù): the substring `closed #95` re-closed the issue when `28f99872` was pushed to `master`. The most insidious surface is the close-out commit/PR that DESCRIBES the incident ‚Äì the natural phrasing (‚Äúauto-closed \#N‚Äù, ‚Äúfixes \#N‚Äù) is itself the trigger. Two safe habits: (a) when writing a `#N` immediately after a keyword, put a noun between them ‚Äì ‚Äúclosed the issue \#95‚Äù / ‚Äúauto-closed issue \#95‚Äù does NOT match (the keyword must be immediately followed by the ref); (b) FILE CONTENTS never trigger (only commit messages + PR/issue bodies parsed on push/merge to the default branch), so describe the incident freely in CHANGELOG/notes, but sanitize the COMMIT MESSAGE. The routine final-state verification (`gh issue view 95`) is what caught all three ‚Äì keep that check at the very end of every merge/push session. Recovery when it fires: `gh issue reopen <n>`; fix the body via `gh api -X PATCH repos/O/R/pulls/<n> -F body=@file` (the `gh pr edit` path 401s on the same Projects-classic deprecation as `gh issue view <n>` ‚Äì use REST / `--json`); add a clarifying comment; re-confirm OPEN. **(2) `git reset --hard` with an unexplained modified tracked file present destroys whatever it is ‚Äì and during a long CI wait that file is probably the OWNER‚Äôs edit.** `data-raw/fgSEValidation.R` was clean at my Phase-0 `git status` and showed `M` only after the ~20-min CI watch (the owner had edited it in RStudio, mtime 16:04). I chained `git reset --hard origin/master` to sync local master and blew it away (owner confirmed it was minor lint cleanup, to be redone ‚Äì harmless only by grace). This is SAFEGUARDS pre-flight (‚Äúuncommitted changes from elsewhere ‚Äì do not touch; report and ask‚Äù) + FM \#22, and Learning 210(2) already gives the safe sync: `git stash push -- SESSION_NOTES.md` (the 1B stub only) -\> `git fetch` -\> `git switch master` -\> `git merge --ff-only origin/master` -\> `git stash pop`; the stash pop applies cleanly post-FF and the stub survives. **Rule: re-check `git status` IMMEDIATELY before any `reset --hard`; if a tracked file is modified that you did not change, STOP, surface it, and stash/ask ‚Äì never reset over it.** (Recoverable-source note: RStudio keeps editor-buffer backups in `.Rproj.user/<id>/sources/session-*/<HASH>-contents` with the path in the sibling `<HASH>` JSON ‚Äì a real recovery path if the buffer is gone, though here the owner waved it off.) **(3) Meta-lesson ‚Äì ‚Äúroutine‚Äù is exactly where regressions hide.** Learning 210 says ‚Äúwhen the arc is routine the value is in NOT regressing.‚Äù The handoff stated the rules but left the PROCEDURES in PROJECT_LEARNINGS; I treated the arc as known and skipped consulting them (\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] in the breach). On any arc with a Learning number, RE-READ that learning before executing, even (especially) the Nth time. **(4) Branch hygiene stays a SEPARATE step (Learning 208/210(4)) ‚Äì did NOT bundle deletion; left `issue95-optionC-slice1` (local+remote) for a future pass (1-and-done).** Carried as applied (mostly in the breach): \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\], \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\]; extends Learning 207/210 (the admin/merge arc whose documented steps I should have followed) and 208 (branch hygiene as the deferred step); integrates Learning 214 (the S228 slice it merged). **This was an admin/merge session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S229, issue \#95 option C Slice 1 ‚Äì merged PR \#96)

#### Learning 216 ‚Äì When a function‚Äôs behavior change is NOT observable through its public output, make the strict-TDD discriminating RED real by extracting a shared helper and testing THAT (which also makes the lockstep structural). Owner picked ‚Äúa then b‚Äù = (a) branch hygiene + (b) option C Slice 2 (bring `gvaConvergence` into lockstep with `reportGV`‚Äôs option-C behavior + document `missingSideFor` in-app). Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session; RED -\> GREEN -\> REFACTOR-skipped; all 3 gates + a pre-RED approach gate + a mid-GREEN review hold, all via `AskUserQuestion`; **0 corrections / 1 owner course-correction**. **(1) The testability wall (the load-bearing insight):** `gvaConvergence` returns NO per-animal mean kinship; its only mean-kinship-sensitive output is the convergence curve, which is gene-drop-uniqueness-driven and (the existing `test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides.R:14-23` documents this) mean-kinship-order-INVISIBLE on qcPed. So the option-C suppression (a one-animal ~1 SD mean-kinship delta) cannot be robustly seen in gvaConvergence‚Äôs output ‚Äì a behavioral RED through it would be fragile or impossible. **Resolution: extract the changed logic into a shared `@noRd` helper (`prepareKinshipOverrides()` = validate -\> warn-drop -\> apply -\> compute the option-C suppress set), unit-test the HELPER (clean discriminating RED: 8 cases error at HEAD because the function is undefined), and route BOTH `reportGV` and `gvaConvergence` through it.** This simultaneously (a) gives a real failing-test-first, (b) makes the report/diagnostic lockstep STRUCTURAL (one source of truth ‚Äì they cannot drift), which IS the slice‚Äôs goal, and (c) realizes the plan‚Äôs C5 ‚Äúdo not widen the divergence‚Äù without doing the deferred full 3-way de-dup (the module fallback paths keep `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix`). The behavior-preserving `reportGV` re-route is guarded by its extensive Slice-1 suite (106 assertions stayed green). **When the public output can‚Äôt show the change, the seam IS the test surface ‚Äì and a shared seam doubles as the anti-drift mechanism.** **(2) Surface the test-design fork as a pre-RED `AskUserQuestion` (it is an author‚Äôs approach decision per the phase-gate format).** The three real options ‚Äì shared helper (chosen) / mirror + expose a new return field / mirror + an empirically-tuned gu-bearing fixture ‚Äì differ in scope, API surface, and fragility; the owner‚Äôs call, not mine. **(3) Recognize when the ‚Äúdelivery‚Äù half is already wired.** Slice 2‚Äôs app-upload path was ALREADY green at HEAD: `readKinshipOverrides()` uses `check.names = FALSE` and returns all columns, so a `missingSideFor` column rides through -\> `checkKinshipOverrides` (validates it, Slice 1) -\> `reportGV` (already option-C-aware). So the `testServer(modGeneticValueServer)` test is a regression GUARD (passes at HEAD), NOT a discriminating RED ‚Äì the only true behavioral gap was the second consumer (`gvaConvergence`). Check what the existing wiring already delivers before assuming a ‚Äúdelivery slice‚Äù needs delivery code. **(4) `spelling::update_wordlist()` is DESTRUCTIVE to a curated WORDLIST ‚Äì it reconciles the file to EXACTLY the currently-flagged set, ADDING new words but REMOVING every curated entry no longer flagged.** Here it added `focals`+`supersession` but silently PRUNED 31 curated function-name entries (rbindlist, runGeneKeepr, ‚Ä¶); the owner interrupted. Recovery: `git checkout inst/WORDLIST` then surgical inserts in sorted position (`focals` after `focalAnimals`; `supersession` after `subfields`) -\> diff is exactly `+2`, 0 removals. **A ‚Äúconvenience‚Äù tool that syncs a curated file to current state will delete curation as a side effect ‚Äì prefer a surgical hand-edit; if you must use it, diff the result before committing** (cf.¬Ý\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\] ‚Äì same family: verify before letting a tool blow away curated content). Note `supersession` was pre-existing in NEWS at HEAD and not in WORDLIST ‚Äì so HEAD was not actually spelling-clean for it; adding both reached spell=0 (the project bar). **(5) Net-zero lint when lifting a block verbatim:** a faithful extraction carries the source block‚Äôs lints into the new file (`any(!x)`, `paste(x, collapse=", ")`); clean them in the new home (`!all(x)`, `toString(x)`) so the new file is lint-0 AND the source loses them = net REDUCTION. Reword comments that trip `commented_code_linter` ‚Äì the trigger is a line that parses as R (a trailing `reportGV()`, or `#NN` creating a nested comment); plain prose without `()` / `#NN` is safe. **(6) Phase-3E runtime smoke for a Shiny-doc + analytical change, achievable headlessly:** (a) FUNCTIONAL smoke ‚Äì call the actual functions the app calls (`reportGV`/`gvaConvergence`) on real `qcPed` and confirm the behavior delta (known-side override KEEPS the `+sexMean/2`: meanKin 0.009198 \> missing-side SUPPRESS 0.006027); (b) APP-BOOT smoke ‚Äì launch `runModularApp(port=, launch.browser=FALSE)` in the background, `curl` the root, and grep the SERVED HTML for the change marker (`missingSideFor` present -\> the helptext + `includeHTML(genetic_value.html)` render live), HTTP 200, then kill the pid. This is a genuine FM \#24 runtime check without a browser. Carried as applied: \[discriminating-RED\], \[regression-read\], \[lint-net-zero\], \[right-sized-orchestration\]; \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\], \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 214 (Slice 1 ‚Äì the caller-computes-the-suppress-set design this helper now centralizes) and 212/213 (the plan + its ratification). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session.** (S230, issue \#95 option C Slice 2)

#### Learning 217 ‚Äì For a PR body / issue comment you author fresh, make the broad `clos|fix|resolv` grep return EMPTY (strip every such token, even harmless hyphenated or non-adjacent ones) rather than reasoning about whether a keyword is adjacent to a `#N`. Owner picked the outward-facing admin step (push branch `issue95-optionC-slice2` + open PR \#97 ‚ÄúRelates to \#95‚Äù), scoped to **push + open PR** (merge deferred, owner-gated). **Admin/PR session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections.** This refines Learning 215‚Äôs grep into a bright-line rule for content you control. **(1) Two surfaces, two rules.** GitHub‚Äôs closing-keyword parser fires only on `keyword #N` in (a) commit messages and (b) PR/issue BODIES that reach the default branch ‚Äì FILE contents never trigger. For PRE-EXISTING commit messages you cannot rewrite, apply the narrow test: is any `clos(e|es|ed)|fix(es|ed)?|resolv(e|es|ed)` IMMEDIATELY followed by a `#N`? Here the 3 branch commits carried ZERO `#N` refs (they write ‚Äú(issue 95)‚Äù / ‚ÄúRelates to issue 95‚Äù ‚Äì the bare word, never ‚Äú#95‚Äù), so the lone ‚Äúclose-out‚Äù substring in the S230 commit subject was provably safe. For the PR body you AUTHOR, do not make that adjacency judgment at all ‚Äì write it with zero `clos|fix|resolv` tokens so a plain `grep -iE 'clos|fix|resolv'` returns empty; I rephrased a harmless hyphenated ‚Äúclose-out‚Äù (quoting a commit subject) to ‚Äúdocs/handoff‚Äù purely to clear the broad grep. S229‚Äôs THREE firings all had a keyword TOKEN in an authored surface (a negated body, then a close-out commit message); a bright-line ‚Äúno token in any authored body‚Äù rule stops all three where ‚Äúnegation is safe‚Äù reasoning did not. **(2) Verify against the RENDERED artifact, not the local draft.** After `gh pr create --body-file`, re-fetch with `gh pr view <n> --json body --jq .body` and grep THAT; then confirm the issue with `gh api repos/O/R/issues/<n> --jq .state` (the `gh issue view <n>` / `gh pr edit` paths 401 on the Projects-classic deprecation ‚Äì use REST / `--json`). Opening a PR does NOT close an issue (only a merge with a body keyword does), but the cheap end-check (`#95` still OPEN) is exactly what caught S229‚Äôs firings ‚Äì run it immediately after PR creation and again after any merge. **(3) Commit messages use the bare-word form too.** Wrote the S231 close-out commit as ‚Äú(issue 95)‚Äù / ‚ÄúPR 97‚Äù (no `#`) with zero clos/fix/resolv, grepped before pushing ‚Äì so the close-out commit that lands on master when PR \#97 merges cannot fire (S229‚Äôs third firing was exactly a close-out commit message). **(4) 1-and-done held:** delivered push + open PR per the owner‚Äôs explicit scope; did NOT merge (the more consequential, owner-gated step) ‚Äì surfaced CI-watch + merge as the next session‚Äôs pick. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\] (re-checked `git status` before the push ‚Äì clean but for the 1B stub + standing untracked HTML), \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\] (pushed the close-out to origin/branch so local==origin/branch; it reaches master at merge); extends Learning 215 (the negation trap) and 207/210 (the careful-admin arc). **This was an admin/PR session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S231, issue \#95 option C Slice 2 ‚Äì PR \#97 opened)

#### Learning 218 ‚Äì The careful-admin push-\>PR-\>CI-\>merge arc runs CLEAN when you read the arc‚Äôs learnings (207/210/215/217) BEFORE acting ‚Äì the 5th run (S218/S221/S224/S229, now S232) and the first clean one since S229‚Äôs triple closing-keyword firing. Owner picked ‚Äúgo with next suggested‚Äù -\> merge the finished S230 Slice 2 (PR \#97 -\> merge commit `e893c90d`; \#95 stayed OPEN). The whole value of this session was NOT regressing (Learning 210), and the way to not regress was the \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] step S229 skipped: I read 207/210/215/217 first, then executed each documented control. **(1) Verify-then-merge held ‚Äì did NOT merge on `MERGEABLE`.** Waited for the full R-CMD-check matrix (macOS/Windows/Ubuntu release + oldrel-1 + devel) + pkgdown + test-coverage + both codecov to go green; the devel leg finished last (22:58:53 success) and only then did I merge. The lone `lint` red was proven pre-existing whole-package noise via `gh api repos/.../check-runs/<JOB>/annotations` (12 findings: 10 in `data-raw/fgSEValidation.R`, 2 `.github` workflow warnings; NONE in this PR‚Äôs changed files) ‚Äì the documented ‚Äúsurface the lone red with evidence‚Äù step (207(1)/210(1)). **(2) Closing-keyword proof can be AIRTIGHT, not just ‚Äúprobably safe‚Äù.** `git log origin/master..branch --format='%H%n%s%n%b' | grep -nE '#[0-9]+'` returned EMPTY ‚Äì NO branch commit carried any `#N` ref at all (they write the bare-word ‚Äú(issue 95)‚Äù), so the lone ‚Äúclose-out‚Äù token in the S230 subject is provably inert (a closing keyword fires only when IMMEDIATELY followed by `#N`). The rendered PR body grepped empty for `clos|fix|resolv`. And I authored the merge commit message keyword-free and ref-bare: `gh pr merge 97 --merge --subject "Merge pull request #97 from rmsharp/issue95-optionC-slice2" --body "option C Slice 2: ... prepareKinshipOverrides helper (issue 95)"` ‚Äì so the merge commit that lands on `master` cannot fire (S229‚Äôs 3rd firing was exactly a close-out commit message). Post-merge `gh api repos/O/R/issues/95 --jq .state` -\> open (the end-check that caught all of S229‚Äôs firings). **Bright-line, three surfaces:** branch commits (narrow adjacency test, they‚Äôre frozen) + PR body (authored empty) + the merge commit message you author at merge time (`--subject`/`--body`, keyword-free, ref-bare). **(3) Local sync = stash-the-stub (Learning 210(2)), preceded by a `git status` re-check (Learning 215).** Confirmed only the 1B stub + standing untracked HTML were dirty (no unexplained modified tracked file), then `git stash push -- SESSION_NOTES.md` -\> `git fetch` -\> `git switch master` -\> `git merge --ff-only origin/master` -\> `git stash pop` (clean pop ‚Äì post-FF master‚Äôs SESSION_NOTES == the stash base). Verified `master == origin/master == e893c90d` and the Slice-2 code is present. The marker survived; no `reset --hard` near a possibly-owner-edited file. **(4) NEW env nugget: `status` is a READ-ONLY special variable in zsh** (the session shell, initialized from the user profile). A `run_in_background` poll script that did `status=$(gh api ... --jq .status)` died immediately with `(eval):3: read-only variable: status` (exit 1). Use neutral names (`st`, `cc`, `concl`) in ANY Bash snippet here; other zsh specials to avoid assigning include `path`, `cdpath`, `pipestatus`. The intended mechanism was sound: a `run_in_background` loop that polls a CI check-run and `break`s on `status == completed` exits and re-invokes the agent ‚Äì the right way to wait on an EXTERNAL CI leg the harness cannot otherwise notify on (here the devel leg finished before the relaunch was even needed, so a foreground re-check sufficed). **(5) 1-and-done held:** branch deletion stays the deferred SEPARATE hygiene step (Learning 208/210(4)) ‚Äì left `issue95-optionC-slice2` (local + remote) for the next session; did not bundle. **General rule:** on any arc with a Learning number, RE-READ that learning before executing the Nth time ‚Äì routine is where regressions hide, and the documented controls (annotations-API proof, both-surfaces + merge-commit closing-keyword discipline with the post-merge end-check, stash-the-stub sync) are exactly what turn a triple-firing arc into a clean one. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\], \[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 207/210/215/217 (the careful-admin arc) and integrates Learning 216 (the S230 slice it merged). **This was an admin/merge session ‚Äì TDD code-phases N/A.** (S232, issue \#95 option C Slice 2 ‚Äì merged PR \#97)
